blocks:
Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوٓا۟ أَوْفُوا۟ بِٱلْعُقُودِ ۚ أُحِلَّتْ لَكُم بَهِيمَةُ ٱلْأَنْعَـٰمِ إِلَّا مَا يُتْلَىٰ عَلَيْكُمْ غَيْرَ مُحِلِّى ٱلصَّيْدِ وَأَنتُمْ حُرُمٌ ۗ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ يَحْكُمُ مَا يُرِيدُ (۱)
O you who have faith! Keep your agreements. You are permitted animals of grazing livestock, except what is [now] announced to you, disallowing game while you are in pilgrim sanctity. Indeed Allah decrees whatever He desires. (1)

Key Points

The word bahīmah (بهیمة) here means quadruped (four-legged animal) and is broader than an‘ām (اَنعام). An‘ām refers to cattle, camels, and sheep, and bahīmat al-an‘ām (بهیمة الانعام), which appears three times in the Quran, means the permissibility of the meat of these quadrupeds. [1] Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: The intent of bahīmat al-an‘ām is the fetus of the slaughtered animal, whose mother's tazkiyah (ritual slaughter/purification) is also counted as its tazkiyah. [2] The late Fayḍ (Feiz) states in Tafsīr al-Sāfī: It is possible that this narration explains a hidden instance of it, or that they are called bahīmah while still in the mother's womb; therefore, it does not contradict the general meaning. [3] The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله said: «لادین لمَن لاعهد له» [4], meaning, whoever is not faithful has no religion. Indeed, if covenants are not fulfilled, the foundation of society and public trust collapses, and chaos ensues. The Quran deems it necessary to fulfill covenants even with polytheists: «فأتمّوا الیهم عهدهم الى مدّتهم» [5] And according to a hadith from Imam Sadiq عليه السلام, fulfilling covenants with transgressors is also necessary. [6] Based on another narration, if a Muslim grants safety (amān) to a disbelieving enemy, even by a gesture, it is obligatory for other Muslims to observe this implied covenant. [7] Heavenly Books are the covenant of Allah, and one must be faithful to them: the Old Covenant (Torah), the New Covenant (Gospel), and the Final Covenant (Quran). We read in a hadith: «القرآن عهداللّه» [8]

1) Tafsīr Majma‘ al-Bayān. 2) Kāfī, Vol. 6, p. 234. 3) Qāmūs al-Qur’ān. 4) Bihār, Vol. 16, p. 144. 5) Tawbah, 4. 6) Kāfī, Vol. 2, p. 162. 7) Mustadrak, Vol. 2, p. 250. 8) Kāfī, Vol. 6, p. 198.

Messages

  1. Muslims must adhere to all covenants (with every person and every group), whether verbal, written, or practical; political, economic, social, or familial covenants; with the strong or the weak, with a friend or an enemy, with Allah (such as vows and oaths) or with people, with an individual or society, with the small or the great, with regional countries or international agreements. The word al-‘uqūd (العقود) has the alif and lām (the definite article), which includes all agreements. «أوفوا بالعقود»

  2. Faith guarantees the fulfillment of oaths and covenants. «یا ایّها الّذین آمنوا اوفوا بالعقود»

  3. Just as breaking covenants and oppression are the key to deprivation from divine blessings and favors [9], adherence to oaths and covenants is the factor for benefiting from them. «أوفوا بالعقود احلّت لكم» (Indeed, keeping promises is the key to having good provision. As the saying goes: A person who settles accounts promptly is a partner in people's wealth.)

  4. Now that Allah has placed various blessings at our disposal, let us be faithful to His covenants. «أوفوا بالعقود احلّت لكم»

  5. One of the divine covenants is attention to the rulings of halāl (lawful) and harām (unlawful) regarding foodstuffs. «اوفوا بالعقود اُحلّت لكم»

  6. Islam pays attention to both social and economic issues. (Fulfilling covenants is a social issue, and benefiting from animals is an economic issue.) «أوفوا بالعقود اُحِلّت لكم بهیمة الانعام»

  7. The consumption of the meat of cattle, camels, and sheep that have died, been strangled, etc., is unlawful (harām). «الاّ ما یتلى علیكم»

  8. Not all quadrupeds are lawful. «الاّ ما یُتلى علیكم» (Based on the interpretation that the phrase relates to the types of quadrupeds.)

  9. To preserve the sanctity of Ihrām (state of consecration), one must experience some deprivation and abstain from hunting. «غیر مُحلّى الصید و انتم حُرُم»

  10. Adhering to the prohibitions of Ihrām is among the instances of fulfilling the covenant and agreement with Allah. «أوفوا بالعقود... غیر مُحلّى الصید و انتم حُرُم»

  11. Ihrām is a covenant and agreement with Allah. «أوفوا بالعقود... و انتم حُرُم»

  12. Ihrām is a special and unique state possessing sanctity. «و انتم حُرُم»

9) Referring to verses 160 of Nisā’ and 146 of An‘ām.

Explanations

Portrait of Surah Al-Ma'idah

This Surah has 120 verses and was revealed a few months before the passing of the Noble Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله. None of its verses have been abrogated. This Surah is named Al-Mā’idah (The Table Spread) because of the prayer of Prophet Jesus عليه السلام for the descent of the heavenly table spread, which is mentioned in verse 114. It has also been named ‘Ahd (Covenant) and ‘Uqūd (Agreements), and overall, the verses of this Surah warn against a significant breach of covenant. The address «یا ایّها الّذین آمنوا» (O you who have believed) is most frequent in this Surah. For example, it appears 11 times in Surah Al-Baqarah, but 16 times in this Surah. This Surah includes issues such as: Wilāyah (Guardianship) and leadership, refutation of the doctrine of the Trinity, fulfillment of oaths and covenants, bearing witness with justice, prohibition of murder, rulings on foodstuffs, rulings on wuḍū (ablution), ghusl (ritual bath), and tayammum (dry ablution), recommendation of social justice, and rulings on wills, retribution (qiṣāṣ), theft, adultery, and more. Since it is one of the last Surahs to be revealed, it begins with the phrase «أوفوا بالعقود», recommending the fulfillment of all commitments and covenants.

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ لَا تُحِلُّوا۟ شَعَـٰٓئِرَ ٱللَّهِ وَلَا ٱلشَّهْرَ ٱلْحَرَامَ وَلَا ٱلْهَدْىَ وَلَا ٱلْقَلَـٰٓئِدَ وَلَآ ءَآمِّينَ ٱلْبَيْتَ ٱلْحَرَامَ يَبْتَغُونَ فَضْلًا مِّن رَّبِّهِمْ وَرِضْوَٰنًا ۚ وَإِذَا حَلَلْتُمْ فَٱصْطَادُوا۟ ۚ وَلَا يَجْرِمَنَّكُمْ شَنَـَٔانُ قَوْمٍ أَن صَدُّوكُمْ عَنِ ٱلْمَسْجِدِ ٱلْحَرَامِ أَن تَعْتَدُوا۟ ۘ وَتَعَاوَنُوا۟ عَلَى ٱلْبِرِّ وَٱلتَّقْوَىٰ ۖ وَلَا تَعَاوَنُوا۟ عَلَى ٱلْإِثْمِ وَٱلْعُدْوَٰنِ ۚ وَٱتَّقُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ ۖ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ شَدِيدُ ٱلْعِقَابِ (۲)
O you who have faith! Do not violate Allah’s sacraments, neither the sacred month, nor the offering, nor the necklaces, nor those bound for the Sacred House, who seek their Lord’s grace and [His] pleasure. But when you emerge from pilgrim sanctity, you may hunt for game. Ill feeling for a people should not lead you, because they barred you from [access to] the Sacred Mosque, to transgress. Cooperate in piety and Godwariness, but do not cooperate in sin and aggression, and be wary of Allah. Indeed Allah is severe in retribution. (2)

Key Points

Hady is an unmarked animal gifted for sacrifice during Hajj, and Qala'id are animals that are marked before the Hajj rituals, either by hanging something around their necks or by branding, so they can be sacrificed during the Hajj rites. Allah, at the beginning of this Ayah, has made respect for all the symbols obligatory and desecrating them forbidden. But among the symbols, He has specifically mentioned several cases, including: the four months of Rajab, Dhul-Qa'dah, Dhul-Hijjah, and Muharram, during which fighting is forbidden; the month of Dhul-Hijjah, during which Hajj is performed; Hady, which is the unmarked sacrifice; and Qala'id, which is the marked sacrifice. In the sixth year of the Hijra, Muslims had traveled eighty farsakhs (leagues) from Medina to Mecca to perform Hajj, but the disbelievers prevented them, leading to the Treaty of Hudaybiyyah. Now that Mecca has been conquered, they must not commit aggression. «صدّوكم عن المسجد الحرام»

If acquiring knowledge is a "Birr" (goodness), then its prerequisites, such as providing schools, books, libraries, laboratories, transportation, teachers, etc., are all "cooperation upon Birr."

Cooperation in goodness and purity. In Ayah 177 of Surah Al-Baqarah, instances of "Birr" are stated: «لكن البرّ مَن آمن باللّه والیوم الاخر و الملائكة والكتاب والنبیّین وآتى المال على حبّه ذوى القُربى والیتامى و... واولئك هم المتّقون» Birr is faith in Allah, the Last Day, the angels, the heavenly Book, and the prophets, attending to the deprived members of society, adhering to contracts, and patience in affairs.

In many narrations, cooperation upon goodness and assisting the oppressed and deprived have been emphasized, and helping oppressors has been forbidden. We mention a few traditions here for blessing:

  • Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: Whoever takes a step to help others has the reward of a Mujahid (fighter/struggler). [10]
  • As long as a person is thinking of helping people, Allah helps him. [11]
  • Helping a Muslim is better than a month of recommended fasting and I'tikaf (seclusion for worship). [12]
  • Whoever helps an oppressor is himself an oppressor. [13]
  • Do not help an oppressor, even in building a mosque. [14]

10) Wasā’il, Vol. 8, p. 602. 11) Wasā’il, Vol. 8, p. 586. 12) Wasā’il, Vol. 11, p. 345. 13) Wasā’il, Vol. 11, p. 345. 14) Wasā’il, Vol. 12, p. 130.

Messages

  1. Allah expects something specific from the believers. «یا ایّها الّذین آمنوا لاتحلّوا...»
  2. Respecting the symbols of Allah is the duty of the people of faith. «یا ایّها الّذین آمنوا لاتحلّوا شعائر اللّه»
  3. Desecrating the sanctity and sacredness of the divine symbols is forbidden. «لاتحلّوا شعائر اللّه»
  4. Not all times are equal. Some days, like the Days of Allah (Ayyam Allah), and some months, like the Sacred Month, have special respect. «ولا الشّهرالحرام»
  5. Even an animal dedicated to a divine purpose deserves respect. «و لا الهَدى و لاالقلائد»
  6. Those heading toward the House of Allah must be respected. «ولا آمّین البیت الحرام» (Any program that harms the respect and security of the pilgrims of the House of Allah is forbidden, whether during Hajj or Umrah.)
  7. The main goal of Hajj is visiting the Ka'bah. «آمّین البیت»
  8. Hajj and Umrah are ways to attain this world and the Hereafter. «یبتغون فضلاً من ربّهم و رضواناً»
  9. Seeking lawful profit is a value. The Quran places the bounty of the Lord (which is business profit) alongside His pleasure and proximity. «یبتغون فضلاً من ربّهم ورضواناً»
  10. All types of economic and commercial activity in Mecca are permissible for Islamic countries. «یبتغون فضلاً من ربّهم»
  11. Material benefits are a favor (bounty) from Allah to humanity and are among the aspects of His Lordship (Rububiyyah). «فضلاً من ربّهم»
  12. The enmity of others at one time does not justify our oppression and aggression at another time. «ولا یجرمنّكم... ان تعتدوا»
  13. Injustice and transgression are forbidden, even against enemies. «شنئان قوم... أن تعتدوا» (Observe justice even in revenge.)
  14. Religious feelings should not become an excuse for oppression. «صدّوكم عن المسجد الحرام ان تعتدوا»
  15. Fulfilling covenants and preserving the sanctity and sacredness of the divine symbols requires cooperation and mutual assistance. «لاتحلّوا شعائراللّه... و تعاونوا»
  16. Overlooking the mistakes of others is one way of cooperating upon goodness. «لایجرمنّكم... تعاونوا»
  17. The Islamic government and society must support the oppressed and good deeds on the international stage, and condemn the oppressor and evil deeds. «تعاونوا على البرّ و التّقوى و لاتعاونوا على الاثم و العدوان»
  18. To achieve the comprehensive growth of virtues, the groundwork must be prepared, and there must be cooperation in reaching that goal. «تعاونوا على البرّ»
  19. Instead of supporting tribe, region, race, and language, one must support the 'truth' (Haqq) and assist 'goodness' (Birr). «تعاونوا على البرّ»
  20. In an Islamic society, the righteous person is not alone, and the oppressor has no helper. «تعاونوا على البرّ... ولاتعاونوا على الاثم والعدوان»
  21. Those who violate the sanctity of the divine symbols and assist in evil must prepare themselves for severe divine punishment. «واتّقوا اللّه انّ اللّه شدید العقاب»

Ayah

حُرِّمَتْ عَلَيْكُمُ ٱلْمَيْتَةُ وَٱلدَّمُ وَلَحْمُ ٱلْخِنزِيرِ وَمَآ أُهِلَّ لِغَيْرِ ٱللَّهِ بِهِۦ وَٱلْمُنْخَنِقَةُ وَٱلْمَوْقُوذَةُ وَٱلْمُتَرَدِّيَةُ وَٱلنَّطِيحَةُ وَمَآ أَكَلَ ٱلسَّبُعُ إِلَّا مَا ذَكَّيْتُمْ وَمَا ذُبِحَ عَلَى ٱلنُّصُبِ وَأَن تَسْتَقْسِمُوا۟ بِٱلْأَزْلَـٰمِ ۚ ذَٰلِكُمْ فِسْقٌ ۗ ٱلْيَوْمَ يَئِسَ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ مِن دِينِكُمْ فَلَا تَخْشَوْهُمْ وَٱخْشَوْنِ ۚ ٱلْيَوْمَ أَكْمَلْتُ لَكُمْ دِينَكُمْ وَأَتْمَمْتُ عَلَيْكُمْ نِعْمَتِى وَرَضِيتُ لَكُمُ ٱلْإِسْلَـٰمَ دِينًا ۚ فَمَنِ ٱضْطُرَّ فِى مَخْمَصَةٍ غَيْرَ مُتَجَانِفٍ لِّإِثْمٍ ۙ فَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ غَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ (۳)
You are prohibited carrion, blood, the flesh of swine, and what has been offered to other than Allah, and the animal strangled or beaten to death, and that which dies by falling or is gored to death, and that which is mangled by a beast of prey—barring that which you may purify —and what is sacrificed on stone altars [to idols], and that you should divide by raffling with arrows. All that is transgression. Today the faithless have despaired of your religion. So do not fear them, but fear Me. Today I have perfected your religion for you, and I have completed My blessing upon you, and I have approved Islam as your religion. But should anyone be compelled by hunger, without inclining to sin, then Allah is indeed all-forgiving, all-merciful. (3)

Key Points

It was mentioned in the first Ayah that consuming the meat of livestock is lawful (halal), except for what will be declared unlawful (haram) later. This Ayah specifies ten instances of unlawful meats. Mun-khanīqah (مُنخَنِقة) is an animal that has been strangled, whether by a human, an animal, or by itself. Mawqūdhah (مَوقوذَة) is an animal that dies due to beating and torture. It was the custom of the Arabs to beat some animals in reverence to idols until they died, considering this act a form of worship. (15) Mutaraddiyah (متردّیة) is an animal that has fallen from a height, and Naṭīḥah (نطیحة) is an animal that has died as a result of being gored. The prohibition of what is mentioned in this Ayah has also appeared in Surahs Al-An'am, An-Nahl, and Al-Baqarah, but in this Ayah, specific examples of carrion (strangled, beaten, gored, etc.) are detailed.

Imam Baqir عليه السلام said: Wilayah (Guardianship) is the last divine obligation, and then He recited the Ayah: "الْيَوْمَ أَكْمَلْتُ لَكُمْ دِينَكُمْ" (This day I have perfected for you your religion). (16)

Idols (buts) were statues that had a form, but Nuṣub (نُصُب) were shapeless stones erected around the Ka'bah, before which sacrifices were offered, and the blood of the sacrifice was smeared upon them.

Ghadir in the Quran Two separate matters are stated in this Ayah: one concerns the prohibition of unlawful meats, except in cases of necessity (ḍarūrah), and the other concerns the completion of the religion and the despair of the disbelievers. This latter part is completely independent, for several reasons:

A: The despair of the disbelievers regarding the religion has no connection to whether or not carrion is consumed.

B: Narrations transmitted by both Shi'a and Sunni sources regarding the occasion of the Ayah's revelation focus on explaining the phrases "الْيَوْمَ يَئِسَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا" (This day have those who disbelieved despaired) and "أَلْيَوْمَ أَكْمَلْتُ لَكُمْ دِينَكُمْ" (This day I have perfected for you your religion), not the preceding and succeeding sentences concerning the rulings on carrion.

C: According to Shi'a and Sunni narrations, this part of the Ayah: "الْيَوْمَ أَكْمَلْتُ..." was revealed after the appointment of Ali ibn Abi Talib علیهما السلام to the Imamate at Ghadir Khumm.

D: Apart from textual evidence, rational analysis also leads to the same conclusion, because four important characteristics are stated for that day: 1. The day of the disbelievers' despair, 2. The day of the religion's perfection, 3. The day of the completion of Allah's favor upon the people, 4. The day when Islam, as the "religion" and a complete creed, was approved by God.

Now, if we examine the events in the history of Islam, no important day—such as the Prophetic Mission (Bi'that), the Migration (Hijrah), the Conquest of Mecca, victories in battles, etc.—despite all their value, encompasses these four important attributes mentioned in this Ayah. Even the Farewell Pilgrimage (Hajjat al-Wada') is not of this significance, because Hajj is only a part of the religion, not the whole religion.

  • As for the Bi'that, it is the first day the mission of the Holy Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله began, and it can never be said that the religion was perfected on the first day of the Bi'that.
  • As for the Hijrah, it was the day the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله fled by God's command, the day the disbelievers attacked the Prophet's house, not the day of their despair.
  • As for the days of victory in the battles of Badr, Khandaq, and others, only the disbelievers present on the battlefield despaired, not all disbelievers, whereas the Quran states: "الْيَوْمَ يَئِسَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا..." (This day have those who disbelieved despaired...), meaning all disbelievers despaired.
  • As for Hajjat al-Wada', where people learned the rituals of Hajj in the presence of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله, only the people's Hajj was perfected through the Prophet's teaching, not the entire religion, whereas the Quran states: "الْيَوْمَ أَكْمَلْتُ لَكُمْ دِينَكُمْ" (This day I have perfected for you your religion).
  • As for Ghadir Khumm, it is the day when Allah issued the command to appoint Hazrat Ali as the successor to the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله. Only that day corresponds to the four titles mentioned in the Ayah: Akmal-tu (perfected), Atmam-tu (completed), Raḍī-tu (approved), and Ya'isa alladhīna kafarū (those who disbelieved despaired).
  • As for the despair of the disbelievers, it was because when slander, war, and assassination attempts against the Prophet proved fruitless, their only hope was the death of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله. The appointment of Ali ibn Abi Talib علیهما السلام made everyone understand that even if the Prophet had a son, he would not be better than Ali, and His religion would not vanish with His death, because a person like Ali ibn Abi Talib علیهما السلام would be the successor to the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله and the leader of the Islamic nation. It was at this point that all the disbelievers despaired.
  • As for the perfection of the religion, it is because if complete regulations and laws are established, but an infallible and perfect leader is not appointed for the nation and society, the regulations remain incomplete.
  • As for the completion of the favor, it is because the Quran has introduced the favor of leadership and guidance as the greatest favor. If the Holy Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله were to pass away and leave the people without a guardian, He would have done something that even a shepherd would not do to his flock. How can the favor be completed without the appointment of a divine leader?
  • As for the approval of God, it is because whenever a complete law and a just executor are linked together, the satisfaction of the Lord is achieved.

If each of the perfection of religion, the completion of favor, the approval of the Truth (God), and the despair of the disbelievers were to occur on a single day, that would be enough for that day to be counted among the Days of Allah (Ayyām Allāh). How much more so for a day like Ghadir, which possesses all these characteristics simultaneously. For this reason, in the narrations of the Ahl al-Bayt علیهم السلام, the Eid of Ghadir is considered one of the greatest Eids.

The effects of things are sometimes dependent on the presence of all their components, such as fasting, which becomes void if we break the fast even a moment before the call to prayer. For this reason, the word tamām (completion) is used: "أَتِمُّوا الصِّيَامَ إِلَى اللَّيْلِ" (Complete the fast until the night). And sometimes, the effect is such that every part has its own effect, such as reciting the verses of the Quran, whose perfection lies in reciting all of it, but however much is read, there is reward. Sometimes, certain components are such that if they are absent, the whole set is incomplete, even if all other components are present, like a pilot or a driver, without whom the airplane or car is fruitless and useless. Righteous leadership and Wilayah are also like this, because they link man to God, and without them, creations and favors turn into misfortunes and do not lead man to God.

In Ayah 109 of Surah Al-Baqarah, the inclination of the disbelievers to divert Muslims from their path is mentioned. Allah commanded: You should overlook this until Allah's command arrives. Thus, Muslims were awaiting a decisive ruling that would cause the disbelievers to despair, until the Ayah "الْيَوْمَ أَكْمَلْتُ..." was revealed.

A Look at Nutrition in Islam Islam is a school of justice; it neither recommends excessive meat consumption like Westerners, nor does it prohibit it like Buddhists, nor does it permit eating the meat of any animal in any form like the Chinese. In Islam, there are conditions and limits for meat consumption, including:

A: Do not eat the meat of carnivorous animals, as they carry various microbial and parasitic contaminations, etc. B: Do not eat the meat of beasts of prey, lest the spirit of cruelty and savagery develop within you. C: Do not consume the meat of animals that cause general revulsion. D: Do not eat the meat of an animal over which the blessed Name of God was not pronounced at the time of slaughter. E: Do not eat carrion. Because when an animal dies, the blood spoils before anything else and causes a type of poisoning. Therefore, the consumption of animals that have been strangled, gored, fallen, beaten, or mauled—whose death does not involve the complete draining of blood—is unlawful (haram) in Islam. (17)

Of all the animals whose meat is unlawful, only the name of the pig is mentioned in this Ayah because its consumption was common. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said regarding carrion: No one approached it except that weakness, emaciation, lethargy, infertility, stroke, and sudden death afflicted him. Blood consumption was a custom of the Age of Ignorance (Jahiliyyah). This act causes hardness of heart and mercilessness, to the extent that it carries the risk of killing one's child or parents. A blood consumer recognizes neither companion nor friend. (18) Therefore, consuming blood is unlawful, but injecting it is permissible.

Question: Is the permission to slaughter animals for meat consumption compatible with Divine Mercy? Answer: The basis of creation is transformation and change. Soil becomes plant, plant becomes animal, and animal transforms into human, and the result of these transformations is growth.

(15) Tafsir Qurtubi. (16) Kafi, Vol. 1, p. 289. (17) Tafsir Namūneh, Vol. 4, p. 259. (18) Tafsir Al-Mizan.

Messages

1- Islam is a comprehensive religion that addresses and gives explicit rulings on all natural and spiritual needs of man. "حُرِّمَتْ" (have been made unlawful)

2- The role of food in the health of the body and soul is so significant that it has been repeatedly emphasized in the Quran. "حُرِّمَتْ عَلَيْكُمْ..." (have been made unlawful to you...)

3- In the monotheistic system, the slaughter of an animal must also have a divine aspect, otherwise, it becomes unlawful. "وَمَا أُهِلَّ لِغَيْرِ اللَّهِ بِهِ" (and what has been dedicated to other than Allah)

4- If a beaten, gored, fallen, or mauled animal still has life in its body and you slaughter it, it becomes lawful (halal). "إِلَّا مَا ذَكَّيْتُمْ" (except that which you [manage to] slaughter)

5- All manifestations of polytheism (shirk), in whatever form, must be fought against. "وَمَا ذُبِحَ عَلَى النُّصُبِ" (and what was sacrificed on the stone altars)

6- Although meat distributed through gambling arrows and sticks is unlawful, it seems that neither the meat nor the means of distribution (the arrows/sticks) is the specific issue, but rather, any income derived from gambling is unlawful. "أَنْ تَسْتَقْسِمُوا بِالْأَزْلَامِ" (that you seek division through divining arrows)

7- The most important glimmer of hope for the disbelievers was the death of the Prophet, and that glimmer was closed by the appointment of Hazrat Ali عليه السلام to leadership. "الْيَوْمَ يَئِسَ..." (This day have despaired...)

8- If the leader of Ghadir is present in society, Muslims should have no fear. "فَلَا تَخْشَوْهُمْ" (so fear them not)

9- Even if the external enemy leaves you alone, there is an internal enemy that must be confronted with the fear of God. "فَلَا تَخْشَوْهُمْ وَاخْشَوْنِ" (so fear them not, but fear Me)

10- If Muslims had not abandoned the path of Ghadir's leadership, no danger from the disbelievers would have afflicted them. "فَلَا تَخْشَوْهُمْ" (so fear them not)

11- Disbelievers fear a complete religion, not a religion whose leader is submissive, whose Jihad is suspended, whose resources are plundered, and whose people are scattered. "الْيَوْمَ يَئِسَ... الْيَوْمَ أَكْمَلْتُ لَكُمْ دِينَكُمْ" (This day have despaired... This day I have perfected for you your religion)

12- If the disbelievers have not despaired of you, perhaps there is a deficiency in your religion regarding leadership. "الْيَوْمَ يَئِسَ... الْيَوْمَ أَكْمَلْتُ" (This day have despaired... This day I have perfected)

13- Religion without a leader is not complete. "أَكْمَلْتُ لَكُمْ دِينَكُمْ" (I have perfected for you your religion)

14- The stability of the school (of thought) depends on correct leadership, and only with its existence do the disbelievers despair, not with anything else. "الْيَوْمَ يَئِسَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا... الْيَوْمَ أَكْمَلْتُ لَكُمْ دِينَكُمْ" (This day have those who disbelieved despaired... This day I have perfected for you your religion)

15- All favors are incomplete without divine leadership. "الْيَوْمَ... أَتْمَمْتُ عَلَيْكُمْ نِعْمَتِي" (This day... I have completed My favor upon you)

16- The appointment of Ali عليه السلام to the Imamate is the completion of the favor, "أَتْمَمْتُ عَلَيْكُمْ نِعْمَتِي," and abandoning His Wilayah is ingratitude for the favor, and ingratitude for the favor has dire consequences. (19) "فَكَفَرَتْ بِأَنْعُمِ اللَّهِ فَأَذَاقَهَا اللَّهُ لِبَاسَ الْجُوعِ وَالْخَوْفِ بِمَا كَانُوا يَصْنَعُونَ" (but it was ungrateful for the favors of Allah, so Allah made it taste the garment of hunger and fear because of what they used to do)

17- Islam without a leader is not acceptable to God. "الْيَوْمَ رَضِيتُ لَكُمُ الْإِسْلَامَ" (This day I have approved for you Islam)

18- Involuntary necessity (ḍarūrah) permits the consumption of the unlawful; therefore, man should not voluntarily make himself desperate. "اضْطُرَّ" (The verb is passive.)

19- A law is complete when it pays attention to all human conditions and circumstances, especially the state of necessity and distress. Indeed, there is no deadlock in the rulings of Islam. "فَمَنِ اضْطُرَّ" (But whoever is compelled)

20- The special conditions of necessity should not lead to sin or absolute license; rather, one must suffice with only the amount necessary to relieve the distress. "غَيْرَ مُتَجَانِفٍ لِإِثْمٍ" (without inclining toward sin)

21- We must not misuse the facilities provided for those in distress. "غَيْرَ مُتَجَانِفٍ لِإِثْمٍ" Imam Baqir عليه السلام said in the interpretation of this phrase: Ghayra mutajānifin is one who does not deliberately seek out sin. (20)

(19) An-Nahl, 112. (20) Tafsir Burhan, Vol. 1, p. 447.

Ayah

يَسْـَٔلُونَكَ مَاذَآ أُحِلَّ لَهُمْ ۖ قُلْ أُحِلَّ لَكُمُ ٱلطَّيِّبَـٰتُ ۙ وَمَا عَلَّمْتُم مِّنَ ٱلْجَوَارِحِ مُكَلِّبِينَ تُعَلِّمُونَهُنَّ مِمَّا عَلَّمَكُمُ ٱللَّهُ ۖ فَكُلُوا۟ مِمَّآ أَمْسَكْنَ عَلَيْكُمْ وَٱذْكُرُوا۟ ٱسْمَ ٱللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ ۖ وَٱتَّقُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ سَرِيعُ ٱلْحِسَابِ (٤)
They ask you as to what is lawful to them. Say, ‘All the good things are lawful to you.’ As for what you have taught hunting dogs [to catch], teaching them out of what Allah has taught you, eat of what they catch for you and mention Allah’s Name over it, and be wary of Allah. Indeed Allah is swift at reckoning. (4)

Key Points

«جَوارح» (Jawāriḥ) is the plural of «جارحة» (Jāriḥah), derived from the root «جَرْح» (Jarḥ), and is used in two meanings: acquisition and wounding. A hunting animal is called Jāriḥah because it wounds the prey, or because it acquires prey for its owner. The limbs of the body, which are means of acquisition and effort, are also called Jawāriḥ. Considering the word «مُكلّبین» (Mukallibīn), derived from the root «كلب» (Kalb), meaning dog, only the catch of trained dogs is lawful (Halal), not the catch of other hunting animals. Furthermore, this applies only to prey that the dog was sent after and held onto, not prey that the dog hunted for itself, the consumption of which is unlawful (Haram).

Messages

1- The Prophets respond to the questions and natural needs of society. «یسألونك»

2- What we do not know, we must ask the Prophets and their inheritors. «یسألونك» (The Muslims in the early days of Islam felt responsible for learning the rulings and religious duties.)

3- Asking questions is a value and a sign of feeling responsible. «یسئلونك»

4- Questions are the basis for the gradual revelation of the verses of the Quran. «یسئلونك»

5- The relationship between the Ummah (community) and the Prophet is direct. «یسئلونك»

6- Islam has given importance to the issue of nutrition. «یسألونك ماذا اُحلّ لهم»

7- Any food that has been made unlawful (Haram) is due to its manifest or hidden impurity. «اُحلّ لكم الطیّبات»

8- The general principle and rule is that all pure and pleasing things are lawful (Halal). «اُحلّ لكم الطیّبات»

9- Since Islam is a natural religion (Fitri), it makes lawful (Halal) whatever the heart approves of and contains no corruption. «اُحلّ لكم الطیّبات» (There is harmony between legislation (Tashrī‘) and creation (Takwīn).)

10- Education is not limited to humans; animals are also capable of being taught and subdued. «تعلّمونهنّ»

11- Knowledge gives value even to a dog and increases its effectiveness. «تعلّمونهنّ» With a little training, the dog offers its lifetime catch to its teacher. «أمسكن علیكم» But what do some humans, who have learned everything from Allah, «علّمكم اللّه» do for Him?

12- A portion of the hunted meat is for the hunting animal. «فكلوا ممّا امسكن علیكم» (The particle «مِن» (min) means eat a portion of it.)

13- When sending the hunting dog, the Name of Allah must be mentioned. «واذكروا اسم اللّه علیه»

14- Observing Divine regulations in meat consumption is a manifestation of piety (Taqwa). «كلوا... واتّقوا»

15- Among wild animals, some are lawful to eat (Halal). (Hunting is a sign of the animal being wild.) «فكلوا ممّا امسكن علیكم»

16- Attention to the Resurrection (Ma'ad) and Allah's swift reckoning is one of the motivations for piety (Taqwa). «اتّقوا اللّه انّ اللّه سریع الحساب»

Ayah

ٱلْيَوْمَ أُحِلَّ لَكُمُ ٱلطَّيِّبَـٰتُ ۖ وَطَعَامُ ٱلَّذِينَ أُوتُوا۟ ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ حِلٌّ لَّكُمْ وَطَعَامُكُمْ حِلٌّ لَّهُمْ ۖ وَٱلْمُحْصَنَـٰتُ مِنَ ٱلْمُؤْمِنَـٰتِ وَٱلْمُحْصَنَـٰتُ مِنَ ٱلَّذِينَ أُوتُوا۟ ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ مِن قَبْلِكُمْ إِذَآ ءَاتَيْتُمُوهُنَّ أُجُورَهُنَّ مُحْصِنِينَ غَيْرَ مُسَـٰفِحِينَ وَلَا مُتَّخِذِىٓ أَخْدَانٍ ۗ وَمَن يَكْفُرْ بِٱلْإِيمَـٰنِ فَقَدْ حَبِطَ عَمَلُهُۥ وَهُوَ فِى ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ مِنَ ٱلْخَـٰسِرِينَ (٥)
Today all the good things have been made lawful to you—the food of those who were given the Book is lawful to you, and your food is lawful to them—and the chaste ones from among faithful women, and chaste women of those who were given the Book before you, when you have given them their dowries, in wedlock, not in license, nor taking paramours. Should anyone renounce his faith, his work shall fail and he will be among the losers in the Hereafter. (5)

Key Points

This Ayah states that the food (ta'am) of the People of the Book is lawful (halal) for Muslims. However, considering that in the preceding verses, mentioning the Name of Allah was stipulated as a condition for the lawfulness of consuming meat, we understand that the intent behind the lawfulness of the food of the Jews and Christians for Muslims is non-meat foods. In the narrations of the Ahl al-Bayt (عليهم السلام), the meaning of ta'am (food) has been understood as wheat, barley, and grains. Some lexicographers, such as Ibn Athir and Khalil, have also stated that in the culture of Hijaz, wheat is also referred to as ta'am. (21)

Since this Ayah has opened the way for interaction, eating, and marriage with religious minorities, and it is possible that Muslims, in order to reach Jewish and Christian women, might increase their daily interactions and gradually fall under the influence of their thoughts and customs, thereby deviating from the path of Islam, the end of the Ayah warns that whoever disregards his faith, his deed is rendered void. «و من یكفر بالایمان فقد حبط عمله»

Akhdān (أخدان), the plural of khidn (خِدْن), means friend, but it is usually used in the context of secret and illicit friendships.

Regarding food, the Ayah permits both parties—Muslims and the People of the Book—to consume each other's food: «اُحلّ لكم... حلّ لهم». However, regarding marriage, it only permits marrying women from the People of the Book and does not permit giving Muslim women in marriage to them: «المحصنات من الّذین اوتوا الكتاب». (This is because women, due to their emotions, are usually influenced by men. Therefore, if women from the People of the Book embrace Islam, it is growth; but if a Muslim woman inclines toward them, it is a downfall. For this reason, giving Muslim women in marriage to the People of the Book is not permissible.)

Muhsin (مُحصِن) and Muhsanah (مُحصَنة) mean being chaste, being free, having a spouse, and being Muslim. Here, the intent of muhsanāt (محصنات) is chaste women, otherwise, marriage with a married woman is definitely forbidden (haram).

The intent behind the permissibility of marriage with the People of the Book in this Ayah is temporary marriage (Mut'ah), (22) due to numerous narrations and because of the word ujūrahunna (اُجورهنّ), which is mostly used for the dowry (Mahr) of temporary marriage.

Whoever acknowledges the truth of something but does not act upon it, his deed is rendered void. Imam Sadiq (عليه السلام), regarding the Ayah «و مَن یكفر بالایمان فقد حبط عمله», said: «تَرك العمل الّذى اقرّ به» (Abandoning the deed which he acknowledged). (23)

In another narration, Imam Sadiq (عليه السلام) said: The least thing for which a person exits faith is that he finds an opinion contrary to the truth, yet he insists upon that opinion and defends it. Then His Holiness recited this Ayah: «و مَن یكفر بالایمان فقد حبط عمله». (24)

21) Tafsir al-Mizan; Kafi, Vol. 6, p. 341. 22) In this matter, everyone must consult the view of their own Marja' al-Taqlid (source of emulation). 23) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn, Vol. 1, p. 595. 24) Wasā'il, Vol. 27, p. 60.

Messages

1- In stating rulings and laws, the element of time must not be overlooked. «الیوم» (Today).

2- Legislative laws are in harmony with the cosmic order. (Where sound nature approves, the law also makes it lawful.) «اُحلّ لكم الطیّبات»

3- The chastity of a woman, regardless of her religion, is a value. «والمحصنات من المؤمنات و المحصنات من الّذین اُوتوا الكتاب»

4- Chastity is a condition for both men and women. «المحصنات, محصِنین»

5- In choosing a spouse, attention must be paid to the principle of faith and chastity. «والمحصنات من المؤمنات»

6- Deception and fraud are forbidden, even against non-Muslims. «آتیتموهنّ اُجورهنّ» (Observing the economic right of a woman is obligatory, even if she is not Muslim.)

7- A woman has the right of ownership, whether Muslim or non-Muslim. «اُجورهنّ»

8- The dowry (Mahr) must be given to the woman herself. «آتیتموهنّ اجورهنّ» (You give them their wages), and He did not say: «آتیتم اجورهنّ» (You give their wages).

9- The payment of the wage to the woman must be for marriage, not for fornication (Zina) or illicit relationships. «آتیتموهنّ اجورهنّ محصنین غیر مسافحین ولا متّخذى اخدان»

10- First, show the lawful path, then block the deviant path. «و المحصنات من المؤمنات... محصنین غیر مسافحین»

11- Establishing illicit and secret relationships is forbidden, even with non-Muslims. «و لامتّخذى أخدان»

12- Eating the food of the People of the Book and marrying chaste women from among them is lawful. The phrase «احلّ لكم... والمحصنات من الذین اوتوا الكتاب من قبلكم»

13- Mixing with the People of the Book, living among them, and traveling to their countries is one of the pitfalls. Therefore, in this Ayah, after permitting interaction, He warns that you must be careful that economic and familial relationships do not change your belief, and that you do not lose your faith for the sake of obtaining a spouse. «و من یكفر بالایمان»

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوٓا۟ إِذَا قُمْتُمْ إِلَى ٱلصَّلَوٰةِ فَٱغْسِلُوا۟ وُجُوهَكُمْ وَأَيْدِيَكُمْ إِلَى ٱلْمَرَافِقِ وَٱمْسَحُوا۟ بِرُءُوسِكُمْ وَأَرْجُلَكُمْ إِلَى ٱلْكَعْبَيْنِ ۚ وَإِن كُنتُمْ جُنُبًا فَٱطَّهَّرُوا۟ ۚ وَإِن كُنتُم مَّرْضَىٰٓ أَوْ عَلَىٰ سَفَرٍ أَوْ جَآءَ أَحَدٌ مِّنكُم مِّنَ ٱلْغَآئِطِ أَوْ لَـٰمَسْتُمُ ٱلنِّسَآءَ فَلَمْ تَجِدُوا۟ مَآءً فَتَيَمَّمُوا۟ صَعِيدًا طَيِّبًا فَٱمْسَحُوا۟ بِوُجُوهِكُمْ وَأَيْدِيكُم مِّنْهُ ۚ مَا يُرِيدُ ٱللَّهُ لِيَجْعَلَ عَلَيْكُم مِّنْ حَرَجٍ وَلَـٰكِن يُرِيدُ لِيُطَهِّرَكُمْ وَلِيُتِمَّ نِعْمَتَهُۥ عَلَيْكُمْ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَشْكُرُونَ (٦)
O you who have faith! When you stand up for prayer, wash your faces and your hands up to the elbows, and wipe a part of your heads and your feet, up to the ankles. If you are junub, purify yourselves. But if you are sick, or on a journey, or any of you has come from the toilet, or you have touched women, and you cannot find water, then make tayammum with clean ground and wipe a part of your faces and your hands with it. Allah does not desire to put you to hardship, but He desires to purify you, and to complete His blessing upon you so that you may give thanks. (6)

Key Points

In Ayah 43 of Surah An-Nisa, the topics of Ghusl (ritual bath) and Tayammum (dry ablution) were mentioned; here, in addition to those two, the issue of Wudu (ablution) is also mentioned. The word qiyām (قیام), whenever accompanied by the particle ilā (الى), means intending or resolving. "اذا قُمتم الى الصّلاة" means whenever you resolve to perform the prayer. The word junub (جُنب) applies equally to male and female, singular and plural. Perhaps in this Ayah, the intent of becoming junub is only nocturnal emission, and the intent of touching women is sexual intercourse. The intent of the command "فاطّهروا" (purify yourselves) is to perform Ghusl, based on the evidence that in Ayah 43 of An-Nisa, instead of "فاطّهّروا," He stated "تغتسلوا" (wash yourselves). The restriction "الى المرافق" (up to the elbows) is for defining the boundary of washing, not the direction of washing. Since the word yad (یَد) in the Arabic language refers both to the hand (from the fingers to the wrist), and the hand (from the fingers to the elbow), and the hand (from the fingers to the shoulder), this Ayah states: in Wudu, wash the hand up to the elbow, neither less nor more. The spirit of servitude is inherent in Tayammum, because striking the hands on the earth and wiping it on the forehead—which is the highest part of the body—along with the intention of seeking closeness (to Allah), is a form of humility and submission before Allah. Just as water removes impurity, pure earth also has germicidal properties, as it is exposed to sunlight and rainfall. Sa'īd (صَعید) comes from su'ūd (صعود), meaning high ground. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام, in interpreting "صعیداً طیّباً" (pure earth), said: High ground from which water flows down. [25] Indeed, low ground where polluted water collects is not suitable for Tayammum, because the condition for Tayammum is pure earth. We read in a Hadith: "لا صلاة الاّ بطهور" [26], prayer is not valid except with purification (Wudu, Ghusl, or Tayammum). We read in a Hadith: "اِبدأ بما بدء اللّه" [27] (Begin with what Allah began with). Just as the Quran mentions washing the face first, then the hands, then wiping the head, and then wiping the feet, the order of Wudu is likewise. The Imam was asked why only a part of the head is wiped in Wudu? He replied: "لمكان الباء" (Because of the letter bā in the word "برؤسكم"), because its meaning implies a part of the head. If the Ayah had been "وامسحوا رؤسكم" (Wipe your heads), then we would have had to wipe the entire head. [28] Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: The intent of "لامستم" (you touched) is sexual intercourse, but Allah has veiled it, and He loves veiling matters; therefore, He did not name it the way you name it. [29] Imam Reza عليه السلام said regarding the philosophy of Wudu: * "یكون العبد طاهراً اذا قام بین یدى الجبّار" (It provides the basis for purity when the servant stands before the Almighty). * "مطیعاً له فیما أمره" (It is a sign of servitude and obedience to Him in what He has commanded). * "نقیّاً من الادناس و النّجاسة" (It is a factor in avoiding impurities and filth). * "ذهاب الكسل و طرد النّعاس" (It causes the removal of laziness and the expulsion of drowsiness). * "وتزكیة الفؤاد للقیام" (It is the preparation and spiritual growth of the heart for standing in prayer). [30]

25) Ma'ani al-Akhbar, p. 283. 26) Tahdhib, Vol. 1, p. 49. 27) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 28) Kafi, Vol. 3, p. 30. 29) Kafi, Vol. 5, p. 555. 30) Wasā'il, Vol. 1, p. 367.

Messages

1- Cleanliness and purification are prerequisites of faith. "یا ایّها الّذین آمنوا... فاغسلوا... فاطّهروا" (O you who have believed... wash... purify yourselves).

2- Purification is a condition for prayer. "فاغسلوا" (wash).

3- Just as touching the Quran is exclusive to the pure, [31] communication with Allah also requires purification. "فاغسلوا" (wash).

4- Physical impurity prevents closeness to Allah. "اذا قمتم الى الصلوة فاغسلوا" (When you rise to [perform] the prayer, wash).

5- The principle in speech is the observance of modesty, chastity, and etiquette, especially concerning marital issues. "لامستم النساء" (you touched women). However, in legal matters, so that no right is lost, the law is stated clearly and explicitly. Examples include the Ayah "دخلتم بهنّ" [32] (you have gone in unto them), which concerns the dowry, or the phrase "احصنت فرجها" [33] (she guarded her private part), which concerns clearing the accusation against Lady Maryam (Mary).

6- The conditions and preliminaries of prayer are subject to alleviation (relief), but not to cancellation. "فلم تجدوا ماءً فتیمموا" (and you find no water, then perform Tayammum).

7- One must strive to find water for Wudu and Ghusl; only if it is not found does the turn for Tayammum arrive. ("فلم تجدوا" [and you find no] applies where a person strives but does not find it.)

8- Communication with the Pure God cannot be established using polluted water or earth. "صعیداً طیّباً" (pure earth).

9- Just as edibles must be pure (tayyib), which was mentioned in the two preceding Ayahs, Tayammum must also be performed with pure earth (tayyib). "صعیداً طیّباً" (pure earth).

10- There is no hardship or difficulty (haraj) in the rulings of the religion. "ما یرید اللّه... من حرج" (Allah does not intend... any difficulty).

11- The goal of Wudu, Ghusl, and Tayammum is spiritual purification and preparation for communication with Allah. " لیطهّركم" (that He may purify you).

12- Divine obligations are a blessing for mankind. "لیتمّ نعمته علیكم" (that He may complete His favor upon you).

13- Fulfilling obligations is one of the manifestations of thanking Allah. "لعلّكم تشكرون" (that you may be grateful).

31) "لایمسّه الاّ المطهّرون". Al-Waqi'ah, 79.

32) An-Nisa, 23.

33) Al-Anbiya, 91.

Ayah

وَٱذْكُرُوا۟ نِعْمَةَ ٱللَّهِ عَلَيْكُمْ وَمِيثَـٰقَهُ ٱلَّذِى وَاثَقَكُم بِهِۦٓ إِذْ قُلْتُمْ سَمِعْنَا وَأَطَعْنَا ۖ وَٱتَّقُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ عَلِيمٌۢ بِذَاتِ ٱلصُّدُورِ (۷)
Remember Allah’s blessing upon you and His covenant with which He has bound you when you said, ‘We hear and obey.’ And be wary of Allah. Indeed Allah knows best what is in the breasts. (7)

Key Points

Although the subject of remembering the blessing and observing the covenant mentioned in the Ayah is a general and universal warning, it is possible that for certain reasons, the issue of the leadership of the Islamic society and obedience to him (the leader) is intended. Those reasons include: 1- Divine leadership is a covenant and pact from God. The story of Prophet Ibrahim (Abraham) and his request for Imamat (leadership) for his descendants, and God's response that the wrongdoers shall not attain My covenant (Imamat), is evidence for this matter. «لاینال عهدى الظالمین» (34) 2- After the appointment of Hadrat Ali عليه السلام to the Imamat at Ghadir Khumm, the third Ayah of this very Surah was revealed, stating that today I have perfected My blessing upon you. 3- At Ghadir Khumm, the people pledged allegiance (Bay'ah) to Ali عليه السلام and said, "We hear and we obey" (سمعاً و طاعةً). This Ayah once again calls the people to loyalty and steadfastness regarding the leadership. The covenant of hearing and obeying, «سمعنا و أطعنا», includes all covenants that humans naturally and instinctively made, verbally or practically, with the Prophets, such as their pledges of allegiance (Bay'ah) and their testimonies to Monotheism (Tawhid) and Prophethood (Nubuwwah).

(34) Baqarah, 124.

Messages

1- The emphasis of this Ayah on concepts such as blessing (Ni'mah), covenant (Mithaq), obedience (Ita'ah), and knowledge of what is in the breasts (علم به ذات الصّدور), especially after the Ayah of Ghadir Khumm, may be a reference to the forgetting of the content of the third Ayah and deviation from the path of Ghadir. (As Imam Baqir عليه السلام stated. (35)) «واذكروا نعمةاللَّه...»

2- Remembering divine blessings is necessary. «واذكروا نعمة اللَّه»

3- Remembering and recalling blessings, covenants, sayings, and God's knowledge and awareness is a factor and basis for piety (Taqwa). «واذكروا نعمة اللَّه...»

4- We should not contemplate breaking covenants, for God is aware. «اتّقوااللّه انّ اللّه علیم بذات الصّدور»

(35) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ كُونُوا۟ قَوَّٰمِينَ لِلَّهِ شُهَدَآءَ بِٱلْقِسْطِ ۖ وَلَا يَجْرِمَنَّكُمْ شَنَـَٔانُ قَوْمٍ عَلَىٰٓ أَلَّا تَعْدِلُوا۟ ۚ ٱعْدِلُوا۟ هُوَ أَقْرَبُ لِلتَّقْوَىٰ ۖ وَٱتَّقُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ خَبِيرٌۢ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ (۸)
O you who have faith! Be maintainers, as witnesses for the sake of Allah, of justice, and ill feeling for a people should never lead you to be unfair. Be fair; that is nearer to Godwariness, and be wary of Allah. Allah is indeed well aware of what you do. (8)

Key Points

A similar verse, with slight differences, appears in Ayah 135 of Surah An-Nisa: «قوّامین بالقسط شهداء للّه ولو على أنفسكم أو الوالدین والاقربین» (Be maintainers of justice, witnesses for Allah, even if it is against yourselves or your parents and relatives). Establish justice and be witnesses for Allah, even if it is to the detriment of yourselves or your parents and relatives. The difference between these two verses is that this verse commands: do not let hatreds and enmities divert you, while the verse in Surah An-Nisa states: do not let affections and attachments lead you outside the bounds of justice. Indeed, the factors leading to deviation from justice are either malice and hatred, or love and affection, and each verse refers to one of them. Since ignoring internal hatreds regarding people is difficult, several commands and encouragements are used in this verse: «لایجرمنّكم... اعدلوا... هو اقرب... اتّقوا اللّه...»

Messages

1- Social justice is established only in the light of faith in Allah and the Resurrection. «یا أیها الّذین آمنوا... اعدلوا»

2- Justice is not merely a moral value; it is a definite Divine command. «كونوا»

3- If a person's motivation becomes their hatreds, there is no sincerity in the action, but if the uprising is for Allah, hatreds become ineffective in the execution of justice. «قوّامین للّه... لایجرمنّكم»

4- Continuous justice, as a habit and disposition, is valuable, not momentary justice. «كونوا... شهداء بالقسط...»

5- Believers maintain a relationship with Allah. «قوّامین للّه» and also bear witness to justice in their relationship with people. «شهداء بالقسط»

6- If we believe that Allah knows our actions, we will behave justly. «شهداء بالقسط... انّ اللّه خبیر بما تعملون»

7- Vengeful and resentful people cannot be just. (To achieve justice, hatreds must be controlled.) «لا یجرمنّكم»

8- Emotions must be subordinate to justice. «لا یجرمنّكم... اعدلوا»

9- Vengefulness is one of the factors leading to deviation from justice. «شنآن قوم على الاّ تعدلوا»

10- In policymaking and internal and external relations, we must be just, even towards enemies. «شنآن قوم»

11- Just and fair people are closer to piety (Taqwa). «اعدلوا هو اقرب للتّقوى»

Ayahs

وَعَدَ ٱللَّهُ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَعَمِلُوا۟ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَـٰتِ ۙ لَهُم مَّغْفِرَةٌ وَأَجْرٌ عَظِيمٌ (۹)
Allah has promised those who have faith and do righteous deeds forgiveness and a great reward. (9)
وَٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ وَكَذَّبُوا۟ بِـَٔايَـٰتِنَآ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ أَصْحَـٰبُ ٱلْجَحِيمِ (۱٠)
As for those who are faithless and deny Our signs, they shall be the inmates of hell. (10)

Key Points

Several types of ajr (reward) are used in the Quran: "اجر عظیم" (great reward), "اجر كبیر" [36], "اجر كریم" [37], "اجر غیر ممنون" [38]. Therefore, divine rewards possess different degrees. The promises of Allah, "وعد اللّه..." (Allah has promised...), are inviolable: "انّ اللّه لایخلف المیعاد" (Indeed, Allah does not break the promise) [39]. "جحیم" (Jahīm) means the intensity of the fire's blaze. In the story of Abraham (عليه السلام), it is mentioned that they cast him into the Jahīm (the blazing fire). "اصحاب الجحیم" (Companions of the Jahīm) means those who are perpetually in Hellfire.

36) Hud, 11. 37) Ya Sin, 11. 38) Fussilat, 8. 39) Al Imran, 9.

Messages

1- Faith is not separate from action. "آمنوا وعملوا الصالحات"

2- Faith and righteous deeds both compensate for the past ("لهم مغفرة" - for them is forgiveness) and secure the future ("أجر عظیم" - a great reward).

3- Attention to the outcome of actions plays an important role in human decisions. "أجر عظیم... اصحاب الجحیم"

4- Forgiveness is the prerequisite for receiving the reward. "لهم مغفرة واجر"

5- The punishment for the disbelievers and deniers is eternal Hellfire. "والّذین كفروا... اصحاب الجحیم"

6- Encouragement is a motivation for action. "لهم مغفرة واجر عظیم," just as warning (threat) is one of the educational methods of the Quran. "اصحاب الجحیم"

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ ٱذْكُرُوا۟ نِعْمَتَ ٱللَّهِ عَلَيْكُمْ إِذْ هَمَّ قَوْمٌ أَن يَبْسُطُوٓا۟ إِلَيْكُمْ أَيْدِيَهُمْ فَكَفَّ أَيْدِيَهُمْ عَنكُمْ ۖ وَٱتَّقُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ ۚ وَعَلَى ٱللَّهِ فَلْيَتَوَكَّلِ ٱلْمُؤْمِنُونَ (۱۱)
O you who have faith! Remember Allah’s blessing upon you when a people set out to extend their hands against you, but He withheld their hands from you, and be wary of Allah, and in Allah let all the faithful put their trust. (11)

Key Points

| There is disagreement regarding which historical event the verse refers to, but it can be considered inclusive of all cases where Muslims, relying on Allah, were victorious and saved in the face of the enemy's malicious intent or aggression.

Messages

| 1- Remembering Allah's blessings upon the believers is necessary. «یا ایّها الّذین آمنوا اُذكروا نعمة اللّه» (Because it is a form of gratitude, it keeps arrogance and heedlessness away from man, and it increases man's love for the Lord.) 2- Warding off the dangers posed by the enemy is among the most important blessings of Allah. «كفّ ایدیهم عنكم» 3- Through piety (taqwa), reliance (tawakkul), and faith, we attract Allah's favor and ward off the danger of enemies. «كفّ ایدیهم عنكم و اتّقواللّه وعلى اللّه فلیتوكّل المؤمنون» (Just as Allah allows the enemy to dominate man because of sin, attention to Him also causes the repulsion of enemies.) 4- Your enemies are striving to gain control over you. «هَمّ َقوم» 5- The prerequisite of faith and piety is reliance upon Allah. «واتّقوا اللّه وعلى اللّه فلیتوكّل المؤمنون» 6- Possessing piety and reliance is the result of remembering Allah and having faith. «اذكروا نعمة اللّه... واتّقوا اللّه... فلیتوكّل المؤمنون»

Ayah

۞ وَلَقَدْ أَخَذَ ٱللَّهُ مِيثَـٰقَ بَنِىٓ إِسْرَٰٓءِيلَ وَبَعَثْنَا مِنْهُمُ ٱثْنَىْ عَشَرَ نَقِيبًا ۖ وَقَالَ ٱللَّهُ إِنِّى مَعَكُمْ ۖ لَئِنْ أَقَمْتُمُ ٱلصَّلَوٰةَ وَءَاتَيْتُمُ ٱلزَّكَوٰةَ وَءَامَنتُم بِرُسُلِى وَعَزَّرْتُمُوهُمْ وَأَقْرَضْتُمُ ٱللَّهَ قَرْضًا حَسَنًا لَّأُكَفِّرَنَّ عَنكُمْ سَيِّـَٔاتِكُمْ وَلَأُدْخِلَنَّكُمْ جَنَّـٰتٍ تَجْرِى مِن تَحْتِهَا ٱلْأَنْهَـٰرُ ۚ فَمَن كَفَرَ بَعْدَ ذَٰلِكَ مِنكُمْ فَقَدْ ضَلَّ سَوَآءَ ٱلسَّبِيلِ (۱۲)
Certainly Allah took a pledge from the Children of Israel, and We raised among them twelve chiefs. And Allah said, ‘I am with you! Surely, if you maintain the prayer and give the zakat and have faith in My apostles and support them and lend Allah a good loan, I will surely absolve you of your misdeeds, and I will surely admit you into gardens with streams running in them. But whoever of you disbelieves after that has certainly strayed from the right way.’ (12)

Key Points

The term «عزّرتموهم» (Azzartumūhum) is derived from «عَزّر» (Azzara), meaning to help or support with respect. «سواء السبیل» (Sawā' al-Sabīl) refers to the middle of the path, deviation from which leads to downfall. The Nuqabā' (chiefs/leaders) of the Children of Israel were the ministers of Prophet Moses عليه السلام and the supervisors of their twelve tribes. It is narrated from the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله that he said: The Caliphs after me are twelve, corresponding to the number of the Nuqabā' of the Children of Israel. [40] Opponents of the path of the Ahl al-Bayt (the Household of the Prophet) tried hard to apply this number to the Rightly Guided Caliphs, the Umayyad Caliphs, and the Abbasid Caliphs, even though it does not fit any of them. This futile effort occurs despite the fact that dozens of narrations from the tongue of the Prophet of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله, which include the names of these twelve individuals—the first of whom is Ali عليه السلام and the last is Imam Mahdi عليه السلام—are found in reliable Shi'a and Sunni books.

40) Musnad Ahmad ibn Hanbal, Vol. 1, p. 398.

Messages

1- Prayer (Salat), Zakat (alms), supporting the Prophets, and spending (in charity) were also part of the programs of other divine religions. «میثاق بنى اسرائیل...» (The covenant of the Children of Israel...)

2- Allah’s companionship with us has several conditions: Prayer, Zakat, faith, supporting the Prophets, and spending (in charity). «انّى معكم لئن اقمتم الصّلوة و...» (Indeed, I am with you if you establish the prayer and...)

3- Faith in the Prophets alone is not enough; supporting them is also necessary. «و عزّرتموهم» (and you support them)

4- Performing all obligations is effective, not just some of them. «لئن اقمتم الصلوة... و اقرضتم اللَّه» (If you establish the prayer... and lend to Allah)

5- Helping Allah’s creation is helping Allah. «أقرضتم اللّه» (you lend to Allah) instead of «اقرضتم النّاس» (you lend to people).

6- Lending must be done in a good manner. (From good wealth, with good intention, promptly, and without obligation/favor.) «قرضاً حسناً» (a good loan)

7- The placement of the topic of lending alongside matters such as prayer, Zakat, faith in the Messengers, and supporting the Prophets, and the resulting divine forgiveness and reward, indicates its extraordinary importance. (Lending includes all general forms of assistance, the clear and common example of which is giving loans to people.)

8- The leader of the community must be appointed by Allah, and the chosen representatives of the Prophets must also be by Allah’s command. «بعثنا منهم اثنى عشر نقیبا» (We raised among them twelve chiefs)

9- Prayer, Zakat, and spending (in charity) are meaningful alongside the acceptance and support of divine leaders. «اقمتم الصلوة و آتیتم الزّكاة وآمنتم برسلى و عزّرتموهم» (you establish the prayer and give the Zakat and believe in My Messengers and support them)

10- A leader is successful if he is chosen from among the people themselves. «بعثنا منهم» (We raised among them)

11- Paradise is given for a "price," not an excuse. If there is prayer, Zakat, faith, assistance, and spending (in charity), then Paradise is also there. «لئن اقمتم الصّلوة... لاُدخلنّكم جنّات» (If you establish the prayer... I will surely admit you to Gardens)

12- Paradise is not a place for the polluted; one must first be purified, and then enter Paradise. «لاكفّرنّ... لاُدخلنّكم» (I will surely remove... I will surely admit you)

13- The path to attaining Allah’s forgiveness is faith and righteous deeds. «لئن آمنتم... واقرضتم... لاكفّرنّ» (If you believe... and lend... I will surely remove)

14- After taking the covenant, completing the proof, and stating the conditions for receiving divine favors, there is no room for excuse or pretext for anyone. «فمن كفر بعد ذلك منكم فقد ضلّ سواء السبیل» (But whoever disbelieves after that among you has certainly strayed from the soundness of the way)

15- Whoever disbelieves in the divine commands has deviated from the path of moderation. «فمن كفر... فقد ضلّ سواء السبیل» (But whoever disbelieves... has certainly strayed from the soundness of the way). (Yes, establishing prayer, paying Zakat, believing in the Prophets, and fulfilling covenants is the middle path that the disobedient have lost.)

Ayah

فَبِمَا نَقْضِهِم مِّيثَـٰقَهُمْ لَعَنَّـٰهُمْ وَجَعَلْنَا قُلُوبَهُمْ قَـٰسِيَةً ۖ يُحَرِّفُونَ ٱلْكَلِمَ عَن مَّوَاضِعِهِۦ ۙ وَنَسُوا۟ حَظًّا مِّمَّا ذُكِّرُوا۟ بِهِۦ ۚ وَلَا تَزَالُ تَطَّلِعُ عَلَىٰ خَآئِنَةٍ مِّنْهُمْ إِلَّا قَلِيلًا مِّنْهُمْ ۖ فَٱعْفُ عَنْهُمْ وَٱصْفَحْ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ يُحِبُّ ٱلْمُحْسِنِينَ (۱۳)
Then, because of their breaking their covenant We cursed them and made their hearts hard: they pervert words from their meanings, and have forgotten a part of what they were reminded. You will not cease to learn of some of their treachery, excepting a few of them. Yet excuse them and forbear. Indeed Allah loves the virtuous. (13)

Key Points

In the first Ayah of this Surah, fulfilling covenants is mentioned, and in the previous Ayah, Allah's covenant with the Children of Israel is mentioned. This Ayah discusses the consequences of breaking promises and covenants. Nisyan (forgetfulness) sometimes means involuntary forgetfulness, which is not subject to criticism. However, sometimes it means neglect and inattention, which is subject to criticism, such as the case mentioned in this Ayah: «نسوا حظّاً» (They forgot a portion).

Messages

1- We should learn a lesson from the covenant-breaking of the Children of Israel and its consequences. «فبما نقضهم... لعنّاهم»

2- Covenant-breaking is a factor leading to deprivation of Divine grace and the ground for the emergence of hard-heartedness. «لعنّاهم... جعلنا قلوبهم قاسیة» (We cursed them... and made their hearts hard). In Ayah 77 of Surah At-Tawbah, covenant-breaking is also the cause of hypocrisy: «فاعقبهم نفاقاً فى قلوبهم» (So He caused hypocrisy to be in their hearts).

3- A group among the Children of Israel were constantly treacherous. «لاتزال تطّلع على خائنة منهم»

4- The Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله was constantly observing the actions and behavior of the Jews. «لاتزال تطّلع»

5- In dealing with the enemy, we must not forget fairness. («الاّ قلیلاً» (Except a few) means that not all of the Children of Israel were conspirators.)

6- Pardon and overlooking are instances of Ihsan (goodness/benevolence). «فاعف عنهم و اصفح... المحسنین»

7- Pardoning and overlooking even a disbeliever is Ihsan and goodness. «فاعف عنهم... انّ اللّه یحبّ المحسنین»

8- The doer of good is beloved by Allah. «انّ اللّه یحبّ المحسنین»

Ayah

وَمِنَ ٱلَّذِينَ قَالُوٓا۟ إِنَّا نَصَـٰرَىٰٓ أَخَذْنَا مِيثَـٰقَهُمْ فَنَسُوا۟ حَظًّا مِّمَّا ذُكِّرُوا۟ بِهِۦ فَأَغْرَيْنَا بَيْنَهُمُ ٱلْعَدَاوَةَ وَٱلْبَغْضَآءَ إِلَىٰ يَوْمِ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ ۚ وَسَوْفَ يُنَبِّئُهُمُ ٱللَّهُ بِمَا كَانُوا۟ يَصْنَعُونَ (۱٤)
Also from those who say, ‘We are Christians,’ We took their pledge; but they forgot a part of what they were reminded. So We stirred up enmity and hatred among them until the Day of Resurrection, and soon Allah will inform them concerning what they had been doing. (14)

Key Points

Nasara (نصارى) is the plural of Nasrani (نصرانى). Perhaps Christians were called Nasara because the slogan of the companions of Prophet Isa (Jesus) was "نحن انصار اللّه" (We are the helpers of Allah). [41] Baghda' (بَغضاء) is enmity in the heart, and 'Adawah (عداوت) is its outward manifestation. Forgetting a portion of the reminders means that the Christians crossed the boundary of Monotheism and reached the Trinity, and instead of accepting Prophet Muhammad صلى الله علیه وآله, they concealed His signs. Aghrayna (أغرینا) originally means to stick something. This means that enmity is the cause of their connection (or constant state).

41) As-Saff, 14.

Messages

1- Claims are numerous, but actions are few. "قالوا انّا نصارى... فنسوا حظّاً مما ذكّروا..."

2- Whoever makes a claim must accept responsibility. "قالوا انّا نصارى اخذنا میثاقهم"

3- The result of forgetting divine reminders and the glad tidings of the two Covenants (Old and New Testaments) is division and enmity. "فنسوا... اغرینا بینهم العداوة"

4- We should learn lessons from the bitter consequences of others' covenant-breaking. (We took a covenant from the Christians, and because they forgot, they became afflicted with misfortune.) "اخذنا میثاقهم... فنسوا... فاغرینا"

5- The Jews and Christians will remain until the Day of Resurrection and will not become extinct. "الى یوم القیامة"

6- Division and enmity are among the divine punishments. "نسوا... فأغرینا"

7- All deeds are under the supervision of Allah and will have reward and punishment. "ینبئهم اللّه بما كانوا یصنعون"

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَهْلَ ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ قَدْ جَآءَكُمْ رَسُولُنَا يُبَيِّنُ لَكُمْ كَثِيرًا مِّمَّا كُنتُمْ تُخْفُونَ مِنَ ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ وَيَعْفُوا۟ عَن كَثِيرٍ ۚ قَدْ جَآءَكُم مِّنَ ٱللَّهِ نُورٌ وَكِتَـٰبٌ مُّبِينٌ (۱٥)
O People of the Book! Certainly Our Apostle has come to you, clarifying for you much of what you used to hide of the Book, and excusing many [an offense of yours]. Certainly, there has come to you a light from Allah, and a manifest Book. (15)

Key Points

Stating the concealed matters is a sign of knowledge of the unseen and one of the ways to recognize the Prophet. «یبیّن لكم... تخفون من الكتاب» (42)

(42) Tafsir al-Mizan.

Messages

1- Islam is a universal religion and invites all religions to the truth. «یا أهل الكتاب قد جائكم رسولنا»

2- In guiding and inviting people, we should not despair, even concerning the People of the Book. «یا أهل الكتاب»

3- The world without the Quran is dark. «قد جاءكم من اللّه نور»

3- Some truths of the Torah and the Gospel have been concealed. «كثیراً ممّا كنتم تخفون»

4- One of the methods of guidance is stating matters only to the extent necessary. «ویعفو عن كثیر»

5- The Prophet and the Quran are highly magnificent. (The words «نور» (43) and «كتاب» are indefinite (nakerah), which is a sign of greatness and magnificence.) «نورٌ و كتابٌ مبین»

(43) Based on the interpretation that «نور» refers to the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله. Tafsir al-Mizan.

Ayah

يَهْدِى بِهِ ٱللَّهُ مَنِ ٱتَّبَعَ رِضْوَٰنَهُۥ سُبُلَ ٱلسَّلَـٰمِ وَيُخْرِجُهُم مِّنَ ٱلظُّلُمَـٰتِ إِلَى ٱلنُّورِ بِإِذْنِهِۦ وَيَهْدِيهِمْ إِلَىٰ صِرَٰطٍ مُّسْتَقِيمٍ (۱٦)
With it Allah guides those who follow [the course of] His pleasure to the ways of peace, and brings them out from darkness into light by His will, and guides them to a straight path. (16)

Key Points

Guidance has levels: One stage is general guidance for all human beings, whether they follow it or not, and the other type is specific to those who have accepted the general guidance. This Ayah describes the second type. Salām (Peace) is one of the Names of Allah, «السّلام المؤمن المهیمن...» (44), and one of the names of Paradise is also Dār al-Salām (The Abode of Peace) (45). Therefore, the paths of Salām (peace) mean the path of Allah and Paradise, reaching which passes through the «سُبل السلام» (Paths of Peace) and the Straight Path (Sirāt al-Mustaqīm). «سُبل السّلام» includes the paths to the well-being (salāmat) of the individual and society, the family and the progeny, the intellect, the soul, honor (nāmūs), and so forth. The clear example of the followers of Allah's pleasure in «مَن اتّبع رضوانه» (who follows His pleasure) are those who tread the path of "Ghadīr Khumm," because the Ayah «رضیت لكم الاسلام دیناً» (I have approved Islam for you as religion) was revealed after the appointment of Ali ibn Abi Talib علیهما السلام to the position of succession to the Holy Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله (46).

44) Hashr, 23. 45) An'am, 127. 46) Tafsir Atayeb al-Bayan.

Messages

1- Those who are worthy of guidance are those who seek to attain the pleasure of the Truth (Allah), not those pursuing status, wealth, carnal desires, or revenge. «یهدى به اللّه مَن اتّبع رضوانه»

2- Man Himself prepares the ground for his own guidance. «یهدى به اللّه من اتّبع رضوانه»

3- Guidance toward the paths of well-being (salāmat) and felicity is dependent on earning Allah's pleasure, and whoever seeks to please others besides Him is in deviation. «یهدى به اللّه من اتّبع رضوانه سبل السلام»

4- The path of Truth is one, but the paths of falsehood (polytheism, disbelief, hypocrisy, division, etc.) are numerous. (The word «نور» (light) is singular, but «ظلمات» (darknesses) is plural. Indeed, all goodness and purity attain a state of unity and oneness under the light of Monotheism, but darkness always causes division and scattering of ranks.)

5- The goal is one, but the paths to reach it are numerous. «سبل السلام»

6- The school of revelation guarantees the well-being and felicity of the individual and society, the soul and the body. «سبل السلام»

7- Under the shade of the Quran, humanity achieves coexistence and purity (safā) in life. «سبل السلام»

8- «سبل السلام» leads to the «صراط مستقیم» (Straight Path), and all those who seek to earn the pleasure of Allah by performing various duties under different circumstances reach the Straight Path. «یهدى... سبل السّلام... یهدیهم الى صراط مستقیم»

9- To reach the Straight Path, the Prophet and the Book alone are not enough; Allah's grace and will are also necessary. «باذنه»

10- Neither compulsion (jabr) nor delegation (tafwīḍ), but rather Amr bayn al-Amrayn (an affair between the two extremes). Man is the selector; «اتّبع رضوانه» (he follows His pleasure), but reaching the goal is impossible without the will of Allah. «باذنه»

11- The Quran is the healing remedy for all darknesses. It transforms the darknesses of ignorance, polytheism, division, savagery, doubts, lusts, superstitions, crimes, and anxieties into the light of knowledge, Monotheism, unity, civilization, certainty, truth, purity (safā), and tranquility. «یخرجهم من الظلمات الى النور»

Ayah

لَّقَدْ كَفَرَ ٱلَّذِينَ قَالُوٓا۟ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ هُوَ ٱلْمَسِيحُ ٱبْنُ مَرْيَمَ ۚ قُلْ فَمَن يَمْلِكُ مِنَ ٱللَّهِ شَيْـًٔا إِنْ أَرَادَ أَن يُهْلِكَ ٱلْمَسِيحَ ٱبْنَ مَرْيَمَ وَأُمَّهُۥ وَمَن فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ جَمِيعًا ۗ وَلِلَّهِ مُلْكُ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ وَمَا بَيْنَهُمَا ۚ يَخْلُقُ مَا يَشَآءُ ۚ وَٱللَّهُ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ قَدِيرٌ (۱۷)
They are certainly faithless who say, ‘Allah is the Messiah, son of Mary.’ Say, ‘Who can avail anything against Allah should He wish to destroy the Messiah, son of Mary, and his mother, and everyone upon the earth?’ To Allah belongs the kingdom of the heavens and the earth, and whatever is between them. He creates whatever He wishes, and Allah has power over all things. (17)

Key Points

Christians have several baseless claims about God that the Quran refers to: 1- Belief in the three gods (Trinity). «لا تقولوا ثلاثة ...» [47] 2- Belief that the Creator God is one of the three gods (God the Father). «قالوا ان اللّه ثالث ثلاثة...» [48] 3- Confining divinity exclusively to Christ, which this Ayah refutes.

«یخلق ما یشاء» (He creates what He wills) refers to the creation of Jesus without a father, and the creation of Adam without a father or mother. If Christ is God, then how was He killed (according to the Christians), and how did the cross become the symbol of His oppression? God should not be subjected to assassination!

47) Nisa, 171. 48) Ma'idah, 73.

Messages

1- Islam opposes disbelief (kufr), polytheism (shirk), and superstition, regardless of the school of thought they belong to. (Exaggeration/extremism (ghuluw) is also a type of disbelief.) «كفر الّذین قالوا انّ اللّه هو المسیح بن مریم»

2- The Noble Prophet (صلى الله علیه وآله) is commanded to engage in logical dialogue with opponents. «قل فمن یملك...»

3- If Christ were God, God would not reside in a woman's womb. «المسیح بن مریم»

4- The possibility of annihilation and destruction is incompatible with divinity. «ان اراد ان یهلك المسیح»

5- Jesus, his mother, and all people on Earth are equal in their humanity and their powerlessness before the might of Allah. «المسیح بن مریم و أمّه ومن فى الارض جمیعاً»

6- Claiming divinity for anyone other than Allah, such as Christ, indicates the Christians' lack of true knowledge regarding Allah. «قالوا انّ اللّه هو المسیح... وللّه ملك السموات والارض»

7- Divine power is not restricted to a specific system and can create a child even without a father. «و اللّه على كل شى ء قدیر»

Ayah

وَقَالَتِ ٱلْيَهُودُ وَٱلنَّصَـٰرَىٰ نَحْنُ أَبْنَـٰٓؤُا۟ ٱللَّهِ وَأَحِبَّـٰٓؤُهُۥ ۚ قُلْ فَلِمَ يُعَذِّبُكُم بِذُنُوبِكُم ۖ بَلْ أَنتُم بَشَرٌ مِّمَّنْ خَلَقَ ۚ يَغْفِرُ لِمَن يَشَآءُ وَيُعَذِّبُ مَن يَشَآءُ ۚ وَلِلَّهِ مُلْكُ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ وَمَا بَيْنَهُمَا ۖ وَإِلَيْهِ ٱلْمَصِيرُ (۱۸)
The Jews and the Christians say, ‘We are Allah’s children and His beloved ones.’ Say, ‘Then why does He punish you for your sins?’ No, you are humans from among His creatures. He forgives whomever He wishes, and punishes whomever He wishes, and to Allah belongs the kingdom of the heavens and the earth, and whatever is between them, and toward Him is the return. (18)

Key Points

When the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله invited a group of Jews to Islam, they said: We are the children and friends of God. [49] The Jews and Christians did not consider themselves the actual sons of God, but rather imagined a kind of ceremonial adoption for themselves. Killing the prophets, concealing the glad tidings regarding the advent of the Prophet of Islam, breaking covenants, distorting the heavenly books, calf-worship, fault-finding, and gluttony are examples of the crimes of the Children of Israel. And the uprooting of the mountain, forty years of wandering, transformation (or metamorphosis), and humiliation are examples of the Divine punishment against them. «یعذّبكم بذنوبكم»

49) The claim of being the son of God and the friend of God is also mentioned in the Gospel of John, Chapter 8, Verse 41. Tafsir Namuneh.

Messages

1- Racism, seeking privilege, and confining the truth exclusively to oneself, one's party, or one's group is forbidden. «بل أنتم بشر ممّن خلق»

2- No individual, tribe, race, or nation should be certain of God's forgiveness, just as they should not despair of His mercy. «یغفر لمن یشاء ویعذّب من یشاء»

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَهْلَ ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ قَدْ جَآءَكُمْ رَسُولُنَا يُبَيِّنُ لَكُمْ عَلَىٰ فَتْرَةٍ مِّنَ ٱلرُّسُلِ أَن تَقُولُوا۟ مَا جَآءَنَا مِنۢ بَشِيرٍ وَلَا نَذِيرٍ ۖ فَقَدْ جَآءَكُم بَشِيرٌ وَنَذِيرٌ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ قَدِيرٌ (۱۹)
O People of the Book! Certainly Our Apostle has come to you, clarifying [the Divine teachings] for you after a gap in [the appearance of] the apostles, lest you should say, ‘There did not come to us any bearer of good news nor any warner.’ Certainly, there has come to you a bearer of good news and a warner. And Allah has power over all things. (19)

Key Points

The fatrah (interruption) and temporal gap between Prophet Jesus (عليه السلام) and Prophet Muhammad (صلى الله عليه وآله) is approximately six hundred years. However, during periods when no prophet is commissioned, the earth is not devoid of Allah's Proof (Hujjah), because the successors (awsiya') of the prophets were present. In the words of Imam Ali (عليه السلام): The earth is never devoid of a Proof, whether He has power or not, because the path of Allah must not remain hidden from those who seek it. [50] Thus, the days of fatrah do not mean abandoning the people. Short-term or long-term fatrahs and gaps have wisdoms and are among the beneficial programs in the Divine educational system. Historical examples include the separation of Prophet Moses (عليه السلام) from his people, the seclusion (i'tikaf) of the prophets, the temporary cessation of revelation from the Prophet, and the Minor and Major Occultations (Ghaybah Sughra wa Kubra) of Imam Mahdi (عليه السلام).

50) Nahj al-Balaghah, Sayings 147.

Messages

1- Islam invites the People of the Book toward faith. «یا اهل الكتاب قد جائكم»

2- Clarifying truths that have been concealed, distorted, or forgotten is one of the missions of the prophets. «یبیّن لكم»

3- Divine laws require clarification by the prophets. «رسولنا یُبیّن لكم»

4- The commissioning (of a prophet) closes the door to excuses for humanity. «ان تقولوا ماجائنا من بشیر»

5- Giving glad tidings (bashir) and warning (nadhir) are among the methods of prophetic propagation. «جائكم بشیر ونذیر»

6- Man is free in choosing his path; the prophets only give glad tidings and warnings, and they do not employ compulsion or coercion. «فقد جائكم بشیر ونذیر»

7- It cannot be questioned how an unlettered prophet can clarify concealed knowledge and distortions that have occurred in religion, because Allah is Capable of everything. «واللّه على كل شى قدیر»

Ayah

وَإِذْ قَالَ مُوسَىٰ لِقَوْمِهِۦ يَـٰقَوْمِ ٱذْكُرُوا۟ نِعْمَةَ ٱللَّهِ عَلَيْكُمْ إِذْ جَعَلَ فِيكُمْ أَنۢبِيَآءَ وَجَعَلَكُم مُّلُوكًا وَءَاتَىٰكُم مَّا لَمْ يُؤْتِ أَحَدًا مِّنَ ٱلْعَـٰلَمِينَ (۲٠)
When Moses said to his people, ‘O my people, remember Allah’s blessing upon you when He appointed prophets among you, and made you kings, and gave you what none of the nations were given. (20)

Key Points

Remembering blessings is a factor leading to love for Allah, gratitude for His blessings, and devotion and submission to His commands. «اذكروا نعمة اللّه علكیم» The word malik (ملِك) linguistically means both ruler/governor and owner/possessor. A narration from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله states: Anyone among the Children of Israel who possessed a wife, a servant, and a mount was called a malik (king/possessor). [51] Furthermore, if mulūk (plural of malik) meant only monarchs/sultans, not all people could be kings. Therefore, malik means one who has authority/discretion. Blessings such as crossing the Nile River, the descent of manna and quails, and the gushing forth of twelve springs of water were special favors granted to the Children of Israel, which this verse refers to with the phrase «آتاكم ما لم یؤت احداً».

51) Tafsir al-Mizan.

Messages

1- To invite people, one must use the leverage of emotion/sympathy. «یاقوم»

2- Before issuing divine commands, we must prepare people by mentioning Allah's favors. «اذكروا نعمة اللَّه...» (In this verse, blessings are mentioned, and in the next verse, an important command is issued.)

3- Among the greatest blessings are the blessings of prophethood, governance, and power. «اذ جَعل فیكم انبیاء و جَعلكم ملوكاً...»

4- One of the duties of the prophets is reminding people of Allah's blessings. «اذكروا نعمة اللّه»

5- Even before Prophet Moses, there were prophets among the Children of Israel, and Moses reminds them of their existence as a blessing. «جَعل فیكم انبیاء»

6- The prophets were the facilitators of sovereignty and independence. (The phrase «جَعل فیكم انبیاء» precedes the phrase «جعلكم ملوكاً».)

7- We must learn lessons from history. The people of Moses, after benefiting from Allah's special favor and attaining governance, became afflicted with humiliation and wretchedness. «لم یؤت احداً من العالمین» turned into «باؤا بغضب من اللّه» [52].

52) Al-Baqarah, 61.

Ayah

يَـٰقَوْمِ ٱدْخُلُوا۟ ٱلْأَرْضَ ٱلْمُقَدَّسَةَ ٱلَّتِى كَتَبَ ٱللَّهُ لَكُمْ وَلَا تَرْتَدُّوا۟ عَلَىٰٓ أَدْبَارِكُمْ فَتَنقَلِبُوا۟ خَـٰسِرِينَ (۲۱)
O my people, enter the Holy Land which Allah has ordained for you, and do not turn your backs, or you will become losers.’ (21)

Key Points

The "Holy Land" («ارض مقدّس») is either the entire region of Sham (Syria, Jordan, Palestine, and so on), or it is only Bayt al-Maqdis (Jerusalem). The intent of «لاترتدّوا» (do not turn back) is either not turning away from the Divine command, or not retreating from the path of advance.

Messages

1- The relationship between the leader and the people must be deep and emotional. «یا قوم»

2- Holy lands must be wrested from the grasp of the unworthy. «ادخلوا الارض المقدّسة»

3- Not all places on earth are the same; some places possess sanctity. «الارض المقدّسة»

4- The prohibition against retreating from battle is a sign of the rebellious spirit of a group among the Children of Israel. «لاترتدّوا على ادباركم»

5- Fleeing the battlefield is forbidden (Haram). «ادخلوا... ولاترتدّوا»

6- Enjoying Divine blessings entails responsibility. «جَعل فیكم انبیاء وجَعلكم ملوكاً... ادخلوا»

7- Among the manifestations and examples of loss (khusran) is deprivation from holy places. «ادخلوا... ولاترتدّوا... فتنقلبوا خاسرین»

Ayah

قَالُوا۟ يَـٰمُوسَىٰٓ إِنَّ فِيهَا قَوْمًا جَبَّارِينَ وَإِنَّا لَن نَّدْخُلَهَا حَتَّىٰ يَخْرُجُوا۟ مِنْهَا فَإِن يَخْرُجُوا۟ مِنْهَا فَإِنَّا دَٰخِلُونَ (۲۲)
They said, ‘O Moses, there are a tyrannical people in it. We will not enter it until they leave it. But once they leave it, we will go in.’ (22)

Key Points

The term Jabbār (جبّار) derives from Jabr (جَبْر), meaning the rectification or compensation of something accompanied by force and pressure. However, it is also used solely for each of these two meanings: 1. Compensation (or making amends) 2. Force and dominance. And regarding Allah, it is used in both meanings. [53] The Qawm Jabbār (tyrannical people) were the Amalekites, of Semitic descent, who lived in the north of Arabia in the Sinai desert, attacked Egypt, and ruled for five hundred years. [54]

53) Tafsir Namūneh. 54) Dā’irat al-Ma‘ārif, Farīd Wajdī.

Messages

1- The presence of the unworthy in a place is not a reason for qualified individuals to withdraw. «يا قوم ادخلوا... فإن يخرجوا منها فإنّا داخلون»

2- The enemy must be expelled; one should not wait for them to leave on their own. «فإن يخرجوا منها فإنّا داخلون»

3- Seeking comfort is forbidden; one must take action and seek aid to drive out the enemy. (The Quran criticizes the Children of Israel who said: «إنّا لن ندخلها»)

4- Expecting victory without struggle is incorrect thinking. «فإن يخرجوا منها فإنّا داخلون»

5- Worse than disobedience is audacity and obstinacy. «لن ندخلها» (Considering that the particle lan [never] implies audacity.)

Ayah

قَالَ رَجُلَانِ مِنَ ٱلَّذِينَ يَخَافُونَ أَنْعَمَ ٱللَّهُ عَلَيْهِمَا ٱدْخُلُوا۟ عَلَيْهِمُ ٱلْبَابَ فَإِذَا دَخَلْتُمُوهُ فَإِنَّكُمْ غَـٰلِبُونَ ۚ وَعَلَى ٱللَّهِ فَتَوَكَّلُوٓا۟ إِن كُنتُم مُّؤْمِنِينَ (۲۳)
Said two men from among those who were Godfearing and whom Allah had blessed: ‘Go at them by the gate! For once, you have entered it, you will be victors. Put your trust in Allah, should you be faithful.’ (23)

Key Points
