It is mentioned in the commentaries (Tafasir) that those two individuals were among the twelve chieftains (Nuqaba') of the Children of Israel, named: "Yusha' ibn Nun" and "Kalib ibn Yufanna," and their names are mentioned in the Torah, the Book of Deuteronomy. [55] Imam Baqir عليه السلام said: These two individuals were the cousins of Moses عليه السلام. [56] According to Allamah Tabataba'i in Tafsir al-Mizan: Whenever the word ni'mah (blessing/favor) appears in the Qur'an without qualification, the intended meaning is the blessing of Wilayah (guardianship) and Nubuwwah (prophethood).

55) Tafsir Namuneh. 56) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- The fear of God (Khawf) is the basis for receiving divine favors and blessings. «یخافون أنعم اللّه...»

2- Whoever fears Allah feels responsibility, considers obedience to the command of the Prophet of Allah obligatory, and does not surrender to other powers. «یخافون انعم اللّه... ادخلوا»

3- The fear of God is the basis for courage, daring, and action. «یخافون... ادخلوا»

4- The fear of God is a divine blessing. «یخافون انعم اللّه علیهما»

5- During an attack, fighters must be given morale. «فانّكم غالبون»

6- Divine fear (Khawf) and piety (Taqwa) grant a person insight and the power for correct and clear analysis of issues. «یخافون ...فانّكم غالبون»

7- Reliance (Tawakkul) must be sincere and solely upon Allah. «على اللّه فتوكّلوا»

8- If the people of faith enter with bravery, the deviants will abandon the field. «فاذا دخلتموه فانّكم غالبون»

9- The basis of success is not merely material resources, but faith, reliance (Tawakkul), determination, and daring are also necessary. «ادخلوا... و على اللَّه فتوكّلوا»

10- Reliance (Tawakkul) alone is not sufficient; rather, it is accompanied by effort that brings us to the goal. «ادخلوا... فتوكّلوا»

11- Reliance (Tawakkul) is a sign of faith and the key to salvation from dead ends and fears. «فتوكّلوا ان كنتم...»

12- Movement is from you; blessing and victory (Nasr) are from Allah. «فاذا دخلتموه فانّكم غالبون و على اللَّه فتوكّلوا ان كنتم مؤمنین»

13- Reliance (Tawakkul) is not merely verbal, but a spirit arising from faith. «فتوكّلوا ان كنتم مؤمنین»

Ayah

قَالُوا۟ يَـٰمُوسَىٰٓ إِنَّا لَن نَّدْخُلَهَآ أَبَدًا مَّا دَامُوا۟ فِيهَا ۖ فَٱذْهَبْ أَنتَ وَرَبُّكَ فَقَـٰتِلَآ إِنَّا هَـٰهُنَا قَـٰعِدُونَ (۲٤)
They said, ‘O Moses, we will never enter it so long as they remain in it. Go ahead, you and your Lord, and fight! We will be sitting right here.’ (24)

Key Points

Mecca and Bayt al-Maqdis (Jerusalem) are both sacred lands. But when Moses (عليه السلام) commanded his people to enter it and fight the enemy, they made excuses and disobeyed his command. However, when the Muslims came near Mecca in the sixth year of the Hijra with the intention of performing Umrah, accompanying the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله), they would have attacked the city if the Prophet had not prevented them. During this journey, the "Treaty of Hudaybiyyah" took place. Yes, both nations reached the gates of two sacred cities, but one was so averse to fighting, and the other was so warlike. The weakness of the Children of Israel in fighting the enemy was so famous that the Muslims, too, before the start of the Battle of Badr (in the second year of the Hijra) and upon entering Mecca (in the sixth year), used to say: We are not like the Children of Israel who say, "إنّا ههنا قاعدون" (Indeed, we will remain seated right here); we are always ready to fight alongside you. The efforts of divine leaders in fulfilling the duties entrusted to them only bear fruit if the people cooperate.

Messages

1- The Children of Israel were an example of disrespect, excuse-making, weakness, and luxury-seeking. "يا موسى إنّا لن ندخلها أبدا..." (O Moses, indeed we will never enter it...)

In the word "لن ندخلها" (we will never enter it), their audacity in the face of Allah's command is evident.

In the word "أبداً" (never), persistence in audacity is seen.

In the word "اذهب" (go), insult towards Prophet Moses is observed.

In the word "ربّك" (your Lord), there is an insult to the essence of the Lord, indicating the weakness of their faith.

"قاعدون" (seated/remaining) signifies their desire for comfort, not their pursuit of honor.

2- People must undertake the reform of society themselves, rather than expecting reform only from Allah and religious leaders. "فقاتلا إنّا ههنا قاعدون"

3- Wishing for victory without effort is not wise. "فقاتلا إنّا ههنا قاعدون"

Ayah

قَالَ رَبِّ إِنِّى لَآ أَمْلِكُ إِلَّا نَفْسِى وَأَخِى ۖ فَٱفْرُقْ بَيْنَنَا وَبَيْنَ ٱلْقَوْمِ ٱلْفَـٰسِقِينَ (۲٥)
He said, ‘My Lord! I have no power over [anyone] except myself and my brother, so part us from the transgressing lot.’ (25)

Key Points

Prophet Moses (عليه السلام), after despairing of the movement of the Children of Israel, complained to Allah. The request for separation was so that the fire of Divine wrath would not also engulf the companions of Moses, and only the enemies would be punished, or that He was requesting that a separation occur between them and the enemies through the death of the disbelievers (kuffār). Regarding why Prophet Moses only mentioned himself and his brother, and did not mention the two pious men who agreed with Moses and invited the people to enter the city, the exegetes (mufassirūn) have offered several explanations, including: * Moses was not certain of the steadfastness of those two men either. [57] * Since those two men had been threatened with stoning by the people, Moses said: O God! I have no power, even those two men are in danger. [58]

57) Tafsir al-Maraghi. 58) Tafsir Atayeb al-Bayan.

Messages

1- Most Quranic supplications seek aid through the Name of the Lord (Rabb). «قال ربّ...»

2- When all people have abandoned us, we must resort to the true Savior. «فاذهب انت و ربّك... قال ربّ...»

3- The complaint and curse of the Prophets to Allah occur when they despair of the people's faith and obedience. «ربّ انّى...»

4- Refusing to participate in Jihad (struggle) is transgression (fisq). «فافرُق بیننا وبین القوم الفاسقین»

5- One of the social calamities is the people distancing themselves from the friends of Allah (Awliyā' Allāh) and being deprived of the grace of their presence. «فافرُق بیننا وبین القوم الفاسقین»

6- One of the afflictions and hardships for believers is living with and among the transgressors (fāsiqīn). «فافرُق بیننا وبین القوم الفاسقین»

Ayah

قَالَ فَإِنَّهَا مُحَرَّمَةٌ عَلَيْهِمْ ۛ أَرْبَعِينَ سَنَةً ۛ يَتِيهُونَ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ ۚ فَلَا تَأْسَ عَلَى ٱلْقَوْمِ ٱلْفَـٰسِقِينَ (۲٦)
He said, ‘It shall be forbidden them for forty years: they shall wander about in the earth. So do not grieve for the transgressing lot.’ (26)

Key Points

The word «یَتیهون» (yatihūn) is derived from the root «تیه» (tīh), meaning bewilderment or wandering. The Sinai desert is also called the Tīh (the place of wandering) of that people, where they spent forty years and were deprived of the blessings of the land. The story of the disobedience of the Children of Israel, the Divine wrath, and their wandering in the Tīh is mentioned in the fourth chapter of the Book of Numbers in the Torah. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: Allah forbade the Children of Israel and their children from entering Bayt al-Maqdis (Jerusalem) due to their disobedience to the command of Prophet Moses. Eventually, the children of their children succeeded in entering. (Meaning, as long as the fathers and their children who were forbidden were alive, entry into the Holy Land was not achieved.) (59) We read in narrations that a story similar to that of the Children of Israel will also occur for Muslims. (60) The number arba'īn (forty) in «أربعین سنة» (forty years) holds a secret, which is used both in Divine wrath and Divine grace. Prophet Moses stayed on Mount Tur for forty nights to receive the Torah, and the duration of the Jewish people's wandering was forty years. Sometimes, the reform of the new generation requires the extinction of the old generation. «أربعین سنة یتیهون فى الارض» (forty years wandering in the land) implies that those defeated by the Pharaonic system who did not appreciate Moses must pass away, and a new generation must grow up in a free environment, facing the hardships of desert life, so that they may appreciate life in the city under the guidance of heavenly leaders. It is mentioned in a hadith that they died in the land of the Tīh, and their children entered the Holy Land.

59) Tafsir Ayyashi. 60) Bihar, Vol. 28, pp. 14 and 30.

Messages

1- The punishment for fleeing from battle is deprivation. «فانّها محرّمة علیهم»

2- Wandering is a type of punishment for the wicked. «یتیهون فى الارض»

3- Those who hold back (qā'idīn) and those who flee from the front must be deprived of certain facilities and privileges. «فانّها محرّمة علیهم»

4- Prophets are compassionate toward the people. «فلا تأس»

5- Do not grieve for the wicked. «فلا تأس» (Punishing the offender, like bitter medicine, is beneficial for the health of the individual and society.)

Ayah

۞ وَٱتْلُ عَلَيْهِمْ نَبَأَ ٱبْنَىْ ءَادَمَ بِٱلْحَقِّ إِذْ قَرَّبَا قُرْبَانًا فَتُقُبِّلَ مِنْ أَحَدِهِمَا وَلَمْ يُتَقَبَّلْ مِنَ ٱلْـَٔاخَرِ قَالَ لَأَقْتُلَنَّكَ ۖ قَالَ إِنَّمَا يَتَقَبَّلُ ٱللَّهُ مِنَ ٱلْمُتَّقِينَ (۲۷)
Relate to them truly the account of Adam’s two sons. When the two of them offered an offering, it was accepted from one of them and not accepted from the other. [One of them] said, ‘Surely I will kill you.’ [The other one] said, ‘Allah accepts only from the Godwary. (27)

Key Points

Perhaps the meaning of "reciting it truthfully" is that this incident was distorted and mixed with superstitions in the Torah, and what has come in the Quran is the truth. It is mentioned in narrations that Habil (Abel) was a shepherd and brought his best sheep for the sacrifice. And Qabil (Cain) was a farmer and chose the worst part of his harvest for spending in the way of Allah. [61] The Quran states: You will never attain righteousness unless you spend from that which you love. «لن تنالوا البرّ حتّى تنفقوا ممّا تحبّون» [62] Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: Qabil's motive for the murder was his jealousy regarding the acceptance of Habil's sacrifice. [63]

61) Kafi, Vol. 8, p. 113. 62) Al Imran, 92. 63) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- The history of past generations must be read and examined for the purpose of taking lessons. «واتل»

2- In relating history, important matters must be addressed. («نبأ» means important news)

3- We must separate history from myth. «بالحقّ» (The incident of Habil and Qabil's sacrifice is not a baseless myth or story.)

4- The principle is drawing near to Allah, not the sacrifice itself, whatever the sacrifice may be. «اذ قرّبا قرباناً» («قرباناً» is indefinite)

5- Being the son of a prophet plays no role in the rejection or acceptance of deeds; thus, it was accepted from one and not accepted from the other. «فتقبّل من احدهما ولم یُتقبّل من الاخر»

6- The history of murder and bloodshed is simultaneous with the history of humanity. «نبأ ابنى آدم... لاقتلنّك»

7- Sometimes jealousy progresses even to fratricide (killing one's brother). «لاقتلنّك»

8- Even the one who threatens murder must be answered logically. «انمّا یتقبّل اللّه»

9- Motives and characteristics are effective in the acceptance or non-acceptance of deeds. «انّما یتقبّل اللّه من المتّقین»

10- The criterion for the acceptance of deeds is piety (taqwa), not the personality of individuals nor the type of work. «انّما یتقبّل اللّه من المتّقین»

11- The difference between acceptance and non-acceptance is based on piety, not discrimination. «من المتّقین»

Ayah

لَئِنۢ بَسَطتَ إِلَىَّ يَدَكَ لِتَقْتُلَنِى مَآ أَنَا۠ بِبَاسِطٍ يَدِىَ إِلَيْكَ لِأَقْتُلَكَ ۖ إِنِّىٓ أَخَافُ ٱللَّهَ رَبَّ ٱلْعَـٰلَمِينَ (۲۸)
Even if you extend your hand toward me to kill me, I will not extend my hand toward you to kill you. Indeed, I fear Allah, the Lord of all the worlds. (28)

Key Points

Habil said: I do not intend to kill you, but this does not mean I will not defend myself, because surrendering to a killer is not compatible with piety (taqwa). «ما أنا بباسط یدى...»

Messages

1- When dealing with the envious person, speak calmly and extinguish the fire of envy with gentleness in speech. «ما أنا بباسط...»

2- One of the ways of enjoining good and forbidding evil (Nahy 'anil-Munkar) is to assure him that you will not transgress against him or attack him. «ما أنا بباسط یدى...»

3- What is valuable is refraining from killing based on the fear of Allah, not due to weakness or feebleness. «انّى أخاف اللّه»

4- Piety (taqwa) and the fear of God are deterrent factors against sin and transgression even in the most critical situations. «انّى أخاف اللّه»

5- The fear of Allah is one of the effects of believing in His comprehensive Lordship (Rubūbiyyah). «انّى أخاف اللّه ربّ العالمین»

6- The universe (or world) is a manifestation of Divine Lordship (Rubūbiyyah). «ربّ العالمین»

Ayahs

إِنِّىٓ أُرِيدُ أَن تَبُوٓأَ بِإِثْمِى وَإِثْمِكَ فَتَكُونَ مِنْ أَصْحَـٰبِ ٱلنَّارِ ۚ وَذَٰلِكَ جَزَٰٓؤُا۟ ٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ (۲۹)
I desire that you earn [the burden of] my sin and your sin, to become one of the inmates of the Fire, and such is the requital of the wrongdoers.’ (29)
فَطَوَّعَتْ لَهُۥ نَفْسُهُۥ قَتْلَ أَخِيهِ فَقَتَلَهُۥ فَأَصْبَحَ مِنَ ٱلْخَـٰسِرِينَ (۳٠)
So his soul prompted him to kill his brother, and he killed him, and thus became one of the losers. (30)

Key Points

Imam Baqir عليه السلام, following the recitation of the Ayah, «فطوّعت له نفسه» (So his soul prompted him), said: Iblis said to Qabil (Cain): Your sacrifice was not accepted, but the sacrifice of your brother Habil (Abel) was accepted. If he remains alive, throughout history his descendants will boast over your descendants that the sacrifice of our father was accepted while the sacrifice of your father was not accepted. Therefore, it is better that you kill him so that your descendants do not suffer humiliation. (64) Imam Baqir عليه السلام said: «من قتل مؤمناً متعمّداً أثبت اللّه على قاتله جمیع الذنوب وبرء المقتول منها، وذلك قوله تعالى: «انّى ارید أن تبوأ...»» Whoever intentionally kills a believer, Allah records all sins against the killer and purifies the victim of them. (65) The meaning of the Ayah is not silence in the face of the oppressor in the hope of forgiveness of sins. In a Hadith from the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله, we read that the location of the incident was Mount Qasioun in Damascus. (66) Imam Sajjad عليه السلام said: Iblis taught Qabil the method of killing. (67)

64) Kafi, Vol. 8, p. 113. 65) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 66) Tafasir Durr al-Manthur. 67) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- The sins of the victim are transferred to the killer. «تبوء باثمى»

2- In conflicts, the initiator is responsible for the loss. «اثمى واثمك»

3- Fratricide leads directly to becoming an inhabitant of the Fire. «فتكون من اصحاب النّار»

4- Belief in the Resurrection (Ma'ad) was among the primary beliefs of the first humans on Earth. «اصحاب النّار»

5- The killer oppresses himself, the victim, the victim's family, and society. «الظالمین»

6- The human soul, through temptation, suggestion, and embellishment, makes a person compliant and naive, leading him to sin. «فطوّعت له نفسه»

7- Vain desires (hawa al-nafs) can suppress even fraternal affections. «فطوّعت له نفسه قتل اخیه»

8- The pure human nature (fitrah) detests murder, but the soul makes this act appear good and compels the person to kill. «فطوّعت»

9- The struggle between truth and falsehood has existed since the beginning of human history. «فقتله»

10- Human death on Earth began with martyrdom. «فقتله»

11- Following vain desires is a loss. «فقتله فأصبح من الخاسرین»

12- The killer suffers internally from pangs of conscience, externally from public criticism, and is subject to retribution (Qisas) and justice, thus gaining no satisfaction. «فقتله فأصبح من الخاسرین»

13- Dominance and victory over an opponent are not always signs of honor. «فقتله فأصبح من الخاسرین»

Ayah

فَبَعَثَ ٱللَّهُ غُرَابًا يَبْحَثُ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ لِيُرِيَهُۥ كَيْفَ يُوَٰرِى سَوْءَةَ أَخِيهِ ۚ قَالَ يَـٰوَيْلَتَىٰٓ أَعَجَزْتُ أَنْ أَكُونَ مِثْلَ هَـٰذَا ٱلْغُرَابِ فَأُوَٰرِىَ سَوْءَةَ أَخِى ۖ فَأَصْبَحَ مِنَ ٱلنَّـٰدِمِينَ (۳۱)
Then Allah sent a crow, exploring in the ground, to show him how to bury the corpse of his brother. He said, ‘Woe to me! Am I unable to be [even] like this crow and bury my brother’s corpse?’ Thus he became regretful. (31)

Key Points

When Cain (Qabil), who had committed the murder of his brother, saw that predators were approaching the body, he carried it on his shoulder, but found no benefit and remained bewildered. However, as soon as he saw the action of the raven burying another dead raven, he learned how to bury his brother's body. Ghurāb (غُراب) is a bird similar to a crow, having a red beak and feet. [68] Early humans were very inexperienced, but Allah constantly poured His grace upon humanity through various means. Imam Sajjad عليه السلام said: Allah sent two ravens to fight each other, and one killed the other. Then the killer dug a hole with its beak and buried the dead one. [69] Imam Sajjad عليه السلام said: "و الذّنوب التّى تورث النّدم قتل النّفْس" (And the sins that cause remorse are the killing of a soul). Among the sins that cause regret is the killing of an innocent person. [70]

68) Qamus Quran. 69) Tafasir Burhan and Rahnamā. 70) Bihar, Vol. 73, p. 375.

Messages

1- Sometimes animals are divine agents, and their movement is by His command and in the path that He wills. «فبعث اللّه غُراباً»

2- Much of human knowledge is inspired by the lives of animals. «فبعث اللّه غُراباً یبحث... لیریه»

3- Humans must always seek learning. The principle is learning and acquisition of knowledge, through any means, even if it is through animals. «فبعث اللّه غراباً... لیریه»

4- Allah sometimes teaches humans through small animals to show that even a raven can be a means of instruction for humanity; therefore, one should not become arrogant. «غراباً... لیریه»

5- The dead body must be buried in the earth (placing it in a container, mummifying it, burning it, etc., is not correct). «فى الارض... یوارى سوأة اخیه»

6- The human deceased also possesses dignity and should not become food for animals; rather, it must be buried. «یوارى سوأة اخیه»

7- Remorse is a sign of the innate nature (fitrah) seeking truth. «فأصبح من النادمین»

Ayah

مِنْ أَجْلِ ذَٰلِكَ كَتَبْنَا عَلَىٰ بَنِىٓ إِسْرَٰٓءِيلَ أَنَّهُۥ مَن قَتَلَ نَفْسًۢا بِغَيْرِ نَفْسٍ أَوْ فَسَادٍ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ فَكَأَنَّمَا قَتَلَ ٱلنَّاسَ جَمِيعًا وَمَنْ أَحْيَاهَا فَكَأَنَّمَآ أَحْيَا ٱلنَّاسَ جَمِيعًا ۚ وَلَقَدْ جَآءَتْهُمْ رُسُلُنَا بِٱلْبَيِّنَـٰتِ ثُمَّ إِنَّ كَثِيرًا مِّنْهُم بَعْدَ ذَٰلِكَ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ لَمُسْرِفُونَ (۳۲)
That is why We decreed for the Children of Israel that whoever kills a soul, without [its being guilty of] manslaughter or corruption on the earth, is as though he had killed all mankind, and whoever saves a life is as though he had saved all mankind. Our apostles certainly brought them manifest signs, yet even after that, many of them commit excesses on the earth. (32)

Key Points

In this Ayah, the killing of one person is presented as equivalent to the killing of all people. To explain this reality, several interpretations and meanings can be offered: A: The killing of one person carries a punishment similar to the killing of all people. B: The sanctity of killing one person in the sight of Allah is equivalent to the killing of all people in your sight. C: The killing of one person is a disregard for the status of humanity. E: The killing of one person removes security from all people. F: Since human beings are like the members of one body, the killing of one is the killing of all. G: The hellish abode of the killer of one person is the abode of one who is the killer of all. (71) H: The killing of one person paves the way for the killing of all. I: One human being can be the source of an entire generation, so killing him is equivalent to killing a generation. (It seems this possibility is better.)

We read in narrations: The annihilation of the entire world is easier for Allah than the killing of one believer. (72)

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: Whoever quenches the thirst of a thirsty person in a place where water is not found is like one who has revived a soul. (73)

According to the Ayahs and narrations, guiding and directing people to the path of truth is a type of revival, and misleading people is a type of killing. Surah Al-Anfal, Ayah 24, calls the Prophet’s invitation a source of life for people: «دعاكم لما یحییكم».

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام also said: «مَن اخرجها من ضلال الى هدى فكانّما أحیاها ومن اخرجها من هدى الى الضلال فقد قتلها» (Whoever brings a soul out of error into guidance has revived it, and whoever brings it out of guidance into error has killed it). (74)

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: «نجاها من غرق او حرق او سَبُع او عدوّ» (Saving it from drowning, or burning, or a beast, or an enemy). The meaning of reviving a soul is saving those afflicted by drowning, fire, a wild animal, or an enemy. (75)

And in another narration, it is stated: Whoever does not feed a hungry person is equivalent to killing him, and feeding him is equivalent to reviving him. (76)

Imam Baqir عليه السلام said: The transgressors (musrifūn) are those who consider forbidden things lawful and shed blood. (77)

71) Kafi, Vol. 7, p. 271. 72) Bihar, Vol. 10, p. 382. I am writing this hadith on the night of the last Friday of Ramadan 1379, the Friday of which is Quds Day, while the world's televisions are showing heartbreaking scenes of the killing of defenseless Palestinian Muslims by the Zionists. 73) Makarim al-Akhlaq, p. 135. 74) Kafi, Vol. 2, p. 210. 75) Tafsir Burhan. 76) Kafi, Vol. 2, p. 204. 77) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- Sometimes historical events are the cause for the issuance of Divine commands. «من أجل ذلك»

2- Human beings and their destinies are interconnected throughout history. «من أجل ذلك كتبنا على بنى اسرائیل»

3- Divine laws possess wisdom and are not arbitrary. «من اجل ذلك»

4- Punishment and retribution are necessary to prevent hard-heartedness and avoid the repetition of the incident. «من اجل ذلك كتبنا»

5- The life of every human being, regardless of their race or region, is sacred. «نفساً»

6- The execution of the corrupt (mufsid) was also present in the law of the Children of Israel. «كتبنا على بنى اسرائیل انّه من قتل نفساً بغیر نفس او فساد فى الارض»

7- Suicide and abortion are examples of «قتل نفس» (killing a soul) and are forbidden (haram). «من قتل نفساً...»

8- The life of one who causes corruption on earth or does not value the lives of people and kills them is worthless and must be eliminated. «بغیر نفس او فساد»

9- Killing a human being is permissible in two cases: A: As retribution (qisas) for the killer. «بغیر نفس» B: For eliminating the corrupt (mufsid). «او فساد»

10- The value of an action relates to its motive and goal. Killing one person with the intent of transgression is killing an entire society; «فكانّما قتل النّاس جمیعاً» but killing as retribution (qisas) is the life of the society. «ولكم فى القصاص حیوة» (78)

11- Transgression against the rights of one individual is a threat to the security of the community. «فكانّما قتل النّاس جمیعاً» Those whose work involves saving human lives, such as doctors, nurses, firefighters, aid workers, pharmacists, etc., must recognize their own worth and the value of their work. «فكانّما قتل النّاس جمیعاً»

12- The sign of a living society is providing aid to the afflicted and saving lives. «من أحیاها فكأنّما أحیى النّاس جمیعاً»

13- The lack of faith and action by people regarding the words of the Messengers has existed throughout history. «و لقد جائتهم رسلنا بالبیّنات ثمّ انّ كثیراً منهم بعد ذلك فى الارض لمسرفون»

14- Man is free-willed; even after the coming of the Prophets, he can choose the wrong path. «و لقد جائتهم رسلنا بالبیّنات... بعد ذلك فى الارض لمسرفون»

78) Baqarah, 179.

Ayah

إِنَّمَا جَزَٰٓؤُا۟ ٱلَّذِينَ يُحَارِبُونَ ٱللَّهَ وَرَسُولَهُۥ وَيَسْعَوْنَ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ فَسَادًا أَن يُقَتَّلُوٓا۟ أَوْ يُصَلَّبُوٓا۟ أَوْ تُقَطَّعَ أَيْدِيهِمْ وَأَرْجُلُهُم مِّنْ خِلَـٰفٍ أَوْ يُنفَوْا۟ مِنَ ٱلْأَرْضِ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ لَهُمْ خِزْىٌ فِى ٱلدُّنْيَا ۖ وَلَهُمْ فِى ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ عَذَابٌ عَظِيمٌ (۳۳)
Indeed the requital of those who wage war against Allah and His Apostle, and try to cause corruption on the earth, is that they shall be slain or crucified, or shall have their hands and feet cut off from opposite sides, or be banished from the land. That is a disgrace for them in this world, and in the Hereafter, there is a great punishment for them, (33)

Key Points

It is mentioned in the occasion of revelation (Sha'n Nuzul) of the Ayah that a group of polytheists from Mecca came to Medina and became Muslim. However, because they were ill, by the command of the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله, they went to an area outside Medina with pleasant weather and were permitted to benefit from the milk of the Zakat camels. But when they recovered, they seized the Muslim shepherds, cut off their hands and feet, blinded their eyes, plundered the camels, and abandoned Islam. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله ordered them to be arrested, and the same punishment they had inflicted upon the shepherds was carried out upon them. The above Ayah was then revealed. The punishments mentioned in the Ayah are considered "Haqq Allah" (the Right of Allah) and cannot be pardoned or altered by the government or the people. [79] And it is stated in Tafsir Al-Mizan that the selection of one of the four punishments rests with the Imam of the Muslims, and even if the guardians of the victim pardon the offender, one of the punishments must still be carried out. The word jazaa' (جزاء) means sufficient punishment and reward; meaning the four prescribed limits (Hudud) and penalties are just and sufficient for the criminal. [80] For the reform of society, both admonition and guidance (maw'izah wa irshad) are necessary, as well as the sword and decisive, revolutionary confrontation. (The previous Ayah contained a message and warning to the murderer, while this Ayah describes the punishment for those who wage war (muharib) and cause corruption (mufsid)). Punishment must be accompanied by justice. Since the types of corruption and the characteristics of the corrupt individuals differ, the punishments are not uniform. For example, if the corruption was heinous, the punishment is execution, but if it was superficial, the punishment is exile. This meaning is understood from narrations, including that: the punishment for murder is execution; the punishment for intimidation is exile; the punishment for highway robbery and theft is the cutting off of the hand and foot; and the punishment for murder, highway robbery, and armed theft is the cutting off of the hand and foot and crucifixion. [81] The implementation of divine laws and limits (Hudud) is possible only under the establishment of an Islamic system and government; therefore, religion is not separate from politics. Those who revolt against the Imam and leader of the Muslims or the Islamic government fall under the category of "یحاربون اللّه" (they wage war against Allah). [82] According to Ayah 279 of Surah Al-Baqarah, the usurer is one who "wages war" (muharib) against Allah and the Prophet, "فأذنوا بحرب من اللّه و رسوله" (then take notice of war from Allah and His Messenger), because he disrupts economic security. Likewise, in narrations, insulting a Muslim is considered waging war against Allah: «من أهان لى ولیاً فقد بارزنى بالمحاربة» (Whoever insults a friend of Mine has openly challenged Me to war). [83]

79) Tafsir Atayeb Al-Bayan. 80) Tafsir Rahnamā. 81) Tafsir Safi. 82) Tafsir Fi Zilal al-Qur'an. 83) Bihar, Vol. 4, p. 65.

Messages

  1. The duty of the system and government is to maintain the security of cities, villages, and roads, and to deal with offenders. «انّما جزاء...»

  2. War against the creation of Allah is war against Allah, and whoever confronts the people is as if he is confronting Allah. «یحاربون اللّه»

  3. Disobedience to the commands of the Prophet and fighting against them is war against Allah. «یحاربون اللّه و رسوله»

  4. Several types of punishment are prescribed for those who disrupt the security of society: execution, exile, cutting off the hand and foot, and crucifixion. «یقتّلوا او یصلّبوا...»

  5. Severe punishment is for the persistent oppressors, not those who commit offenses rarely. «یحاربون, یسعون» (The present tense verb indicates the continuity of the action).

  6. Opponents of the authority (wilayah) of the Messenger of Allah who intend to overthrow the system and are at war with the divine order must be punished with the severest penalty. «یحاربون اللَّه و رسوله... ان یقتّلوا»

Ayah

إِلَّا ٱلَّذِينَ تَابُوا۟ مِن قَبْلِ أَن تَقْدِرُوا۟ عَلَيْهِمْ ۖ فَٱعْلَمُوٓا۟ أَنَّ ٱللَّهَ غَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ (۳٤)
excepting those who repent before you capture them, and know that Allah is all-forgiving, all-merciful. (34)

Key Points

Since, in narrations (Ahadith), a person who threatens Muslims and draws a weapon against them—even if no killing occurs—is also called a Mufsid fil-Ardh (corruptor on earth), this Ayah addresses their repentance (Tawbah). Therefore, the repentance of the Muharib (one who wages war against Allah and His Messenger) and the Mufsid only removes the punishment for threat and intimidation, not the punishment for murder and theft. That is, repentance is effective regarding Haqq Allah (the right of Allah), but not Haqq an-Nas (the right of the people), because the rights of the people depend on the satisfaction of the right-holders. The designation of Muharib has one ruling, and the designation of murderer (Qatil) and thief (Sariq) has another. [84] Meaning, if the Muharib committed murder and repented, the ruling of Qisas (retaliation) remains, but if he only engaged in threat and intimidation and repented, the punishment for being a Muharib is lifted from him. Divine punishments have an educational and reformative aspect for the individual and society, not vengeance. Hence, the sinner must repent, and his repentance is effective. To lift the penalty, the verification of genuine repentance is necessary. For example, his repentance must be proven through a change evident in the morals, behavior, and speech of the criminal, or by the testimony of just individuals, and similar means.

84) Tafsir-e Nemuneh.

Messages

1- The path of repentance is open to everyone. «یحاربون اللَّه و رسوله... الا الّذین تابوا»

2- Repentance is effective only if it occurs before being arrested and apprehended. «من قبل ان تقدروا علیهم» (In other sins, too, repentance is beneficial until before death. [85])

3- We must believe in Allah's forgiveness. «فاعلموا انّ اللَّه غفور رحیم»

4- Allah's forgiveness is accompanied by mercy, not reproach and humiliation. «غفور رحیم»

85) Nisa, 18.

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ ٱتَّقُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ وَٱبْتَغُوٓا۟ إِلَيْهِ ٱلْوَسِيلَةَ وَجَـٰهِدُوا۟ فِى سَبِيلِهِۦ لَعَلَّكُمْ تُفْلِحُونَ (۳٥)
O you who have faith! Be wary of Allah, and seek the means of recourse to Him, and wage jihad in His way, so that you may be felicitous. (35)

Key Points

Imam Ali عليه السلام says: The best means by which one can draw near to Allah is faith in Allah and His Prophet, and striving (Jihad) in His way, and the word of sincerity (Kalimat al-Ikhlas), and establishing prayer (Salat), and paying Zakat, and fasting the month of Ramadan, and Hajj and Umrah, and maintaining kinship ties (Silat al-Rahim), and spending (in charity) secretly and openly, and good deeds. [86] The Ahl al-Bayt علیهم السلام are the firm rope and the means of drawing near to Allah. «هم العروة الوثقى والوسیلة الى اللّه» [87] Tawassul (seeking intercession/means) is a subject about which narrations have been cited in many books of the Ahl al-Sunnah, such as Sawa'iq by Ibn Hajar, Sunan by Bayhaqi, Sahih by Darimi, and Wafa al-Wafa. Furthermore, Ayahs 64 of An-Nisa, 97 of Yusuf, and 114 of At-Tawbah can also serve as evidence for Tawassul.

86) Nahj al-Balaghah, Sermon 110. 87) Tafsir Safi.

Messages

1- Faith (آمنوا), piety (اتّقوا اللَّه), seeking a means (وابتغوا الیه الوسیلة), and striving (وجاهدوا فى سبیله) are the path to happiness and salvation. (لعّلكم تفلحون)

2- To achieve salvation (Falah), one must both abandon sins (اتّقوا اللَّه) and perform acts of obedience (وابتغوا الیه الوسیلة).

3- All good deeds are the foundation and means for happiness. (Provided, of course, that we do not destroy those foundations through our sins.) (لعلّكم تفلحون)

Ayahs

إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ لَوْ أَنَّ لَهُم مَّا فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ جَمِيعًا وَمِثْلَهُۥ مَعَهُۥ لِيَفْتَدُوا۟ بِهِۦ مِنْ عَذَابِ يَوْمِ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ مَا تُقُبِّلَ مِنْهُمْ ۖ وَلَهُمْ عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌ (۳٦)
Indeed if the faithless possessed all that is on the earth, and as much of it besides, to redeem themselves with it from the punishment of the Day of Resurrection, it shall not be accepted from them, and there is a painful punishment for them. (36)
يُرِيدُونَ أَن يَخْرُجُوا۟ مِنَ ٱلنَّارِ وَمَا هُم بِخَـٰرِجِينَ مِنْهَا ۖ وَلَهُمْ عَذَابٌ مُّقِيمٌ (۳۷)
They would long to leave the Fire, but they shall never leave it, and there is a lasting punishment for them. (37)

Key Points

All paths to salvation are closed on the Day of Judgment. Disbelievers (Kafiroon) will not benefit from Divine Mercy, because it is exclusive to the people of piety (Taqwa). «رحمتى وسعت كلّ شى ء فساكتبها للّذین یتّقون» [88] Nor will they attain intercession (Shafa'ah), because intercession is exclusive to those with whom Allah is pleased. «لا تنفع الشفاعة الاّ من اذن له الرّحمن و رضى له قولاً» [89] Furthermore, there is no news of death, because they will remain perpetually in the Fire, and their request for death will not be accepted. «نادوا یا مالك لیقض علینا ربّك قال انّكم ماكثون» [90]

88) A'raf, 156. 89) Taha, 109. 90) Zukhruf, 77.

Messages

  1. The effectiveness of wealth is only in this world; in the Hereafter, riches are fruitless. «ما تُقبّل...»

  2. On the Day of Judgment, in the system of Divine Justice, ransom (fidyah) will not be accepted. «لیفتدوا... ما تُقبّل»

  3. The main factor for felicity (Sa'adah) lies within the human being (faith, piety, and struggle/Jihad), not externally (wealth and riches). «لهم ما فى الارض جمیعاً... ما تقبّل منهم»

  4. The punishment of the disbelievers (Kafiroon) will neither be averted by ransom nor terminated by the passage of time. «و ما هم بخارجین»

  5. Whoever does not emerge from the darkness of polytheism (Shirk) and ignorance in this world, despite all the proofs and guidance, will not emerge from Hell in the Hereafter either. «لهم عذاب مقیم»

Ayah

وَٱلسَّارِقُ وَٱلسَّارِقَةُ فَٱقْطَعُوٓا۟ أَيْدِيَهُمَا جَزَآءًۢ بِمَا كَسَبَا نَكَـٰلًا مِّنَ ٱللَّهِ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ عَزِيزٌ حَكِيمٌ (۳۸)
As for the thief, man or woman, cut off their hands as a requital for what they have earned. [That is] an exemplary punishment from Allah, and Allah is all-mighty, all-wise. (38)

Key Points

The word "nakāl" (نكال) means a deterrent matter. Islamic penal laws are for deterrence, not revenge. Nakāl refers to a punishment that serves as a lesson for others. [91]

In this Ayah, the male thief is mentioned first, then the female thief, but in the second Ayah of Surah An-Noor, which states the ruling on fornication/adultery, the female fornicator/adulterer is mentioned first, then the male fornicator/adulterer. Perhaps this is because in theft, the role of the male offender is greater, while in fornication/adultery, the role of the female offender is greater.

The late Sayyid Murtada Alam al-Huda (one of the scholars from a thousand years ago) was asked: Why is a hand, whose blood money (diyah) is five hundred mithqals of gold, cut off for stealing only one-fourth of a mithqal? He replied: "Trust" elevates the price of the hand, and "treachery" reduces its value.

According to narrations, the extent of the hand to be cut is four fingers, and the thumb and the palm must remain. The value of the property for which the thief's hand is cut must also be at least the price of one-fourth of a dinar (one-fourth of a mithqal of gold). The property must also be in a protected place, not in places like a caravanserai, bathhouse, mosque, or public areas. The thief must also be aware of the law of hand cutting, otherwise, his hand is not cut. Furthermore, if someone steals food items out of necessity during years of famine, his hand is not cut. Of course, in all cases where the hand is not cut, other punishments are applied, the details of which are found in books of jurisprudence. [92]

The Prophet of Islam صلى الله علیه وآله considered the worst type of theft to be stealing from prayer and performing the rukūʿ (bowing) and sujūd (prostration) incompletely. [93]

The implementation of these rulings requires government, power, system, and organization. Thus, Islam is a religion of governance and politics.

Islam, before cutting the hand, emphasizes the importance and necessity of work and managing the lives of the poor through the public treasury (Bayt al-Māl), close relatives, interest-free loans (Qard al-Hasanah), cooperation, and so on. However, despite this, poverty is not an excuse or justification for theft. [94]

The world's penal system, because it relies only on imprisonment and financial fines, is incapable of combating theft. Cutting the hand serves as a permanent warning for the criminal and prevents him and others from repeating the transgression. «فاقطعوا ایدیهما»

91) Qāmūs Qur’ān. 92) Tafsīr Sāfī. 93) Tafsīr Qurṭubī. 94) Tafsīr Fī Ẓilāl al-Qur’ān.

Messages

1- The punishment for the male and female thief is the same. «السارق و السارقة»

2- Social security must be maintained with decisiveness. «فاقطعوا»

3- Severe penalty and punishment are deterrents against theft. «نكالا»

4- In Islamic penal laws, besides punishing the offender, the lesson for others is also considered. «نكالاً»

5- In implementing divine limits (Hudūd), one must not be influenced by emotions. «فاقطعوا»

6- Although the cutting of the hand is carried out by the judge and ruler, Allah addresses all believers, «فاقطعوا ایدیهما», so that they may facilitate the implementation of divine limits.

7- Private ownership and social security are so important that the thief's hand must be cut off for their sake. «فاقطعوا»

8- The exercise of power must be calculated/wise. «عزیز حكیم»

9- The command to cut off the thief's hand is a manifestation of Allah's Might and Power. «فاقطعوا... عزیز حكیم»

Ayah

فَمَن تَابَ مِنۢ بَعْدِ ظُلْمِهِۦ وَأَصْلَحَ فَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ يَتُوبُ عَلَيْهِ ۗ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ غَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ (۳۹)
But whoever repents after his wrongdoing, and reforms, then Allah shall accept his repentance. Indeed Allah is all-forgiving, all-merciful. (39)

Key Points

In Islam, punishment exists alongside guidance, education, and mercy. In the previous Ayah, the penalty for the thief was stated; here, there is an invitation to repentance before the Forgiving God and the rectification of misdeeds, which causes Allah to restore His favor upon the servant. If the thief repents before being apprehended and returns the property, he is forgiven both in this world and the Hereafter, [95] but after being apprehended, the hadd (prescribed penalty) is executed, and the role of repentance is only for the Day of Judgment. It is narrated in a hadith: A thief whose hand had been severed asked the Holy Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله: Is the path of repentance open for me? He replied: Yes! Today (when the hadd was executed upon you), you are like the very day you were born from your mother. [96]

95) Kafi, Vol. 7, p. 250. 96) Tafsir al-Mizan.

Messages

1- For the erring person, the path of return and rectification is always open. «فمن تاب»

2- Repentance is not merely an internal regret; rather, it must be accompanied by compensating for past corruption. «واصلح»

3- If a person repents, Allah also accepts his repentance. «یتوب علیه»

4- Theft is oppression against oneself and society, and an infringement upon societal security. «بعد ظلمه»

5- Criminals must be invited to the path of Allah and given hope. «فانّ اللّه یتوب، غفور، رحیم»

Ayah

أَلَمْ تَعْلَمْ أَنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَهُۥ مُلْكُ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ يُعَذِّبُ مَن يَشَآءُ وَيَغْفِرُ لِمَن يَشَآءُ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ قَدِيرٌ (٤٠)
Do you not know that to Allah belongs the kingdom of the heavens and the earth? He punishes whomever He wishes, and forgives whomever He wishes, and Allah has power over all things. (40)

Messages

1- The One who rules over existence has the right to legislate the laws of existence. «السارق و السارقة... له ملك السموات...»

2- Criminals should know that they have no escape route and must return to Allah. «الم تعلم انّ اللّه له ملك السموات والارض»

3- Allah has no need for the repentance of His servants, because all of existence belongs to Him. «له ملك السماوات والارض»

4- Man must be in a state between fear and hope. «یعذّب من یشاء و یغفر لمن یشاء»

5- Forgiveness or punishment, both are examples of His power. «یعذّب...یغفر... على كلّ شى قدیر»

Ayah

۞ يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلرَّسُولُ لَا يَحْزُنكَ ٱلَّذِينَ يُسَـٰرِعُونَ فِى ٱلْكُفْرِ مِنَ ٱلَّذِينَ قَالُوٓا۟ ءَامَنَّا بِأَفْوَٰهِهِمْ وَلَمْ تُؤْمِن قُلُوبُهُمْ ۛ وَمِنَ ٱلَّذِينَ هَادُوا۟ ۛ سَمَّـٰعُونَ لِلْكَذِبِ سَمَّـٰعُونَ لِقَوْمٍ ءَاخَرِينَ لَمْ يَأْتُوكَ ۖ يُحَرِّفُونَ ٱلْكَلِمَ مِنۢ بَعْدِ مَوَاضِعِهِۦ ۖ يَقُولُونَ إِنْ أُوتِيتُمْ هَـٰذَا فَخُذُوهُ وَإِن لَّمْ تُؤْتَوْهُ فَٱحْذَرُوا۟ ۚ وَمَن يُرِدِ ٱللَّهُ فِتْنَتَهُۥ فَلَن تَمْلِكَ لَهُۥ مِنَ ٱللَّهِ شَيْـًٔا ۚ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ ٱلَّذِينَ لَمْ يُرِدِ ٱللَّهُ أَن يُطَهِّرَ قُلُوبَهُمْ ۚ لَهُمْ فِى ٱلدُّنْيَا خِزْىٌ ۖ وَلَهُمْ فِى ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ عَذَابٌ عَظِيمٌ (٤۱)
O Apostle! Do not grieve for those who are active in [promoting] unfaith, such as those who say, ‘We believe’ with their mouths, but whose hearts have no faith, and the Jews who eavesdrop with the aim of [telling] lies [against you] and eavesdrop for other people who do not come to you. They pervert words from their meanings, [and] say, ‘If you are given this, take it, but if you are not given this, beware!’ Yet whomever Allah wishes to mislead, you cannot avail him anything against Allah. They are the ones whose hearts Allah did not desire to purify. For them is disgrace in this world, and there is a great punishment for them in the Hereafter. (41)

Key Points

The Ayah indicates that the Jewish scholars sent a group of Jews and hypocrites to the Prophet of Islam, while they themselves avoided confrontation («لم یأتوك»). Their objective was that the instructions and rulings of Islam might conform to what they had distorted from the Torah. They advised (the delegation): if the Prophet of Islam's statement matched ours, accept it; otherwise, reject it. The hypocrites and the dispatched delegation came to the Prophet and listened attentively so they could attribute lies and rumors to His words. («سمّاعون للكذب»).

Messages

  1. Prophets are compassionate towards the misguided. «یا ایّها الرّسول لایحزنك»
  2. Disbelief (Kufr) is a field where a group advances rapidly, and their disbelief increases every moment. «یسارعون فى الكفر»
  3. Enemies hasten in disbelief and hypocrisy; why should Muslims be sluggish on the path of truth? «یسارعون فى الكفر»
  4. Hypocrisy leads to an increase in disbelief. «یسارعون فى الكفر من الّذین... بافواههم»
  5. Faith (Iman) is based on heartfelt acceptance, not verbal declaration. «آمنّا بأفواههم و لم تؤمن قلوبهم»
  6. Faith is valuable only when it is firm in the heart. «و لم تؤمن قلوبهم»
  7. The heart is the center of faith. «لم تؤمن قلوبهم»
  8. Hypocrites and Jews pursue a single goal together. «من الّذین قالوا آمنّا بافواههم... و من الّذین هادوا»
  9. Listening is not important; the motive behind it is important. «سمّاعون للكذب»
  10. Disbelievers have infiltrators and spies among the Muslims; religious preachers should not assume all listeners have good intentions. «سمّاعون لقوم آخرین»
  11. Distortion (Tahrif) is the cultural betrayal of the Jews. «یحرّفون الكلم»
  12. Reporting without piety is one of the dangerous professions. «سمّاعون للكذب... یحرّفون...»
  13. Every heavenly word has a specific place. «من بعد مواضعه »
  14. We must submit to the commands of Allah, rather than expecting divine rulings to conform to our desires. «فخذوه... فأحذروا» (97)
  15. Sin deprives a person of the worthiness for guidance. «و مَن یرد اللّه فتنته»
  16. Hypocrites are deprived of the Prophet's intercession. «فلن تملك له من اللَّه شیئاً»
  17. Even the Messenger of Allah has no role for the stubborn, obstinate, and pleasure-seeking individuals. «فلن تملك»
  18. Divine wrath follows human misconduct. «سمّاعون للكذب... لم یرد اللّه ان یطهّر قلوبهم»
  19. The stubborn and obstinate heart is deprived of receiving divine favors. «لم یرد اللَّه ان یطهّر قلوبهم»
  20. The punishment of this world is nothing compared to the punishment of the Hereafter. «فى الدّنیا خزى و لهم فى الاخرة عذاب عظیم»
  21. Hypocrites face both worldly disgrace and the great torment of the Resurrection awaits them. «لهم فى الاخرة عذاب عظیم»

97) Some people seek out a Marja' Taqlid (source of emulation), a scholar, a court, or a law that rules according to their desires. The Ayah prohibits this.

Ayah

سَمَّـٰعُونَ لِلْكَذِبِ أَكَّـٰلُونَ لِلسُّحْتِ ۚ فَإِن جَآءُوكَ فَٱحْكُم بَيْنَهُمْ أَوْ أَعْرِضْ عَنْهُمْ ۖ وَإِن تُعْرِضْ عَنْهُمْ فَلَن يَضُرُّوكَ شَيْـًٔا ۖ وَإِنْ حَكَمْتَ فَٱحْكُم بَيْنَهُم بِٱلْقِسْطِ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ يُحِبُّ ٱلْمُقْسِطِينَ (٤۲)
Eavesdroppers with the aim of [telling] lies, eaters of the unlawful—if they come to you, judge between them, or disregard them. If you disregard them, they will not harm you in any way. But if you judge, judge between them with justice. Indeed Allah loves the just. (42)

Key Points

Some Jews who had committed zina al-muhsana (adultery by a married person) came to the Prophet for judgment, hoping to escape the punishment of stoning, which is also mentioned in the Jewish religion [98]. They were unaware that the ruling of Islam is also stoning the adulterer. When they saw that the ruling of Islam was the same, they refused to accept the Prophet's judgment.

According to narrations, suht (سُحت) refers to bribes and gifts given to accomplish a task [99]. Linguistically, suht is an unlawful act that brings shame upon its perpetrator [100].

The phrase «سَمّاعون للكذِب» (sammā‘ūna lil-kadhib - listeners to falsehood) either means that they listen carefully to statements with the intention of fabricating lies and distorting them, or it means that the common people among the Jews listen to and accept the words of their scholars, even though they know their scholars are liars and distorters.

The Ayah addresses the Prophet, saying: Although the Jewish scholars are intent on harming you, and you are concerned for the Islamic school of thought and society, Allah gives the good news that if you deem it expedient to turn away, you should have no concern. «وان تعرض عنهم فلن یضرّوك شیئاً»

98) The ruling on adultery is stated in the Torah, Deuteronomy, Chapter 23. 99) Wasā’il, Vol. 17, p. 95. 100) Mufradāt Rāghib.

Messages

1- The Jewish scholars were bribe-takers, «أكّالون للسُّحت» (devourers of suht), and their common people were eager listeners to falsehood. «سمّاعون للكذب» (The repetition of sammā‘ūna in two consecutive verses may indicate that this method has gradually become their characteristic.)

2- The coexistence of Muslims with the People of the Book was such that they would come to the Prophet of Islam for judgment. «جاءوك»

3- The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله had the responsibility of governance in addition to the responsibility of prophethood. «فاحكم بینهم أو أعرض عنهم»

4- Prophets, in matters of governance and judgment, have authority from Allah to act according to their discretion. «فاحكم ... أو أعرض»

5- The scope of Islamic governance includes the People of the Book. «فاحكم بینهم بالقسط»

6- If an Islamic ruler is chosen to judge between non-Islamic countries, he must fully observe justice, courage, and clarity. «فاحكم بینهم بالقسط»

7- Racial issues, regional matters, group biases, personal desires, and threats must not influence judgments. «فاحكم بینهم بالقسط»

8- Justice, always and with every group, is a value pleasing to Allah. «فاحكم بینهم بالقسط انّ اللّه یحبّ المقسطین»

Ayah

وَكَيْفَ يُحَكِّمُونَكَ وَعِندَهُمُ ٱلتَّوْرَىٰةُ فِيهَا حُكْمُ ٱللَّهِ ثُمَّ يَتَوَلَّوْنَ مِنۢ بَعْدِ ذَٰلِكَ ۚ وَمَآ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ بِٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ (٤۳)
And how should they make you a judge, while with them is the Torah, in which is Allah’s judgement? Yet in spite of that, they turn their backs [on Him] and they are not believers. (43)

Key Points

As We mentioned in the previous Ayah, the reason the Jews referred to the Prophet of Islam was to find a way to mitigate the punishment. Although they had access to the Torah, which answered their needs, they were looking for a simpler law.

Messages

1- Not all of the Torah has been corrupted. «عندهم التوراة فیها حكم اللّه»

2- If only the People of the Book were committed to the laws of their own scripture. «و عندهم التوراة فیها حكم اللَّه»

3- What is important for some groups is the mitigation of the law, not faith in the law and fulfilling their duty. (This is why, despite having the law of the Torah, they came to the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله to find an easier path to punishment). «ثمّ یتولّون بعد ذلك»

4- The sign of faith is submission to the law. «و ما اولئك بالمؤمنین»

Ayah

إِنَّآ أَنزَلْنَا ٱلتَّوْرَىٰةَ فِيهَا هُدًى وَنُورٌ ۚ يَحْكُمُ بِهَا ٱلنَّبِيُّونَ ٱلَّذِينَ أَسْلَمُوا۟ لِلَّذِينَ هَادُوا۟ وَٱلرَّبَّـٰنِيُّونَ وَٱلْأَحْبَارُ بِمَا ٱسْتُحْفِظُوا۟ مِن كِتَـٰبِ ٱللَّهِ وَكَانُوا۟ عَلَيْهِ شُهَدَآءَ ۚ فَلَا تَخْشَوُا۟ ٱلنَّاسَ وَٱخْشَوْنِ وَلَا تَشْتَرُوا۟ بِـَٔايَـٰتِى ثَمَنًا قَلِيلًا ۚ وَمَن لَّمْ يَحْكُم بِمَآ أَنزَلَ ٱللَّهُ فَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ هُمُ ٱلْكَـٰفِرُونَ (٤٤)
We sent down the Torah containing guidance and light. The prophets, who had submitted, judged by it for the Jews, and so did the rabbis and the scribes, as they were charged to preserve the Book of Allah and were witnesses to it. So do not fear the people, but fear Me, and do not sell My signs for a paltry gain. Those who do not judge by what Allah has sent down—it is they who are the faithless. (44)

Key Points

Rabbānī (ربّانى) derives from Rabbān (ربّان), meaning educator or trainer. According to some, a Rabbānī is one who is connected to the Lord of the Worlds (Rabb al-Ālamīn), has become godly through knowledge and action, and is responsible for educating the people. It is narrated from Hadrat Ali عليه السلام that He said: "أنَا ربّانىّ هذه الاُمّة" (I am the Rabbānī of this nation). [101] Imam Sadiq عليه السلام also said: The Rabbāniyyūn are the Household of the Prophet (Ahl al-Bayt) علیهم السلام. [102]

Hibr (حِبْر) means a good effect or trace. Since scholars are the source of good effects in society, they are called Hibr and Ahbār. Ibn Abbas was also given the title Hibr al-Ummah (Scholar of the Nation).

The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said: Anyone who issues an unjust judgment, even if it is for the equivalent of two dirhams, falls under the scope of the last phrase of this Ayah: "هم الكافرون" (they are the disbelievers). [103]

101) Tafsir Maraghi. 102) Tafsir Safi. 103) Tafsir Safi; Kafi, Vol. 7, p. 408.

Messages

1- While acknowledging the distortion of the existing Torah, the original heavenly Books must be honored. "فیها هُدىً و نورٌ" (in it is guidance and light).

2- Although the Torah was revealed to Moses عليه السلام, all the prophets and scholars who came after him were commanded to judge according to it. "یحكم بها النبیّون" (The prophets judge by it).

3- Laws based on revelation must be accepted. (The necessity for prophets and scholars to judge implies acceptance by the people.) "یحكم بها النبیّون..."

4- Prophets do not issue judgments based on their own accord; rather, they are submissive to Allah's command. "أسلموا" (who submitted).

5- Islam (submission to Allah) is the religion of all monotheists; the prophets of the Children of Israel are also described by the phrase "أسلموا" (who submitted). "النبیّون الّذین اسلموا" (The prophets who submitted).

6- The scholars of every nation are responsible for implementing divine laws among the people. (The Guardianship of the Jurist (Wilāyat al-Faqīh) has roots in all religions.) "یحكم بها النبیّون... والربّانیّون والاحبار" (The prophets... and the masters and the rabbis judge by it).

7- Hierarchy must be observed. First the prophets, then the Imams, and then the scholars. "النبیّون... والربّانیّون والاحبار"

8- The Jews had multiple prophets. "یحكم بها النبیّون..."

9- The Torah contains judicial and governmental laws and was the axis of judgment. "یحكم بها النبیّون..."

10- Light and guidance flow in society only when judgment and governance are based on the heavenly Book. "فیها هدىً و نور یحكم بها" (in it is guidance and light, by which they judge).

11- The way to preserve the heavenly Books is to act, judge, and govern according to them. "یحكم بها... بما استحفظوا" (they judge by it... because they were entrusted with its preservation). (Assuming that istihfāz refers to its comprehensive preservation.)

12- Scholars must be the protectors of the doctrine. "بما استحفظوا من كتاب اللَّه" (because they were entrusted with the preservation of the Book of Allah).

13- Judgment is the responsibility of the scholars. "یحكم بها... الربّانیون و الاحبار بما استحفظوا من كتاب اللَّه" (The masters and the rabbis judge by it... because they were entrusted with the preservation of the Book of Allah).

14- Scholars and judges bear two great responsibilities: A: They must not alter Allah's command out of fear of the people. "فلا تخشوا النّاس" (So fear not men). B: They must not fall into concealment or error out of greed for worldly wealth. "و لاتشتروا بایاتى ثمناً قلیلاً" (and do not sell My signs for a small price).

15- Deviation in judgment is disbelief (kufr). "ومن لم یحكم بماانزل اللَّه فاولئك هم الكافرون" (And whoever does not judge by what Allah has revealed—it is they who are the disbelievers).

16- In judgment, clarity, courage, and decisiveness are necessary, and one must not be influenced by threats or atmosphere creation. "فلاتخشوا النّاس" (So fear not men).

17- The fear of Allah is the guarantee of just judgment. "یحكم... فلاتخشوا النّاس واخشون" (They judge... So fear not men, but fear Me).

18- Distortion, silence, concealment, and judging by other than Allah's command—even if it results in gaining the entire world—is a loss. "ولاتشتروا... ثمناً قلیلا... فاولئك هم الكافرون" (And do not sell... for a small price... it is they who are the disbelievers). (Because the entire world is a "small enjoyment" (matā' qalīl).) [104]

19- When possessing divine law, resorting to the laws of the East and the West is disbelief, and such laws have no validity. "و من لم یحكم بما أنزل اللّه فاولئك هم الكافرون" (And whoever does not judge by what Allah has revealed—it is they who are the disbelievers).

20- Whoever has the conditions available to judge justly but isolates himself has committed disbelief. "و من لم یحكم بما انزل اللَّه فاولئك هم الكافرون" (And whoever does not judge by what Allah has revealed—it is they who are the disbelievers).

21- Silence and concealment before tyrants (Tāghūts), selling religion for the sake of capitalists, and deceiving the masses are among the dangers that threaten scholars. "فلاتخشوا النّاس... و لاتشتروا..." (So fear not men... and do not sell...).

22- Cowardly and greedy scholars are weak in religiosity and in protecting the heavenly Book. "فلا تخشوا النّاس... و لاتشتروا بایاتى ثمناً قلیلاً" (So fear not men... and do not sell My signs for a small price).

23- Income resulting from unjust judgment is illicit income. "لاتشتروا بایاتى..." (Do not sell My signs...).

24- Fear and greed cause deviation in judgment, and deviation in judgment causes disbelief. "فلاتخشوهم... ولاتشتروا....فاولئك هم الكافرون" (So fear them not... and do not sell....it is they who are the disbelievers).

104) انّ الذین یفترون على اللَّه الكذب لایفلحون. متاع قلیل ولهم عذاب الیم». Nahl, 116 to 117.

Ayah

وَكَتَبْنَا عَلَيْهِمْ فِيهَآ أَنَّ ٱلنَّفْسَ بِٱلنَّفْسِ وَٱلْعَيْنَ بِٱلْعَيْنِ وَٱلْأَنفَ بِٱلْأَنفِ وَٱلْأُذُنَ بِٱلْأُذُنِ وَٱلسِّنَّ بِٱلسِّنِّ وَٱلْجُرُوحَ قِصَاصٌ ۚ فَمَن تَصَدَّقَ بِهِۦ فَهُوَ كَفَّارَةٌ لَّهُۥ ۚ وَمَن لَّمْ يَحْكُم بِمَآ أَنزَلَ ٱللَّهُ فَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ هُمُ ٱلظَّـٰلِمُونَ (٤٥)
In it We prescribed for them: a life for a life, an eye for an eye, a nose for a nose, and an ear for an ear, a tooth for a tooth, and retaliation for wounds. Yet whoever remits it out of charity, that shall be an atonement for him. Those who do not judge by what Allah has sent down—it is they who are the wrongdoers. (45)

Key Points

In the previous Ayah, it was stated that the Torah is guidance and light. In this Ayah, the law of retribution (Qisas) is introduced, and perhaps this indicates that the law of Qisas in society is a source of light and guidance. Just as we read in another Ayah: «ولكم فى القصاص حیوة» (105) For you, there is life in retribution (Qisas). Mentioning the eye and the ear in the law of Qisas is by way of example; otherwise, harm to any limb incurs retribution. (106) Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said regarding this Ayah: Allah forgives the sins of a person to the extent that retribution (Qisas) is pardoned. (107) In the Torah, there is no Diyah (blood money); the offender is either subject to Qisas or pardoned. However, in Islam, Diyah is introduced as a third option. (108)

105) Al-Baqarah, 179. 106) Tafsir Atayeb al-Bayan. 107) Kafi, Vol. 7, p. 358. 108) Tafsir Qurtubi.

Messages

1- All human beings, regardless of race, tribe, poverty, or wealth, are equal before the law, and no one's blood is more valuable than another's. (109) «النّفس بالنفس»

2- Retribution (Qisas) existed in previous religions as well. (110) «كتبنا علیهم فیها» (Islam and other religions share similar laws.)

3- Sadaqah (charity) is not only financial expenditure; pardoning and forgiving the offender is also a type of Sadaqah. «فمن تصدّق به»

4- Our pardon of the offender leads to Divine forgiveness. «فهو كفّارة له» (Perhaps the intention is that our pardon serves as an expiation for the offender's sin, so that he is not afflicted by punishment on the Day of Judgment because of our forgiveness.) (111)

5- In Islam, ethical issues are presented alongside penal matters. «وكتبنا علیهم فیها... قصاص فمن تصدّق به»

6- Financial penalties and imprisonment alone cannot be a sufficient deterrent for the offender. «النّفس بالنّفس»

7- If the command of Allah is not implemented, injustice will engulf society. «و من لم یحكم بما انزل اللّه فأولئك هم الظالمون»

109) Of course, wise differences between men and women regarding the ruling of Qisas have been explained in jurisprudential books.

110) Torah, Exodus, Chapter 21, 23, and 26.

111) Tafsir Al-Mizan.

Ayah

وَقَفَّيْنَا عَلَىٰٓ ءَاثَـٰرِهِم بِعِيسَى ٱبْنِ مَرْيَمَ مُصَدِّقًا لِّمَا بَيْنَ يَدَيْهِ مِنَ ٱلتَّوْرَىٰةِ ۖ وَءَاتَيْنَـٰهُ ٱلْإِنجِيلَ فِيهِ هُدًى وَنُورٌ وَمُصَدِّقًا لِّمَا بَيْنَ يَدَيْهِ مِنَ ٱلتَّوْرَىٰةِ وَهُدًى وَمَوْعِظَةً لِّلْمُتَّقِينَ (٤٦)
We followed them with Jesus son of Mary, to confirm that which was before him of the Torah, and We gave him the Evangel containing guidance and light, confirming what was before it of the Torah, and as guidance and advice for the Godwary. (46)

Key Points

Both the Quran is a guide for the pious, "هُدىً للمتّقین" [112], and the Gospel is a source of guidance and admonition for the people of piety. "هُدىً و موعظة للمتّقین" In this Ayah, the word "هُدىً" (guidance) appears twice: once referring to guidance for the general public, and once referring to benefiting from guidance, which is specific to the pious (al-Muttaqin). The word "قفّینا" (We sent following) is derived from taqfiyah, meaning to bring something following something else. The meaning of "مصدّقاً" (confirming) might be that the personal characteristics of Prophet Jesus (عليه السلام) correspond with the signs mentioned about him in the Torah; thus, Prophet Jesus Himself and the signs confirm one another. In the Gospel of Matthew (Chapter 5, Verse 17), it is stated: Do not think that I have come to abolish the Torah or the writings of the Prophets, but rather to fulfill them. The Torah, the Gospel, and the Quran have all three been considered "Light" (Noor). Allah said in Ayah 15 of this Surah: The Quran is Light. "قد جائكم من اللّه نور" In Ayah 44, He said: The Torah is Light. "انّا انزلنا التوراة فیها هدىً و نور" And in this Ayah, He considered the Gospel to be Light. "آتیناه الانجیل فیه هدىً و نور"

112) Al-Baqarah, 2.

Messages

1- The Prophets and their Books all originate from one source and share one goal, and they confirm one another. "مصدّقاً"

2- Although the Prophets and the Divine Books are meant to guide all people, only the people of piety and God-consciousness (Taqwa) are guided by the light of this guidance. "هدىً و رحمة للمتّقین"

Ayah

وَلْيَحْكُمْ أَهْلُ ٱلْإِنجِيلِ بِمَآ أَنزَلَ ٱللَّهُ فِيهِ ۚ وَمَن لَّمْ يَحْكُم بِمَآ أَنزَلَ ٱللَّهُ فَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ هُمُ ٱلْفَـٰسِقُونَ (٤۷)
Let the people of the Evangel judge by what Allah has sent down in it. Those who do not judge by what Allah has sent down—it is they who are the transgressors. (47)

Key Points

Regarding those who do not judge according to the Divine Law, the terms ẓālimūn (wrongdoers/oppressors), fāsiqūn (defiantly disobedient), and kāfirūn (disbelievers) are used in several consecutive verses. This is because such people are kāfirūn due to trampling upon the law of Allah, they are fāsiqūn because they exit the boundaries of their responsibility, and they are ẓālimūn because they commit injustice against the accused. Verses 44 and 45 address the Jews, whom [Allah] considers ẓālim and kāfir because they distort the law of Allah, sell the religion for a small price, and fear people instead of fearing Allah. (He says,) أولئك هم الكافرون (Those are the disbelievers) and they oppress the rights of individuals in society. (He says,) أولئك هم الظالمون (Those are the wrongdoers). However, this verse is about the Christians (Naṣārā) who merely did not judge according to the Gospel (neither silence regarding retribution/Qisas nor selling the religion). Therefore, they are fāsiqūn because they have gone outside the boundary of truth (or right/justice).

Messages

1- The criterion for recognizing truth and falsehood in Divine religions is that which has been revealed by Allah. وَلْيَحْكُمْ أَهْلُ الْإِنجِيلِ بِمَا أَنزَلَ اللَّهُ فِيهِ

2- Governmental laws exist in the Gospel. وَلْيَحْكُمْ أَهْلُ الْإِنجِيلِ بِمَا أَنزَلَ اللَّهُ فِيهِ

3- Anyone who, for any motive, does not judge based on Divine Law or refrains from judging justly is fāsiq (defiantly disobedient). مَن لَّمْ يَحْكُم... فَأُولَئِكَ هُمُ الْفَاسِقُونَ

Ayah

وَأَنزَلْنَآ إِلَيْكَ ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ بِٱلْحَقِّ مُصَدِّقًا لِّمَا بَيْنَ يَدَيْهِ مِنَ ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ وَمُهَيْمِنًا عَلَيْهِ ۖ فَٱحْكُم بَيْنَهُم بِمَآ أَنزَلَ ٱللَّهُ ۖ وَلَا تَتَّبِعْ أَهْوَآءَهُمْ عَمَّا جَآءَكَ مِنَ ٱلْحَقِّ ۚ لِكُلٍّ جَعَلْنَا مِنكُمْ شِرْعَةً وَمِنْهَاجًا ۚ وَلَوْ شَآءَ ٱللَّهُ لَجَعَلَكُمْ أُمَّةً وَٰحِدَةً وَلَـٰكِن لِّيَبْلُوَكُمْ فِى مَآ ءَاتَىٰكُمْ ۖ فَٱسْتَبِقُوا۟ ٱلْخَيْرَٰتِ ۚ إِلَى ٱللَّهِ مَرْجِعُكُمْ جَمِيعًا فَيُنَبِّئُكُم بِمَا كُنتُمْ فِيهِ تَخْتَلِفُونَ (٤۸)
We have sent down to you the Book with the truth, confirming what was before it of the Book and as a guardian over it. So judge between them by what Allah has sent down, and do not follow their desires against the truth that has come to you. For each [community] among you We had appointed a code [of law] and a path, and had Allah wished He would have made you one community, but [His purposes required] that He should test you in respect to what He has given you. So take the lead in all good works. To Allah shall be the return of you all, whereat He will inform you concerning that about which you used to differ. (48)

Key Points

Shar'ah (شرعة) is a path that leads to a river. Minhāj (منهاج) is a clear path. Ibn Abbas says: Shar'ah (شرعة) refers to the rulings mentioned in the Quran, and Minhāj (منهاج) is the Sunnah of the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله. [113] The word Muhaymin (مهیمن) means supervisor and protector. Affirming the divine origin of the Torah and the Gospel does not mean their perpetual preservation, but rather signifies the alignment and harmony of the content of all heavenly books. (Muṣaddiqan limā bayna yadayhi) The fundamentals (uṣūl) of all religions are one, but their laws (sharā’i‘) are multiple, like a sea into which multiple rivers flow. Religion (Dīn) and Divine Law (Sharī‘ah) are paths that lead us to true and human life, but only one Divine Law is accepted in any given time.

113) Mufradāt of Raghib.

Messages

1- The Quran is entirely truth and purified from all falsehood. «الكتاب بالحقّ»

2- The Quran was in written form even during the era of the Prophet. «الكتاب»

3- The Quran is the guardian of the fundamentals of other heavenly books. «مهیمناً علیه»

4- It is permissible to judge among the People of the Book according to the Quran. «فاحكم بینهم بما انزل اللّه»

5- The danger that threatens leaders is following the desires of the people and ignoring the truth. «ولا تتّبع أهواءهم»

6- One of the tools of divine testing is the arrival of a new Divine Law, so that it becomes clear who believes and who commits disbelief and shows fanaticism. «لكلّ جعلنا منكم شرعة... لیبلوكم»

7- Throughout history, humanity has needed the guidance of the prophets. «لكلّ جعلنا منكم شرعة و منهاجاً»

8- Instead of negative obstinacy, and before we lose the opportunity, let us race toward good deeds. «فاستبقوا الخیرات»

9- The focus and goal of competitions must be good and spiritual matters. «فاستبقوا الخیرات»

10- Belief in the Resurrection (Ma‘ād) is a factor in resolving differences and racing toward goodness. «فاستبقوا الخیرات... فینبّئكم بماكنتم فیه تختلفون»

Ayah

وَأَنِ ٱحْكُم بَيْنَهُم بِمَآ أَنزَلَ ٱللَّهُ وَلَا تَتَّبِعْ أَهْوَآءَهُمْ وَٱحْذَرْهُمْ أَن يَفْتِنُوكَ عَنۢ بَعْضِ مَآ أَنزَلَ ٱللَّهُ إِلَيْكَ ۖ فَإِن تَوَلَّوْا۟ فَٱعْلَمْ أَنَّمَا يُرِيدُ ٱللَّهُ أَن يُصِيبَهُم بِبَعْضِ ذُنُوبِهِمْ ۗ وَإِنَّ كَثِيرًا مِّنَ ٱلنَّاسِ لَفَـٰسِقُونَ (٤۹)
Judge between them by what Allah has sent down, and do not follow their desires. Beware of them lest they should beguile you from part of what Allah has sent down to you. But if they turn their backs [on you], then know that Allah desires to punish them for some of their sins, and indeed many of the people are transgressors. (49)

Key Points

The meaning of fitnah (trial/temptation) in «یفتنوك» (that they may tempt you) is plotting to deviate the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله. Regarding the occasion of the revelation (Sha'n Nuzul) of the Ayah, it is said that a group of Jewish scholars told the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله: If you judge in our favor concerning a certain disputed matter between us and others, we will embrace faith, and following us, all the Jews will also embrace faith. This Ayah was revealed and informed the Prophet of this plot.

Messages

  1. Judging and arbitration are among the functions of prophethood. «و ان احكم بینهم»
  2. The basis of judgment and governance is the law of Allah. «وان احكم بینهم بما انزل اللَّه»
  3. The Prophet of Islam has authority (Wilayah) over the Jews and Christians, in addition to the Muslims. «و ان احكم بینهم...»
  4. In important and vital matters, repetition of reminders is necessary. (The command "Judge only by the decree of Allah and do not follow the desires of the people" is repeated in this Ayah and the previous Ayah.)
  5. Paying attention to the carnal desires of the people is one of the great dangers for a judge and a ruler. «و لاتتّبع اهواءهم»
  6. Divine warnings are the factor ensuring the immunity and infallibility ('Ismah) of the Prophets. «واحذرهم»
  7. We must be vigilant against the cultural infiltration of enemies. «واحذرهم أن یفتنوك»
  8. When the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله must be wary of the plots of the disbelievers, the duty of others is clear. «واحذرهم أن یفتنوك»
  9. Even abandoning some of the Divine decrees is a sign of following the desires of the people. «أن یفتنوك عن بعض...»
  10. Opponents also approve of some Divine commands. The danger lies in turning away from and distorting some decrees. «عن بعض ما انزل اللّه»
  11. We must judge according to revelation, even if we anticipate that people might turn away. «وان احكم بینهم بما انزل اللَّه... فان تولّوا»
  12. The end does not justify the means. One should not resort to unjust judgment and arbitration for the sake of a group embracing Islam. (Considering the occasion of the revelation)
  13. Sharp warnings such as «لا تتّبع» (do not follow), «احذرهم» (beware of them), «یفتنوك» (they tempt you)... demonstrate the sincerity of the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله in conveying the Divine revelation; otherwise, no one would address himself so sharply.
  14. Turning away from the Prophet's judgment is a factor leading to downfall and a cause of Divine punishment. «ان یصیبهم ببعض ذنوبهم»
  15. The root cause of the disbelievers' turning away is transgression (fisq); otherwise, there is no flaw in the Divine religion. «فان تولّوا فاعلم... لفاسقون»

Ayah

أَفَحُكْمَ ٱلْجَـٰهِلِيَّةِ يَبْغُونَ ۚ وَمَنْ أَحْسَنُ مِنَ ٱللَّهِ حُكْمًا لِّقَوْمٍ يُوقِنُونَ (٥٠)
Do they seek the judgement of [pagan] ignorance? But who is better than Allah in judgement for a people who have certainty? (50)

Key Points

The best law is one whose Lawgiver possesses the following conditions: * He must be aware of all the secrets of existence and humanity, in the present and the future. * He must have no self-serving goal. * He must have no intentional or unintentional slip. * He must fear no power. * He must be benevolent toward everyone. These conditions exist only in Allah, the Exalted. Therefore, the Quran states: «مَن أحسن من اللّه حكماً» (Who is better than Allah in judgment?) And any human law that contradicts the judgment of Allah is ignorant. «أفحكم الجاهلیّة» (Do they seek the judgment of the Time of Ignorance?) (Because these laws are often established based on whims and desires, fear, greed, ignorance, error, imagination, and scientific limitations.)

Messages

1- Some people (polytheists, People of the Book) sought the Prophet's judgment based on the laws of the Time of Ignorance (Jahiliyyah). «افحكم الجاهلیّة یبغون»

2- Transgression (Fisq) and sin lead man toward the culture of ignorance (Jahiliyyah). «وانّ كثیراً من النّاس لفاسقون... افحكم الجاهلیّة»

3- Those who turn away from divine laws despite having them and resort to human laws are walking the path of the Time of Ignorance. «فان تولّوا... أفحكم الجاهلیّة یبغون»

4- The Time of Ignorance is not restricted to a single era; whenever people separate themselves from Allah, it is the era of Ignorance. «افحكم الجاهلیّة...»

5- Reason and conscience are the best judge and court. «و مَن احسن من اللّه حكماً»

6- Those who focus solely on human laws and consider them the path to perfection should doubt their faith and certainty. «لقوم یوقنون»

Ayah

۞ يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ لَا تَتَّخِذُوا۟ ٱلْيَهُودَ وَٱلنَّصَـٰرَىٰٓ أَوْلِيَآءَ ۘ بَعْضُهُمْ أَوْلِيَآءُ بَعْضٍ ۚ وَمَن يَتَوَلَّهُم مِّنكُمْ فَإِنَّهُۥ مِنْهُمْ ۗ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَا يَهْدِى ٱلْقَوْمَ ٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ (٥۱)
O you who have faith! Do not take the Jews and the Christians for allies: they are allies of each other. Any of you who allies with them is indeed one of them. Indeed Allah does not guide the wrongdoing lot. (51)

Key Points

Mentioning the Jews and Christians in the Ayah is by way of example; otherwise, there is no doubt that the guardianship (wilayah) of any disbeliever should not be accepted. It is understood from other verses of the Qur'an that utilizing non-meat foods of the People of the Book, or temporary marriage (mut'ah) with them, or trade and peaceful coexistence with them is permissible. And none of these issues means accepting their dominance (sultah).

Messages

1- Allah has specific expectations of His believing servants. «یا أیّها الّذین آمنوا لاتتّخذوا...»

2- Disassociation (tabarri) from the enemy is one of the conditions of faith. «یا أیّها الّذین آمنوا لاتتّخذوا...»

3- Islam is a religion of politics and does not deal only with individual rulings. «لا تتّخذوا الیهود و النصارى اولیاء»

4- When seeking help from disbelievers (kuffar) is prohibited by the Qur'an, then certainly, in foreign relations and policy, accepting the guardianship (wilayah) and dominance (sultah) of the People of the Book and the disbelievers is forbidden. «لاتتّخذوا»

5- We should not accept the guardianship (wilayah) of disbelievers and enemies, because they only care about those of their own creed. «بعضهم أولیاء بعض»

6- Despite the severe differences between the Jews and the Christians, their bond is for the sake of destroying Islam. «بعضهم اولیاء بعض»

7- Disbelievers are only loyal to the commitments among themselves, but they are not bound by commitments made with Muslims. «لاتتّخذوا الیهود والنصارى اولیاء... بعضهم اولیاء بعض...»

8- Those Islamic governments that have accepted the guardianship (wilayah) and dominance (sultah) of the disbelievers are considered part of the disbelievers. «فانّه منهم» (Friendship with any person or group makes one part of them.)

9- Neither take the disbelievers as your 'wali' (guardian) nor maintain a relationship of guardianship (wilayah) with those who have accepted the guardianship of the disbelievers. «فانّه منهم»

10- The result of accepting the guardianship (wilayah) of the disbelievers is the severing of the guardianship of Allah. «فانّه منهم انّ اللَّه لایهدى القوم الظالمین»

11- Relying on the disbelievers is oppression (zulm). «لا یهدى القوم الظالمین»

Ayahs

فَتَرَى ٱلَّذِينَ فِى قُلُوبِهِم مَّرَضٌ يُسَـٰرِعُونَ فِيهِمْ يَقُولُونَ نَخْشَىٰٓ أَن تُصِيبَنَا دَآئِرَةٌ ۚ فَعَسَى ٱللَّهُ أَن يَأْتِىَ بِٱلْفَتْحِ أَوْ أَمْرٍ مِّنْ عِندِهِۦ فَيُصْبِحُوا۟ عَلَىٰ مَآ أَسَرُّوا۟ فِىٓ أَنفُسِهِمْ نَـٰدِمِينَ (٥۲)
Yet you see those in whose hearts is a sickness rushing to them, saying, ‘We fear lest a turn of fortune should visit us.’ Maybe Allah will bring about a victory, or a command from Him, and then they will be regretful for what they kept secret in their hearts, (52)
وَيَقُولُ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوٓا۟ أَهَـٰٓؤُلَآءِ ٱلَّذِينَ أَقْسَمُوا۟ بِٱللَّهِ جَهْدَ أَيْمَـٰنِهِمْ ۙ إِنَّهُمْ لَمَعَكُمْ ۚ حَبِطَتْ أَعْمَـٰلُهُمْ فَأَصْبَحُوا۟ خَـٰسِرِينَ (٥۳)
and the faithful will say, ‘Are these the ones who swore by Allah with solemn oaths that they were with you?!’ Their works have failed, and they have become losers. (53)

Key Points

The sick-hearted (bīmārdilān) are those who are in doubt regarding the truthfulness (haqqāniyyat) of the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله or regarding some religious teachings, and thus possess weak faith. [114] Regarding the meaning of the Ayah, there are three possibilities: A: At the time of the Muslims' victory and the exposure of the hypocrites, the believers say in astonishment: Were these the Jews and Christians who swore solemn oaths to aid you, the believers? B: Some of the true believers say to one another after the victory: Were these the sick-hearted who swore that they were with you, and you saw that they were not? C: Could these sick-hearted people, who swore that they were with the Jews and Christians, offer any help to you, the People of the Book?

114) Tafsir Rahnama.

Messages

1- The Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله witnesses the open and hasty efforts of the weak-in-faith individuals to join the Jews and Christians. «فترى الّذین فى قلوبهم مرض یسارعون فیهم»

2- A clear example of those deprived of Divine guidance are the sick-hearted who strive quickly to join the disbelievers (kuffār). «انّ اللَّه لایهدى القوم الظالمین... الّذین فى قلوبهم مرض» (The letter 'fā'' indicates that the wrongdoers (ẓālimīn) of the previous Ayah are the sick-hearted of this Ayah.)

3- The weak-in-faith sick-hearted quickly seek to establish friendship with the disbelieving enemies. «فى قلوبهم مرض یسارعون فیهم»

4- Weak-in-faith individuals and hypocrites desire to be among the disbelievers. «یسارعون فیهم», not «الیهم» (to them).

5- One must not engage in ijtihād (independent reasoning) or expediency when faced with the explicit text and command of Allah. «لاتتّخذوا الیهود... اولیاء... یقولون نخشى»

6- The motivation for accepting a humiliating relationship with superpowers is weakness of faith and fear of other than Allah. «یقولون نخشى»

7- Muslims should be hopeful for the victory and expansion of Islam and the exposure of the hypocrites. «فعسى اللّه أن یأتى بالفتح...»

8- Political honor, economic power, and military victory all come from Allah and are in His Hand. «أن یأتى بالفتح أو أمر من عنده»

9- Do not maintain a relationship of guardianship (walāyah) with the disbelievers, so that Allah's unseen aids may flow towards you. «لا تتّخذوا... یأتى بالفتح او امر من عنده»

10- The end result of hypocrisy and dependence on the disbelievers is nullification (hubūt), disgrace, and shame. «نادمین»

11- Not every oath is a sign of sincerity; do not be deceived by oaths. «أهؤلاء الّذین اقسموا باللّه انّهم معكم»

12- Dependence on foreigners is subject to the blame of the believers. «ویقول الّذین آمنواأهؤلاء... انّهم معكم»

13- The sign of human loss (khusrān) is the nullification of good deeds. «حبطت اعمالكم فاصبحوا خاسرین»

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ مَن يَرْتَدَّ مِنكُمْ عَن دِينِهِۦ فَسَوْفَ يَأْتِى ٱللَّهُ بِقَوْمٍ يُحِبُّهُمْ وَيُحِبُّونَهُۥٓ أَذِلَّةٍ عَلَى ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ أَعِزَّةٍ عَلَى ٱلْكَـٰفِرِينَ يُجَـٰهِدُونَ فِى سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ وَلَا يَخَافُونَ لَوْمَةَ لَآئِمٍ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ فَضْلُ ٱللَّهِ يُؤْتِيهِ مَن يَشَآءُ ۚ وَٱللَّهُ وَٰسِعٌ عَلِيمٌ (٥٤)
O you who have faith! Should any of you desert his religion, Allah will soon bring a people whom He loves and who love Him, [who will be] humble towards the faithful, stern towards the faithless, waging jihad in the way of Allah, not fearing the blame of any blamer. That is Allah’s grace, which He grants to whomever He wishes, and Allah is all-bounteous, all-knowing. (54)

Key Points

It is narrated: When this Ayah was revealed, the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله placed His hand on the shoulder of Salman al-Farsi and said: Your countrymen are the manifestation of this Ayah. [115] In the previous Ayahs, the danger of the dominance of disbelievers and hypocrites was discussed; here, the discussion is about apostasy (irtidad). Perhaps this is an indication that hypocrisy, friendship, and relations with the disbelievers, and accepting their dominance, lead to apostasy. Imam Baqir علیه السلام said regarding "یحبّهم و یحبّونه" (He loves them and they love Him): The reference is to Ali علیه السلام and his followers (Shi'a). [116] It is mentioned in Shi'a and Sunni narrations that the Prophet of Islam صلى الله علیه وآله, during the Battle of Khaybar, after the commanders failed to conquer the enemy fortresses, said: By Allah, tomorrow I shall give the banner to a man whom Allah and His Messenger love, and who also loves Allah and His Messenger, and he will bring victory. He then gave the banner to Ali علیه السلام. [117]

115) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 116) Tafsir Furat al-Kufi, p. 123. 117) Ihqaq al-Haqq, Vol. 3, p. 200.

Messages

1- The believer must be concerned about his ultimate fate, for many believers may apostatize. «یا ایها الّذین آمنوا مَن یرتدّ»
