2- An insightful leader must consider the possibility of apostasy and defection among his followers. «مَن یرتدّ منكم»

3- The disbelief or apostasy of a group of believers does not harm the path of Allah. «فسوف یأتى اللّه...»

4- Apostasy is the result of lacking knowledge and love for the religion and for Allah. «یأتى اللّه بقوم یحبّهم و یحبّونه»

5- Allah has no need for our faith. «فسوف یأتى اللّه»

6- On the path of faith and breaking the traditions and habits of ignorance (Jahiliyyah), one must not fear the reproaches, clamor, and negative propaganda of the enemy, nor surrender to the atmosphere and environment. «لا یخافون لومة لائم»

7- The conduct of a Muslim is gentleness towards religious brethren and firmness against the enemy; neither harshness nor gentleness is absolute. «اذلّة على المؤمنین أعزّة على الكافرین»

8- The favor (fadl) of Allah is not only wealth and status; the love of Allah, striving (Jihad) in His way, and decisiveness in religion are also manifestations of Divine grace and favor. «ذلك فضل اللَّه»

9- Mutual love between the servant and Allah is among the perfections of humanity. «یحبّهم و یحبّونه»

10- Prevent despair and fear by giving the promise of replacement by others. «من یرتدّ... فسوف یأتى... و لایخافون»

11- The true believer is one who is both a lover of Allah and beloved by Him. «یحبّهم و یحبّونه»

12- Whoever possesses true faith never feels humiliation before the disbelievers. «اعزّة على الكافرین»

13- A heart devoid of the love of Allah becomes sick and dependent on the disbelievers, but a heart full of the love of Allah never becomes dependent. «فى قلوبهم مرض یسارعون فیهم... یحبّهم و یحبّونه... اعزّة على الكفارین»

14- Islam is expanding; let us not only look at the losses but also at the growth. «من یرتدّ... یأتى اللَّه»

15- The banner of Islam will be held in the future by those who are lovers of Allah, striving (Mujahid), and firm. «یأتى اللَّه بقوم یحبّهم و یحبّونه... اعزّة على الكافرین یجاهدون فى سبیل اللّه»

16- Psychological warfare has no effect on true believers. «لا یخافون لومة لائم»

17- The favor of Allah is not limited, and He grants it knowingly to everyone according to their merit. «واللَّه واسع علیم»

18- If Allah bestows a favor upon someone, it is done knowingly. «واسع علیم»

Ayah

إِنَّمَا وَلِيُّكُمُ ٱللَّهُ وَرَسُولُهُۥ وَٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ ٱلَّذِينَ يُقِيمُونَ ٱلصَّلَوٰةَ وَيُؤْتُونَ ٱلزَّكَوٰةَ وَهُمْ رَٰكِعُونَ (٥٥)
Your guardian is only Allah, His Apostle, and the faithful who maintain the prayer and give the zakat while bowing down. (55)

Key Points

Regarding the occasion of the revelation (Sha'n Nuzul) of the Ayah, it is narrated: A beggar entered the Mosque of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله and asked the people for help. No one gave him anything. Imam Ali عليه السلام, while engaged in prayer, gave his ring to the beggar during the Ruku' (bowing). In honor of this act of charity, this Ayah was revealed. The above incident has been narrated by ten companions of the Prophet, such as Ibn Abbas, Ammar Yasir, Jabir ibn Abdullah, Abu Dharr, Anas ibn Malik, Bilal, and others, and both Shi'a and Sunni agree on this occasion of revelation. (118) Ammar Yasir says: It was after the charity of the ring during prayer and the revelation of the Ayah that the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله said: «مَن كنتُ مولاه فعلىّ مولاه» (Whoever I am his Mawla, Ali is his Mawla). (119) The Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله recited this Ayah at Ghadir Khumm to explain the status of Imam Ali عليه السلام. (120) And Ali عليه السلام himself recited this Ayah many times to assert His rightful claim. (121) Abu Dharr, who was himself a witness to the incident, narrated the above story to the people in the Sacred Mosque (Masjid al-Haram). (122) The word Wali (ولىّ) in this Ayah does not mean friend or helper, because friendship and help pertain to all Muslims, not just those who give charity while bowing (Ruku'). Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: The intent of «الّذین آمنوا...» (Those who believe...) is Ali عليه السلام and His descendants, the Imams علیهم السلام, until the Day of Judgment. Thus, whoever among His descendants reaches the position of Imamate is like Him with this characteristic: they give charity while bowing (Ruku'). (123) The late Fayd Kashani, in the book Nawadir, narrates a Hadith according to which the other infallible Imams also gave charity to the poor while praying and bowing (Ruku'), which is more consistent with the plural form of the words «یقیمون» (they establish), «یؤتون» (they give), and «الراكعون» (the bowers). Imam Baqir عليه السلام said: Allah commanded His Prophet to present the Wilayah (Guardianship) of Ali عليه السلام and revealed this Ayah. (124) The best introduction is to state the attributes and characteristics of a person, allowing the audience to find the specific example (the Ayah enumerates the attributes and actions of Ali عليه السلام without naming Him). Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: Ali عليه السلام had thousands of witnesses at Ghadir Khumm, yet He could not secure His right, whereas if a Muslim has two witnesses, he secures his right! (125) The Wilayat al-Faqih (Guardianship of the Jurist) is in line with the Wilayah of the Infallible Imam. In the Maqbula (Accepted Narration) of Umar ibn Hanzala from Imam Sadiq عليه السلام, we read: Look to the one who narrates our Hadith, examines our lawful and unlawful matters, and knows our rulings. Then be satisfied with his governance, for I have appointed him as a ruler over you. «فانّى قد جعلته علیكم حاكماً...» (For I have indeed appointed him as a ruler over you...). (126)

118) Al-Ghadir, Vol. 2, p. 52; Ihqaq al-Haqq, Vol. 2, p. 400; and Kanz al-Ummal, Vol. 6, p. 391. 119) Tafsir al-Mizan. 120) Tafsir Safi. 121) Tafsir al-Mizan. 122) Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan. 123) Kafi, Vol. 1, p. 288. 124) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn and Kafi, Vol. 1, p. 281. 125) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 126) Kafi, Vol. 1, p. 67.

Messages

  1. Islam is both the religion of Wilayah (Guardianship) and the religion of Bara'ah (Dissociation). It has both attraction and repulsion. The previous Ayahs forbade accepting the Wilayah of the Jews and Christians; this Ayah states: Take Allah, the Messenger, and the one who gave the ring while bowing (Ruku') as your Wali. «یا ایّها الّذین آمنوا لا تتّخذوا... انّما ولیّكم اللَّه...»
  2. From the fact that «ولیّكم» (Your Wali - singular) is used instead of «اولیائكم» (Your Awliya - plural), it can be inferred that the spirit of the Wilayah of the Prophet and Ali علیهما السلام is a ray of Divine Wilayah. «ولیّكم»
  3. Usually, prayer (Salat) and Zakat are mentioned together in the Quran, but in this Ayah, the two are intertwined (giving Zakat during prayer). «الّذین یقیمون الصلوة و یؤتون الزكاة و هم راكعون»
  4. Those who do not establish prayer and pay Zakat do not have the right of Wilayah over the people. «انّما ولیّكم اللَّه...» (Innama is a sign of the exclusivity of Wilayah to specific individuals).
  5. Attention to the deprived does not impede prayer. «یؤتون الزكاة و هم راكعون» (Indeed, the poor should not return empty-handed from the Muslim community.)
  6. Wilayah belongs to those who are steadfast in establishing prayer and paying Zakat. «یقیمون الصلوة ویؤتون...» (Yuqimun and Yu'tun indicate permanence/continuity).
  7. Any form of Wilayah, governance, or guardianship that is not through Allah, the Messenger, and the Imam is void. «انّما ولیّكم اللَّه...» (The word Innama is a sign of exclusivity).
  8. Attention to creation for the sake of Allah during prayer does not contradict the establishment of prayer. «یؤتون الزكاة و هم راكعون»
  9. The one who is indifferent toward the poor should not be your leader and Wali. «انّما ولیّكم اللَّه... و یؤتون الزكاة»
  10. Minor actions (like giving a ring in charity) do not invalidate the prayer. «یقیمون الصلوة و یؤتون الزكاة و هم راكعون»
  11. In the culture of the Quran, even voluntary charity (Sadaqah Mustahabbah) is called Zakat. «یؤتون الزكاة»
  12. Wilayahs are in sequence (longitudinal), not in opposition (transverse). Guardianship over Muslims belongs first to Allah, then to the Prophet, and then to the Imam. «انّما ولیّكم اللَّه و رسوله و الّذین آمنوا...»
  13. Imam Ali عليه السلام possessed Wilayah even during the time of the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله. «انّما ولیّكم اللَّه و رسوله» (The apparent meaning of Wilayah is actual Wilayah, not potential Wilayah. This is because Wilayah is mentioned only once in the Ayah, implying that either all of it is actual or all of it is potential.)

Ayah

وَمَن يَتَوَلَّ ٱللَّهَ وَرَسُولَهُۥ وَٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ فَإِنَّ حِزْبَ ٱللَّهِ هُمُ ٱلْغَـٰلِبُونَ (٥٦)
Whoever takes for his guardians Allah, His Apostle and the faithful [should know that] the confederates of Allah are indeed the victorious. (56)

Key Points

In the previous Ayah, it was stated that the intent of "الّذین آمنوا" (those who believe) is Hadrat Ali عليه السلام and the infallible Imams علیهم السلام after him. The characteristics of the Party of Allah (Hizbullah) are described in Surah Al-Mujadilah, Ayah 22, as follows: 1. They believe in the Origin (of creation) and the Return (Resurrection). 2. They do not befriend the enemies of Allah. This Ayah, which expresses the necessity of accepting the Wilayah (guardianship/authority) of Allah, the Messenger, and the Imams, makes it clear that the meaning of "ولیّكم" (your Wali/Guardian) in the previous Ayah is the guardian and ruler, not merely a friend or helper. This is because the expression "حزب اللّه" (Party of Allah) and their dominance (غلبه) point toward a powerful system and government. The word "حزب" (Hizb) literally means a group possessing power and firmness. [127] Since victory is impossible without organization, management, power, unity, courage, and so on, the "حزب اللّه" (Party of Allah) must possess these qualities for governance and dominance. The Party of Allah is not only dominant and victorious, but they are also prosperous. "حزب اللّه هم الغالبون" (The Party of Allah, they are the dominant ones), "حزب اللّه هم المفلحون" (The Party of Allah, they are the prosperous ones). [128] In Ayah 52 of this very Surah, we read that a group, fearing calamities, takes the disbelievers as their guardians (Wali). In this Ayah, He states: The Party of Allah is dominant, meaning those affiliated with the disbelievers are defeated, so do not turn to them. "فترى الّذین... یسارعون فیهم..." (You see those who... hasten toward them...), "ومَن یتولّ اللَّه... هم الغالبون" (And whoever takes Allah as Wali... they are the dominant ones).

127) Mu'jam al-Wasit. 128) Al-Mujadilah, 22.

Messages

  1. The Party of Allah consists only of those who have accepted the Wilayah (guardianship/authority) of Allah, the Prophet, and the Ahl al-Bayt علیهم السلام. "ومَن یتولّ اللَّه... فانّ حزب اللّه"
  2. The ultimate dominance belongs to those who accept the Wilayah of Allah, the Prophet, and the Ahl al-Bayt علیهم السلام. "ومَن یتولّ... هم الغالبون"
  3. Since Allah is Dominant, "واللّه غالب على أمره" [129] (And Allah is dominant over His command), those affiliated with Him are also dominant. "حزب اللّه هم الغالبون"
  4. Based on divine promises, the Party of Allah is confident about its future. "هم الغالبون"

129) Yusuf, 21.

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ لَا تَتَّخِذُوا۟ ٱلَّذِينَ ٱتَّخَذُوا۟ دِينَكُمْ هُزُوًا وَلَعِبًا مِّنَ ٱلَّذِينَ أُوتُوا۟ ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ مِن قَبْلِكُمْ وَٱلْكُفَّارَ أَوْلِيَآءَ ۚ وَٱتَّقُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ إِن كُنتُم مُّؤْمِنِينَ (٥۷)
O you who have faith! Do not take those who take your religion in derision and play, from among those who were given the Book before you, and the infidels, as friends, and be wary of Allah, should you be faithful. (57)

Messages

1- The worldly punishment for insulting and mocking the religion of Allah and religious sanctities is cutting off relations. «لا تتّخذوا...» (In relations with others, respect for sanctities is a necessary condition.)

2- The condition of faith is possessing religious zeal and disassociating oneself from the unworthy. «یا ایّها الّذین امنوا لاتتّخذوا»

3- Mocking the religion (through cold and psychological warfare) is one of the methods of struggle employed by the enemies. «اتّخذوا دینكم هزوا»

4- Divine piety necessitates that we do not fear the enemies and do not take them as allies. «لا تتّخذوا... اولیاء و اتّقوا اللَّه...»

Ayah

وَإِذَا نَادَيْتُمْ إِلَى ٱلصَّلَوٰةِ ٱتَّخَذُوهَا هُزُوًا وَلَعِبًا ۚ ذَٰلِكَ بِأَنَّهُمْ قَوْمٌ لَّا يَعْقِلُونَ (٥۸)
When you call to prayer, they take it in derision and play. That is because they are a people who do not exercise their reason. (58)

Key Points

| It is mentioned regarding the occasion of revelation (Shān Nuzūl) of the above Ayah that a group of Jews and some Christians, upon hearing the voice of the caller (Mu'adhdhin) or seeing the Muslims standing for prayer, began to mock and ridicule them. [130]

130) Tafsir Namūneh.

Messages

1- Avoid relationships and friendship with those who mock the Adhān (call to prayer) (and religious rituals). «لاتتّخذوا... و اذا نادیتم...»

2- Prayer (Salat) is the face and manifestation of religion. (In the previous Ayah, mocking religion was discussed, and in this Ayah, mocking prayer is discussed; meaning prayer is the countenance of religion.) «اتّخذوا دینكم هزواً... و اذا نادیتم الى الصلوة اتّخذوها هزوا»

3- A call must be raised for prayer so that everyone gathers. «نادیتم» (Prayer must be public.)

4- In an Islamic society, the call for prayer must be shouted and publicized, and no one should prevent it. «نادیتم الى الصلوة»

5- The method of the wise is logical engagement, but the foolish are prone to mockery. «ذلك بانّهم قوم لا یعقلون»

Ayah

قُلْ يَـٰٓأَهْلَ ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ هَلْ تَنقِمُونَ مِنَّآ إِلَّآ أَنْ ءَامَنَّا بِٱللَّهِ وَمَآ أُنزِلَ إِلَيْنَا وَمَآ أُنزِلَ مِن قَبْلُ وَأَنَّ أَكْثَرَكُمْ فَـٰسِقُونَ (٥۹)
Say, ‘O People of the Book! Are you vindictive toward us for any reason except that we have faith in Allah and in what has been sent down to us, and in what was sent down before, and that most of you are transgressors?’ (59)

Key Points

Tanqimūn (or tanqamūn) is derived from the root naqmah, meaning the denial or disapproval of something, whether by tongue, or by means of force, punishment, or confrontation. [131] The People of the Book did not remain faithful to the contents of the Torah and the Gospel; therefore, they are not worthy for the heavenly books to be attributed to them. Wa mā nuzila min qabl.

131) Mufradāt of Rāghib.

Messages

  1. Even with opponents, one must engage in a good debate (or dispute). Argumentation mixed with emotional questions. Hal tanqimūna minnā.

  2. The enmity of the enemies towards the believers is only because of their faith. Hal tanqimūna minnā illā an āmannā billāh.

  3. The Muslims' faith in all heavenly books is one of the factors causing the malice and hostility of the People of the Book towards them. Hal tanqimūna minnā illā an āmannā billāh wa unzila ilaynā wa mā nuzila min qabl.

  4. The hostility of the People of the Book towards Muslims is historical and long-standing. Hal tanqimūna minnā.

  5. In the face of the enemies' harshness, we must not lose justice and fairness, nor consider everyone equally wicked (or corrupt). Aktharukum fāsiqūn.

  6. Denying the truth and harming the followers of the truth is wickedness (or transgression). Fāsiqūn.

Ayah

قُلْ هَلْ أُنَبِّئُكُم بِشَرٍّ مِّن ذَٰلِكَ مَثُوبَةً عِندَ ٱللَّهِ ۚ مَن لَّعَنَهُ ٱللَّهُ وَغَضِبَ عَلَيْهِ وَجَعَلَ مِنْهُمُ ٱلْقِرَدَةَ وَٱلْخَنَازِيرَ وَعَبَدَ ٱلطَّـٰغُوتَ ۚ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ شَرٌّ مَّكَانًا وَأَضَلُّ عَن سَوَآءِ ٱلسَّبِيلِ (٦٠)
Say, ‘Shall I inform you concerning something worse than that as a requital from Allah? Those whom Allah has cursed and with whom He is wrathful, and turned some of whom into apes and swine, and worshippers of fake deities! Such are in a worse situation and more astray from the right way.’ (60)

Key Points

The terms mathūbah (مَثُوبة) and thawāb (ثواب) originally mean returning or reverting to the initial state. They are also used for any kind of destiny or recompense (jazā), but they are mostly applied to good rewards. [132] Those who mock the religion, Islam, and the prayers of Muslims, and who torment Muslims because of their faith, why do they not look at their own dark and shameful past, when they were transformed (masakh) and disgraced by divine wrath? Although the Jews of the Prophet's time were not physically in the form of pigs and monkeys, since the Children of Israel considered themselves a single nation with a collective identity and attributed past glories to themselves, mentioning the previous disgraces breaks their pride. Many commentators believe that the Jews who violated the sanctity of the Sabbath through legalistic tricks were transformed into monkeys, and the Christians who denied the heavenly table (Mā'idah) of Jesus were transformed into pigs. [133]

132) Tafsir-e Nemooneh. 133) Tafsir-e Rāhnamā.

Messages

1- Divine punishments are just, and if a group is transformed into monkeys and pigs, it is the recompense for their actions. «مثوبةً»

2- Those who obey the Tāghūt (tyrant/false deity) are on the same level as those who were transformed. «و جعل منهم القردة و الخنازیر و عبد الطاغوت»

3- Stating the ominous destiny of historical evildoers is one of the methods of guidance and propagation used by the Quran. «جعل منهم...»

4- One who violates sanctity has no sanctity. «اولئك شرّ مكاناً و اضّل...»

5- Severe punishment is for severe deviation. «بشرٍّ من ذلك مثوبة... اضلّ عن سواء السبیل»

Ayah

وَإِذَا جَآءُوكُمْ قَالُوٓا۟ ءَامَنَّا وَقَد دَّخَلُوا۟ بِٱلْكُفْرِ وَهُمْ قَدْ خَرَجُوا۟ بِهِۦ ۚ وَٱللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ بِمَا كَانُوا۟ يَكْتُمُونَ (٦۱)
When they come to you, they say, ‘We believe.’ Certainly, they enter with disbelief and leave with it, and Allah knows best as to what they have been concealing. (61)

Messages

1- Do not let the declaration of faith by individuals deceive you. «قالوا آمنّا... واللَّه اعلم» Declaring faith merely with the tongue is not a sign of faith penetrating the heart.

2- Allah is more aware of the inner thoughts of people than they are themselves. «اللّه أعلم»

3- Those who hypocritically declare faith should know that Allah is aware of their inner state. «قالوا آمنّا و قد دخلوا بالكفر... واللَّه اعلم...»

Ayah

وَتَرَىٰ كَثِيرًا مِّنْهُمْ يُسَـٰرِعُونَ فِى ٱلْإِثْمِ وَٱلْعُدْوَٰنِ وَأَكْلِهِمُ ٱلسُّحْتَ ۚ لَبِئْسَ مَا كَانُوا۟ يَعْمَلُونَ (٦۲)
You see many of them actively engaged in sin and aggression, and consuming illicit gains. Surely, evil is what they have been doing. (62)

Key Points

In the previous Ayah, the discussion was about the spirit of disbelief (kufr) and hypocrisy (nifāq) among the People of the Book. Here, it describes their moral, social, and economic corruption. The word suḥt (سُحت) originally means separating the skin (peeling) and also means severe hunger. It was then applied to any illicit wealth, especially bribery, because such wealth removes purity, freshness, and blessing (barakah) from human society. Just as peeling the bark off a tree causes it to wither or dry up. Therefore, suḥt has a broad meaning, and if a specific instance of it is mentioned in some narrations (Riwāyāt), it does not imply exclusivity. [134]

134) Tafsir Namūneh.

Messages

1- When criticizing the deviated, one must be fair. (kathīran minhum - "many of them") (not all of them).

2- The characteristic of the Islamic society is hastening towards good deeds (khayrāt), "يُسَارِعُونَ فِي الْخَيْرَاتِ" [135], but the characteristic of the society of disbelief and hypocrisy is hastening towards corruption. "يُسَارِعُونَ فِي الْإِثْمِ وَالْعُدْوَانِ"

3- Worse than moral corruption (ithm), social corruption (ʿudwān), and administrative corruption (suḥt), is the habit of corruption and speed in committing it. "يُسَارِعُونَ فِي الْإِثْمِ..." (Yusāriʿūn denotes continuity/persistence).

4- How often are there claimants of faith who hasten towards sin, transgression, and consuming illicit wealth. "تَرَىٰ ... يُسَارِعُونَ..."

5- Worse than being tainted by sin is being immersed in sin. (fī al-ithm - "in the sin").

6- Although transgression (ʿudwān) and consuming illicit wealth are examples of ithm (sin), these two are mentioned separately due to their importance and danger, or due to their prevalence among the People of the Book. "يُسَارِعُونَ فِي الْإِثْمِ وَالْعُدْوَانِ وَأَكْلِهِمُ السُّحْتَ"

135) Al-Mu’minūn, 61.

Ayah

لَوْلَا يَنْهَىٰهُمُ ٱلرَّبَّـٰنِيُّونَ وَٱلْأَحْبَارُ عَن قَوْلِهِمُ ٱلْإِثْمَ وَأَكْلِهِمُ ٱلسُّحْتَ ۚ لَبِئْسَ مَا كَانُوا۟ يَصْنَعُونَ (٦۳)
Why do not the rabbis and the scribes forbid them from sinful speech and consuming illicit gains? Surely, evil is what they have been working. (63)

Key Points

Imam Ali عليه السلام states near the end of the sermon "Al-Qasi'ah" [136]: Allah did not curse the previous nations except because they abandoned enjoining good (Amr bil Ma'ruf) and forbidding evil (Nahy anil Munkar). Allah cursed the common people for committing sins and the scholars for abandoning the forbidding of evil. The intent of al-Rabbaniyyūn (the devout scholars) is the Christian scholars, and the intent of al-Ahbār (the rabbis) is the Jewish scholars. Imam Husayn عليه السلام said regarding this Ayah: The reason for the silence of the scholars and their abandonment of enjoining good and forbidding evil is either greed for the benefits they received from the oppressors or fear of them: «رغبة فیما كانوا ینالون منهم او رهبة مما یحذرون» [137] The previous Ayah used the phrase «ما كانوا یعملون» (what they used to do) regarding the sinners, but here, regarding the silence of the scholars, it uses the phrase «ما كانوا یصنعون» (what they used to craft/produce). The late Fayd Kashani says: The difference between ya'malūn and yaṣna'ūn is that a ṣāni' (crafter/maker) is someone who has become experienced and skillful through practice, and the action has become a characteristic and an ingrained habit for him.

136) Nahj al-Balaghah, Sermon 192. 137) Tafsir Rahnama and Bihar al-Anwar, Vol. 100, p. 79.

Messages

1- The responsibility of enjoining good and forbidding evil primarily falls upon the scholars. «لولاینهاهم الربانیون...»

2- The silence and indifference of the scholars pave the way for the promotion of corruption. «ترى ... یسارعون فى الاثم... لولا ینهاهم...»

3- Scholars must possess the power to prevent corruption, in addition to merely preaching and inviting people (to good). «لولا ینهاهم»

4- If forbidding evil does not stop the sin entirely, it at least prevents the speed of its commission. «یسارعون فى الاثم... لولا ینهاهم»

5- The scholars of the People of the Book did not even fulfill the minimum of their duty. (In the previous Ayah, ithm (sin), ʿudwān (transgression), and consuming the unlawful (suḥt) were counted as their sins. The word ʿudwān is not mentioned here, perhaps indicating that even if the scholars of the People of the Book lacked the power to prevent ʿudwān, why did they not prevent ithm and suḥt?)!

6- The beauty of knowledge lies in its manifestation, and its ugliness lies in silence and concealment. «لبئس ما كانوا یصنعون»

7- The silence of the scholar is worse than the sin of the transgressor. «لبئس ما كانوا یصنعون»

8- Open display of sin, the multitude of sinners, speed in sin, variety in sin, etc.—none of these are reasons for the scholars to remain silent. «ترى كثیراً منهم یسارعون فى الاثم و... لولا ینهاهم» (The word «تَرى» symbolizes the open display of sin; the word «كثیر» symbolizes the large number of people; the word «یسارعون» symbolizes speed in sin; and the words «اثم», «عدوان», and «سُحت» symbolize the variety of sins.)

Ayah

وَقَالَتِ ٱلْيَهُودُ يَدُ ٱللَّهِ مَغْلُولَةٌ ۚ غُلَّتْ أَيْدِيهِمْ وَلُعِنُوا۟ بِمَا قَالُوا۟ ۘ بَلْ يَدَاهُ مَبْسُوطَتَانِ يُنفِقُ كَيْفَ يَشَآءُ ۚ وَلَيَزِيدَنَّ كَثِيرًا مِّنْهُم مَّآ أُنزِلَ إِلَيْكَ مِن رَّبِّكَ طُغْيَـٰنًا وَكُفْرًا ۚ وَأَلْقَيْنَا بَيْنَهُمُ ٱلْعَدَٰوَةَ وَٱلْبَغْضَآءَ إِلَىٰ يَوْمِ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ ۚ كُلَّمَآ أَوْقَدُوا۟ نَارًا لِّلْحَرْبِ أَطْفَأَهَا ٱللَّهُ ۚ وَيَسْعَوْنَ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ فَسَادًا ۚ وَٱللَّهُ لَا يُحِبُّ ٱلْمُفْسِدِينَ (٦٤)
The Jews say, ‘Allah’s hand is tied up.’ Tied up be their hands, and cursed be they for what they say! No, His hands are wide open: He bestows as He wishes. Surely many of them will be increased in rebellion and unfaith by what has been sent to you from your Lord, and We have cast enmity and hatred amongst them until the Day of Resurrection. Every time they ignite the flames of war, Allah puts them out. They seek to cause corruption on the earth, and Allah does not like the agents of corruption. (64)

Key Points

The previous Ayah referred to the improper and irrelevant statements of the Jews, "و قولهم الاثم" (and their sinful saying), and the failure of their scholars to prevent them, "لولا ینهاهم..." (Why do not their rabbis forbid them...). In this Ayah, an example is presented where the Jews used to say: The Hand of Allah is tied up, and He can no longer grant us power and glory as He did in the past.

It is mentioned in narrations that this Ayah expresses the Jewish belief regarding Divine Decree (Qada wa Qadar) and destiny. This is because the Jews imagined that the Hand of Allah was open at the beginning of creation, but after He created everything, His Hand became tied up. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام, explaining this Ayah, said: The belief of the Jews was that after creation, Allah withdrew from managing affairs and does not increase or decrease anything. "قد فرغ من الامر فلایزید و لاینقص" (He has finished the matter, so He neither increases nor decreases). [138]

In the Arabic language, the word "یَد" (Yad), and in Persian, the word "Dast" (hand), is a metaphor for power and influence. For example, we say: So-and-so has a hand in the region or the administration. So-and-so's hand was cut off (meaning their influence was removed). Our hand does not reach so-and-so (meaning we lack the power to affect them). It is also mentioned in narrations that the friends of Allah (Awliya) are the Hand of Allah. Meaning, they are the intermediaries of His grace toward the people.

Imam Baqir عليه السلام said regarding "كلّما اوقدوا ناراً..." (Every time they kindled a fire...): Whenever a tyrant intends to destroy the Family of Muhammad علیهم السلام, Allah crushes him. [139]

According to Allamah Tabataba'i: Whenever poverty, hunger, or famine occurred, or when verses regarding lending to Allah were revealed, they would say: This famine and this recommendation for a good loan (Qard al-Hasanah) are signs that the Hand of Allah is tied up.

138) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn and Tawhid al-Saduq, p. 167. 139) Tafsir Ayyashi.

Messages

1- Consent to the sins of others is participation in them. "قالت الیهود" (The Jews said) (Although only some Jews attributed the tying of the Hand to Allah, because others were also content with it, this deviant thought is attributed to all of them.)

2- We should not justify our own incompetence and unworthiness. (Satan attributed his arrogance to Divine temptation). "ربّ بما أغویتنى ..." (My Lord, because You have put me in error...) [140]. The Jews also justified their deprivations by claiming Allah was stingy. "ید اللّه مغلولة" (The Hand of Allah is tied up).

3- The response to a doubt must be stronger than the doubt itself. "ید اللّه مغلولة، یداه مبسوطتان" (The Hand of Allah is tied up; rather, both His Hands are outstretched). (They said: The Hand of Allah is tied up. The Ayah says: Rather, both Hands of Allah are open, meaning He possesses perfect power.)

4- Reprimand must be commensurate with the action. "ید اللّه مغلولة، غلّت أیدیهم" (The Hand of Allah is tied up; their hands are tied up).

5- Just as the Quran is light and guidance for the pious, it brings transgression and disbelief for the obstinate. "ولیزیدنّ... طغیاناً و كفرا" (And it increases... in transgression and disbelief).

6- Transgression and disbelief pave the way for enmity and malice. "وألقینا بینهم العداوة..." (And We cast among them enmity...).

7- The Jews constantly stir up mischief, but they are defeated. "كلّما أوقدوا ناراً للحرب أطفأها اللَّه" (Every time they kindled a fire for war, Allah extinguished it).

8- Both the casting of malice and enmity as a punishment is from Allah, and the extinguishing of the fire of war is from Him. "ألقینا" (We cast), "أطفأها اللَّه" (Allah extinguished it).

9- Attaining Divine love or wrath is a result of our actions. "واللَّه لایحبّ المفسدین" (And Allah does not love the mischief-makers).

140) Al-Hijr, 39.

Ayah

وَلَوْ أَنَّ أَهْلَ ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَٱتَّقَوْا۟ لَكَفَّرْنَا عَنْهُمْ سَيِّـَٔاتِهِمْ وَلَأَدْخَلْنَـٰهُمْ جَنَّـٰتِ ٱلنَّعِيمِ (٦٥)
Had the People of the Book believed and been Godwary, We would surely have absolved them of their misdeeds and admitted them into gardens of bliss. (65)

Messages

1- The People of the Book during the era of the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله were people without faith and without piety. «و لو انّ اهل الكتاب آمنوا و اتّقوا»

2- Faith and piety are the expiation for deviations and erase previous matters. «لكفّرنا» (It is stated in a Hadith: «الاسلام یَجُبّ ماقبله») [141]

3- Faith without piety is ineffective. «آمنوا واتّقوا»

4- To receive divine favors, one must first be purified from sins. «كفّرنا، أدخلنا»

5 - Allah, in addition to forgiveness, also grants favor. «كفّرنا، ادخلنا»

141) Bihar, Vol. 6, p. 22.

Ayah

وَلَوْ أَنَّهُمْ أَقَامُوا۟ ٱلتَّوْرَىٰةَ وَٱلْإِنجِيلَ وَمَآ أُنزِلَ إِلَيْهِم مِّن رَّبِّهِمْ لَأَكَلُوا۟ مِن فَوْقِهِمْ وَمِن تَحْتِ أَرْجُلِهِم ۚ مِّنْهُمْ أُمَّةٌ مُّقْتَصِدَةٌ ۖ وَكَثِيرٌ مِّنْهُمْ سَآءَ مَا يَعْمَلُونَ (٦٦)
Had they observed the Torah and the Evangel, and what was sent down to them from their Lord, they would surely have drawn nourishment from above them and from beneath their feet. There is an upright group among them, but evil is what many of them do. (66)

Key Points

In the previous Ayah, the role of faith in spiritual and otherworldly (Akhira) prosperity was discussed; in this Ayah, its role in worldly prosperity (Dunya) and economic welfare is stated. If the followers of other heavenly books had submitted to the Quran, and had not considered submission to the Quran as the submission of the Israelite race to the Arab race, and had known that the principles of the teachings of the Prophets are one, and that believing in what was revealed after the Torah and the Gospel is finding a path to a higher class, not invalidating previous commands—in that case, besides the otherworldly benefits on the Day of Judgment, various blessings would have poured forth upon them in this world as well. In the previous Ayah, the belief of the Jews regarding the tying up of God's hand was discussed. In this Ayah, He says: You should turn to the heavenly books, and then judge whether the hand of God is tied up or not. If your greatness has turned into humiliation, it is because of your disbelief and turning away from the heavenly commands, not because of the inability or the tied hand of Allah. Ayah 96 of Surah Al-A'raf is also similar to this Ayah: «و لو انّ اهل القُرى آمنوا و اتّقوا لفتحنا علیهم بركات من السماء و الارض» (And if the people of the towns had believed and feared Allah, We would have opened upon them blessings from the heaven and the earth.)

Question: Does upholding the heavenly books cause the expansion of sustenance (Rizq)? Answer: Either Allah expands the sustenance as a reward, or the natural ways of expanding sustenance are mentioned within the heavenly books themselves, which they will attain if they uphold the heavenly book. The Noble Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله said: The Jews will divide into 71 sects and the Christians into 72 sects, of which only one sect will be in Paradise. Whenever Hadrat Ali عليه السلام narrated this Hadith from the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله, he would also recite this Ayah. [142]

142) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

  1. The People of the Book do not believe in all the heavenly books. «و لو انّهم» (The word law [لو] indicates impossibility/refusal.)

  2. The heavenly books (unadulterated) are the constitution, the axis, and the guide for all movements, and must always be upheld like a banner. «اقاموا»

  3. When inviting others to Islam, we should not harm their correct beliefs and sanctities. «اقاموا التوراة ...»

  4. Merely reciting the heavenly book is not enough; upholding (establishing) it is also necessary. «أقاموا التوراة و الانجیل»

  5. All heavenly books are respected. «اقاموا التوراة والانجیل و ما انزل الیهم»

  6. The Quran is for all nations (Ummahs), not just Muslims. «ما انزل الیهم من ربّهم»

  7. Islam also pays attention to the welfare of the people. «لأكلوا»

  8. Attention to religious commands also organizes and improves material life. «لو انّهم... لأكلوا...»

  9. In the cosmic order, the faith of individuals plays a role in the fertility of the earth, the rainfall, and the growth of agriculture and produce. [143] «اقاموا التوراة... لاكلوا»

  10. A corrupt society does not compel a person to corruption. Among the multitude of evildoers, there is also a moderate group. «منهم اُمّة مقتصدة»

  11. Moderation is necessary in upholding the heavenly books, not extremism or negligence. «مقتصدة»

  12. Never condemn all individuals of a group, race, region, or religion. «منهم اُمّة مقتصدة»

  13. Population size is not a sign of righteousness. «كثیر منهم ساء مایعملون»

143) Tafsir Al-Mizan.

Ayah

۞ يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلرَّسُولُ بَلِّغْ مَآ أُنزِلَ إِلَيْكَ مِن رَّبِّكَ ۖ وَإِن لَّمْ تَفْعَلْ فَمَا بَلَّغْتَ رِسَالَتَهُۥ ۚ وَٱللَّهُ يَعْصِمُكَ مِنَ ٱلنَّاسِ ۗ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَا يَهْدِى ٱلْقَوْمَ ٱلْكَـٰفِرِينَ (٦۷)
O Apostle! Communicate that which has been sent down to you from your Lord, and if you do not, you will not have communicated His message, and Allah shall protect you from the people. Indeed Allah does not guide the faithless lot. (67)

Key Points

This ayah, for reasons that will follow, relates to the succession of Hadrat Ali (عليه السلام) and his appointment to Wilayah (guardianship/leadership) at Ghadir Khumm upon the return of the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله) from the Hajj pilgrimage in the tenth year of the Hijra. It is interesting that this ayah is placed between two verses related to the establishment of the Divine Book, perhaps indicating a strong link between the infallible leader (Imam) and the establishment of the Divine Book. Yes, it is the Imam who can uphold the Divine Book, and it is the Book of God that can introduce the true Imam. All Shia commentators, relying on the narrations of the Ahl al-Bayt (عليهم السلام), and also some Sunni commentators, have considered the verse, as one of its primary instances, to be related to the appointment of Hadrat Ali (عليه السلام) to Wilayah and Imamate at Ghadir Khumm. [144] It is interesting that the author of Tafsir Al-Manar cited the hadith, «مَن كنتُ مولاه فعلىّ مولاه» (Whoever I am his Mawla, Ali is his Mawla), with a sound and reliable chain of transmission from Musnad Ahmad, Tirmidhi, Nasa'i, and Ibn Majah. He then, in his own estimation, cast doubt upon it with two statements: First, that the meaning of wilayah is friendship. (He is oblivious that friendship did not require taking an oath of allegiance, offering congratulations, and all those ceremonies at Ghadir Khumm). Second, that if Hadrat Ali (عليه السلام) was the Imam and leader after the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله), why did he not cry out and mention the event of his appointment at Ghadir? (He is oblivious that throughout the Nahj al-Balaghah and the words of Hadrat Ali (عليه السلام), lamentations and cries are mentioned, but the author of Al-Manar apparently did not hear those lamentations and cries.) [145]

In the entire Quran, only in this verse is the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله) threatened regarding the concealment of a message, stating that if He does not convey it, everything He has said during the 23 years of His mission will be rendered void. So, one must see what important message is being conveyed in this manner? The Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله) said to Hadrat Ali (عليه السلام): «لو لم ابلغ ما اُمرت به من ولایتك لحبط عملى» (If I do not convey what I have been commanded regarding your Wilayah, my entire work will be nullified.) [146]

There are several points in this verse that clarify its contextual orientation: 1. Surah Al-Ma'idah was revealed late in the Prophet's noble life. 2. The address «یا أیّها الرّسول» (O Messenger) is used instead of «یا أیّها النّبى» (O Prophet), which signifies an important mission. 3. The command «بَلّغ» (Convey/Deliver) is used instead of «أبلغ» (Inform), which signifies a definitive, formal, and important conveyance. 4. The Prophet is threatened regarding the non-delivery of an important message, such that if He does not convey it, all His efforts will be wasted. 5. The Messenger of God (صلى الله عليه وآله) fears the consequences of an action, and God reassures Him that We will protect You from the people. 6. The Messenger of Allah does not fear for His life. Because in the days of solitude when He fought idols, and in the wars where He engaged militarily with the polytheists, He did not fear dangers. (While He was stoned and His companions were tortured, should He now fear in the last years of His life and among so many companions?!) 7. The verse contains a message that, due to its importance, is equivalent to all the messages of the period of prophethood and messengership; if this message is not conveyed to the people, it is as if all messages are nullified. 8. The content of the message must be a fundamental issue; otherwise, such extensive threat and reassurance are unnecessary for minor or individual matters. 9. The message of the verse is not related to Monotheism (Tawhid), Prophethood (Nubuwwah), or the Resurrection (Ma'ad), because these principles were stated in Mecca during the first days of the mission and do not require such extensive emphasis late in His life. 10. The message of the verse is also not related to prayer (Salat), fasting (Sawm), Hajj, Zakat, Khums, or Jihad, because these were explained throughout the 23 years of the Prophet's call, and the people acted upon them, and there was no fear involved.

So, what is the content of this important message that was revealed late in the Prophet's noble life (صلى الله عليه وآله)? Numerous narrations from both Shia and Sunni sources save us from confusion and show the way. The narrations state: The verse relates to the eighteenth of Dhu al-Hijjah in the tenth year of the Hijra, during the Farewell Pilgrimage (Hajjat al-Wida') of the Prophet of Islam. Upon returning to Medina, the Prophet, by Divine command, ordered a halt at a place called "Ghadir Khumm." Everyone gathered in this area—a place that had water and trees, which was helpful in the heat of the Hijaz, and was also the point where the caravans of the pilgrims separated (people of Yemen, Iraq, Sham, Medina, and Abyssinia separated there). There, the Messenger of God, among the multitude of companions, stood atop a pulpit made of camel saddles and delivered a long sermon. The beginning of the sermon covered Tawhid, Nubuwwah, and Ma'ad, which was not new. The new point began when the Prophet announced His impending departure (death) and asked the Muslims for their opinion regarding Him. Everyone affirmed His utmost dignity, greatness, service, and mission. When He was certain that His voice reached all the people in the four directions, He conveyed His important message regarding the future. He said: «من كنت مولاه فعلىّ مولاه» (Whoever I am his Mawla, this Ali is his Mawla), and thereby explicitly announced the succession of Hadrat Ali (عليه السلام) after Him.

But after His passing, when Hadrat Zahra (عليها السلام) went to the doors of the people's houses and asked, "Were you not there, and did you not hear what the Messenger of God said at Ghadir Khumm?" They replied, "We were far away at Ghadir Khumm and did not hear the Prophet's voice!!" Allahu Akbar (God is Greater) concerning concealment, fear, disloyalty, and lying to the daughter of the Prophet of God. Yes, people obtain their rights with two witnesses, but Hadrat Ali (عليه السلام), despite tens of thousands of witnesses, could not obtain His right. Alas for the love of the world, jealousy, and the grudges from Badr, Khaybar, and Hunayn that they held against Hadrat Ali (عليه السلام).

Imam Baqir (عليه السلام) said: «بُنى الاسلام على خمس على الصلاة و الزّكاة و الصوم و الحج و الولایة و لم یناد بشى ء كما نودى بالولایة فاخذ النّاس باربع و تركوا هذه» (Islam is founded upon five things: upon prayer, Zakat, fasting, Hajj, and Wilayah (guardianship) of the Ahl al-Bayt. Nothing was called for as much as Wilayah, but the people accepted four of them and abandoned this one.) [147]

Imam Reza (عليه السلام) said: After the revelation of this verse, where God guaranteed the protection of the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله علیه وآله), «واللَّه یعصمك من النّاس» (And Allah will protect you from the people), the Prophet abandoned all forms of dissimulation (taqiyyah). [148]

144) Tafasir Kabir Fakhr Razi and Al-Manar. 145) I wrote these lines on the night of the martyrdom of Hadrat Ali (عليه السلام), midnight of 21st of Ramadan 1380. 146) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn and Amali Saduq, p. 400. 147) Kafi, Vol. 2, p. 18. 148) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn and Uyun Akhbar al-Ridha, Vol. 2, p. 130.

Messages

  1. The type of address must be consistent with the type of goal. Since the goal is messengership and conveying the message, the address is «یا أیّها الرّسول» (O Messenger).

  2. The selection of the Islamic leader (Infallible Imam) must be from God. «بلّغ ما انزل الیك من ربّك» (Convey what has been sent down to you from your Lord).

  3. Sometimes the conveyance of the Divine message must be in the presence of the people, in public ceremonies, and by taking an oath of allegiance; conveying Wilayah is not sufficient merely through speech, but must be through action. «و ان لم تفعل» (And if you do not do so) instead of "if you do not convey."

  4. Divine rulings and messages are not all on the same level. Sometimes concealing one truth is equivalent to concealing all truths. «و ان لم تفعل فما بلّغتَ رسالته» (And if you do not do so, then you have not conveyed His message).

  5. If the leadership is not correct, the school (of thought) will be destroyed and the Ummah will be misguided. «فما بلّغتَ رسالته» (Then you have not conveyed His message).

  6. The main pillar of Islam is Imamate and governance. «وان لم تفعل فما بلّغتَ رسالته» (And if you do not do so, then you have not conveyed His message).

  7. Denial of Wilayah is a type of disbelief (kufr). «انّ اللّه لایهدى القوم الكافرین» (Indeed, Allah does not guide the disbelieving people).

  8. The elements of time and place are two important principles in propagation. (Considering that this verse was revealed on the eighteenth of Dhu al-Hijjah and at the location where the Hajj caravans separated).

Ayah

قُلْ يَـٰٓأَهْلَ ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ لَسْتُمْ عَلَىٰ شَىْءٍ حَتَّىٰ تُقِيمُوا۟ ٱلتَّوْرَىٰةَ وَٱلْإِنجِيلَ وَمَآ أُنزِلَ إِلَيْكُم مِّن رَّبِّكُمْ ۗ وَلَيَزِيدَنَّ كَثِيرًا مِّنْهُم مَّآ أُنزِلَ إِلَيْكَ مِن رَّبِّكَ طُغْيَـٰنًا وَكُفْرًا ۖ فَلَا تَأْسَ عَلَى ٱلْقَوْمِ ٱلْكَـٰفِرِينَ (٦۸)
Say, ‘O People of the Book! You do not stand on anything until you observe the Torah and the Evangel and what was sent down to you from your Lord.’ Surely many of them will be increased in rebellion and unfaith by what has been sent down to you from your Lord. So do not grieve for the faithless lot. (68)

Key Points

Although the heavenly Book, the Quran, is a means of guidance, yet due to a spirit of obstinacy and enmity, the more the Quran is recited to a group, the more their inner impurity becomes manifest. Imam Baqir عليه السلام said regarding «لیزیدنّ كثیراً منهم...» (it will surely increase many of them...): That which causes the transgression (tughyan) and disbelief (kufr) of many is the Wilayah (guardianship) of Amir al-Mu'minin, Hadrat Ali عليه السلام. [149]

149) Tafsir Ayyash.

Messages

1- The People of the Book must believe in all heavenly scriptures. «تقیموا التوراة والانجیل و ما انزل الیكم»

2- The mere claim of faith is not enough; practical establishment and action are necessary. «حتّى تقیموا»

3- Heavenly laws must be the axis of action and must sit upon the seat of power. «تقیموا»

4- The personality and worth of individuals depend on the extent of their religious commitments. «لستم على شى ء حتّى تقیموا»

5- In propagation, we should first respect the correct beliefs of others, and then show our own path. «تقیموا التوراة والانجیل وما انزل الیكم من ربّكم...»

6- Transgression (tughyan) is the ground and prelude to disbelief (kufr). «طغیاناً و كفراً»

7- Transgression and disbelief are capable of increase and decrease. «لیزیدنّ...»

8- A small group among the People of the Book are on the correct path. «لیزیدنّ كثیراً منهم»

9- It is not permissible to grieve over obstinate individuals and disbelievers. «لیزیدنّ...فلا تأس على القوم الكافرین»

Ayah

إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَٱلَّذِينَ هَادُوا۟ وَٱلصَّـٰبِـُٔونَ وَٱلنَّصَـٰرَىٰ مَنْ ءَامَنَ بِٱللَّهِ وَٱلْيَوْمِ ٱلْـَٔاخِرِ وَعَمِلَ صَـٰلِحًا فَلَا خَوْفٌ عَلَيْهِمْ وَلَا هُمْ يَحْزَنُونَ (٦۹)
Indeed the faithful, the Jews, the Sabaeans, and the Christians—those who have faith in Allah and the Last Day and act righteously—they will have no fear, nor will they grieve. (69)

Key Points

The word "Sābi'īn" (Sabeans), which appears three times in the Qur'an alongside the Jews and Christians, refers to those who followed one of the heavenly religions but gradually developed deviations, including the belief in the influence of stars, withdrawal from social interaction, and having specific rituals in life. Followers of different religions must believe in the new religion after its arrival; otherwise, the mission (Bi'that) of subsequent prophets would be futile. If the Jews, Christians, and Sabeans—like the Muslims—believe in the Prophet of Islam and perform righteous deeds (عمل صالح), they will have no fear or sorrow. Among all past religions, the names of the Jews, Christians, and Sabeans are mentioned because they are Divine religions and are more deserving than others to accept the call of truth.

Messages

1- Among the common principles of all heavenly religions are belief in Allah, the Day of Judgment (Qiyāmah), and the mission (Risālah) of the prophets. «مَن آمن باللّه ...»

2- The criterion for happiness (Sa'ādah) in all heavenly religions is faith and righteous deeds (عمل صالح), not mere claims or titles. «مَن آمن باللّه و الیوم الاخر...»

3- Faith is not separate from action. «آمن ... وعمل»

4- True tranquility is found under the shade of faith and righteous deeds. «مَن آمن... و عمل صالحا فلاخوف علیهم و لا هم یحزنون»

Ayah

لَقَدْ أَخَذْنَا مِيثَـٰقَ بَنِىٓ إِسْرَٰٓءِيلَ وَأَرْسَلْنَآ إِلَيْهِمْ رُسُلًا ۖ كُلَّمَا جَآءَهُمْ رَسُولٌۢ بِمَا لَا تَهْوَىٰٓ أَنفُسُهُمْ فَرِيقًا كَذَّبُوا۟ وَفَرِيقًا يَقْتُلُونَ (۷٠)
Certainly We took a pledge from the Children of Israel, and We sent apostles to them. Whenever an apostle brought them that which was not to their liking, they would impugn a part of them, and a part they would slay. (70)

Key Points

Perhaps the intent of the covenant of the Children of Israel is the same content as the previous Ayah: faith in Allah, the Resurrection, and righteous deeds. Or perhaps the intent is following the prophets who are mentioned in this very Ayah with the word "رُسلاً" (messengers). Or perhaps it is the same covenant that was mentioned in Ayahs 93 of Surah Al-Baqarah and 81 of Surah Aal-Imran—that is, acting upon what Allah had revealed to them. In the previous Ayah, we said that mere affiliation with heavenly religions is not enough for salvation from Allah's wrath; rather, faith and righteous deeds are necessary. This Ayah serves as an example for the previous Ayah, showing what the Children of Israel did, despite being affiliated with a heavenly religion! The fact that this Surah begins with the phrase «اوفوا بالعقود» (Fulfill the contracts), and the event of Ghadir is mentioned in Ayah 67, and in this Ayah, covenant-breaking, the killing of Allah's saints, and their denial are mentioned, can, while exposing the conditions of the Children of Israel, serve as a lesson and a warning bell for Muslims so that they do not ignore the message and covenant of Ghadir Khumm.

Messages

1- The Children of Israel, besides Prophet Moses, also had other prophets. «ارسلنا الیهم رسلاً»

2- The path of the prophets does not conform to carnal desires. «جاءهم رسول بما لاتهوى انفسهم»

3- The root of denying and killing the prophets is the carnal desire of the Children of Israel. «بما لا تهوى انفسهم فریقاً كذّبوا»

4- Covenant-breaking, prophet-killing, denial, and obstinacy are among the characteristics of the Children of Israel. «فریقاً كذّبوا و فریقاً یقتلون»

5- In a corrupt society, either the character of the men of God is assassinated (denial), or their person is assassinated (killing). «فریقاً كذّبوا و فریقاً یقتلون»

6- The prophets went forward in the way of Allah even to the point of death. «فریقاً یقتلون»

Ayah

وَحَسِبُوٓا۟ أَلَّا تَكُونَ فِتْنَةٌ فَعَمُوا۟ وَصَمُّوا۟ ثُمَّ تَابَ ٱللَّهُ عَلَيْهِمْ ثُمَّ عَمُوا۟ وَصَمُّوا۟ كَثِيرٌ مِّنْهُمْ ۚ وَٱللَّهُ بَصِيرٌۢ بِمَا يَعْمَلُونَ (۷۱)
They supposed there would be no testing, so they became blind and deaf. Thereafter Allah accepted their repentance, yet [again] many of them became blind and deaf, and Allah watches what they do. (71)

Key Points

The word fitnah (فتنة) in the Ayah means either trial/test or punishment. The Children of Israel imagined that the divine trials, wrath, or punishment were related only to the era of Prophet Moses (عليه السلام) and did not include them. Therefore, they became preoccupied with material life, seeking luxury, and indifference towards the divine signs (Ayat). Imam Sadiq (عليه السلام) said regarding «وحسبوا ان لا تكون فتنة»: The intent is the time when the Prophet was not among them. «فعموا و صمّوا» is the time when the Prophet passed away. «ثمّ تاب اللّه» is the time when Amir al-Mu'minin (عليه السلام) attained the Caliphate. And «ثمّ عموا و صمّوا» lasts until the Day of Judgment. [150]

150) Kafi, Vol. 8, p. 200.

Messages

1- Divine testing is universal; let us never be heedless of the trials (fitan). «حسبوا ألاّتكون فتنة»

2- Beliefs should not be based on conjecture and speculation. «حَسبوا...»

3- Being on the straight path for a period is not important; what is important is a good outcome (Hasan Aqibat). «ثمّ تاب اللَّه علیهم ثمّ عموا وصمّوا» «ثُمّ عموا» indicates that the Children of Israel were on the correct path for a while after Allah's grace returned, but they deviated again.

4- Arrogance, self-superiority, conjecture, and illusion make a person blind and deaf, depriving him of correct recognition. «و حسبوا... فعموا و صمّوا»

5- No one should expect to achieve anything without being tested. «حسبوا ألا تكون فتنة»

6- Divine grace is such that it flows towards man even without him offering an apology. «فعموا و صمّوا ثمّ تاب اللّه»

7- Allah is Merciful, but humans are obstinate. «تاب اللّه علیهم ثم عموا»

8- The multitude of followers is not a sign of the path's truthfulness. «ثم عموا و صمّوا كثیر منهم»

9- Belief in Allah's supervision plays an important role in human life. «واللَّه بصیر»

Ayah

لَقَدْ كَفَرَ ٱلَّذِينَ قَالُوٓا۟ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ هُوَ ٱلْمَسِيحُ ٱبْنُ مَرْيَمَ ۖ وَقَالَ ٱلْمَسِيحُ يَـٰبَنِىٓ إِسْرَٰٓءِيلَ ٱعْبُدُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ رَبِّى وَرَبَّكُمْ ۖ إِنَّهُۥ مَن يُشْرِكْ بِٱللَّهِ فَقَدْ حَرَّمَ ٱللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ ٱلْجَنَّةَ وَمَأْوَىٰهُ ٱلنَّارُ ۖ وَمَا لِلظَّـٰلِمِينَ مِنْ أَنصَارٍ (۷۲)
They are certainly faithless who say, ‘Allah is the Messiah, son of Mary.’ But the Messiah had said, ‘O Children of Israel! Worship Allah, my Lord, and your Lord. Indeed whoever ascribes partners to Allah, Allah shall forbid him [entry into] paradise, and his refuge shall be the Fire, and the wrongdoers will not have any helpers.’ (72)

Key Points

It is stated in Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan: The Jacobite sect of Christians says: God united with Christ in essence and they became one thing. This Ayah expresses the belief of some Christians who consider God to be Christ Himself, and the next Ayah quotes the belief of another group who have accepted the Trinity. It is worth noting that in the Gospel of Mark (Chapter 12, Verse 29), Jesus عليه السلام invited people to Monotheism and said: Our God is the One Lord. It is also stated in the Gospel of Matthew (Chapter 6, Verse 24): It is impossible for a person to have two masters and two beloveds. To serve both God and another. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام, in explaining the major sins (al-Kaba'ir), said: The greatest of the major sins is associating partners with Allah (Shirk), and then He recited this Ayah. [151]

151) Bihar, Vol. 79, p. 6; Uyun Akhbar al-Ridha, Vol. 1, p. 285.

Messages

1- Firmness must be shown against major deviations. «لقد كفر»

2- The belief in the indwelling (Hulul) of Allah in individuals, even in the best of humans, is disbelief (Kufr). «لقد كفر»

3- How can someone who was born of a mother be God? «مسیح ابن مریم»

4- We must not exaggerate (Ghuluww) concerning the friends of Allah, the pure, and the righteous. Jesus Himself invited people to the worship of Allah. «وقال المسیح ...»

5- The polytheist (Mushrik) will never enter Paradise. «فقد حرّم اللّه ...»

6- Those who consider Jesus to be God are disbelievers (Kafir), polytheists (Mushrik), wrongdoers (Zalim), and deprived of Paradise. «لقد كفر... و من یشرك... مأواه النار... ما للظالمین من انصار»

7- Associating partners with Allah (Shirk) is wrongdoing (Dhulm). «للظالمین»

8- Intercession (Shafa'ah) exists on the Day of Judgment, but not for the polytheists. «ماللظالمین من انصار»

Ayah

لَّقَدْ كَفَرَ ٱلَّذِينَ قَالُوٓا۟ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ ثَالِثُ ثَلَـٰثَةٍ ۘ وَمَا مِنْ إِلَـٰهٍ إِلَّآ إِلَـٰهٌ وَٰحِدٌ ۚ وَإِن لَّمْ يَنتَهُوا۟ عَمَّا يَقُولُونَ لَيَمَسَّنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ مِنْهُمْ عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌ (۷۳)
They are certainly faithless who say, ‘Allah is the third [person] of a trinity,’ while there is no god except the One God. If they do not relinquish what they say, there shall befall the faithless among them a painful punishment. (73)

Key Points

The previous Ayah rejected the deviated belief in the incarnation of Allah in Christ. This Ayah explains the deviation of the belief in the Trinity, because both are polytheism (Shirk) and must be fought against. In the belief of the Trinity, God, Christ, and the Holy Spirit (Father, Son, and Holy Spirit) are all considered God. However, recently a group of Christian scholars have said: the belief in the Trinity lacks scientific validity. (152)

152) Tafsir al-Mizan.

Messages

1- Islam is not indifferent toward the deviation of the followers of other heavenly religions. «لقد كفر الّذین قالوا...»

2- Polytheism and multiple gods (the Trinity) is disbelief (Kufr). «لقد كفر»

3- Not all those who believed in the Trinity will be afflicted by Divine punishment. «كفروا منهم» (It can be said: those who remained upon the belief in the Trinity and did not return to Monotheism after the invitation of the Quran will be in punishment.)

4- Warning is necessary before punishment. «وان لم ینتهوا»

5- The consequence of disbelief (Kufr) and polytheism (Shirk) is punishment. «عذاب الیم»

Ayah

أَفَلَا يَتُوبُونَ إِلَى ٱللَّهِ وَيَسْتَغْفِرُونَهُۥ ۚ وَٱللَّهُ غَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ (۷٤)
Will they not repent to Allah and plead to Him for forgiveness? Yet Allah is all-forgiving, all-merciful. (74)

Messages

1- One must repent from deviant beliefs. «أفلا یتوبون»

2- We should invite the deviants to the correct path by stating Allah's pardon and mercy. «واللّه غفور رحیم»

3- Even disbelief (kufr) and polytheism (shirk) are forgiven through repentance and belief in Monotheism (Tawhid). «لقد كفر... واللَّه غفور رحیم»

4- Allah both forgives the past and grants mercy. «غفور رحیم»

5- Alongside the punishment mentioned in the previous Ayah, mercy is presented for those who repent. «ان لم ینتهوا... عذاب الیم... واللَّه غفور رحیم»

Ayah

مَّا ٱلْمَسِيحُ ٱبْنُ مَرْيَمَ إِلَّا رَسُولٌ قَدْ خَلَتْ مِن قَبْلِهِ ٱلرُّسُلُ وَأُمُّهُۥ صِدِّيقَةٌ ۖ كَانَا يَأْكُلَانِ ٱلطَّعَامَ ۗ ٱنظُرْ كَيْفَ نُبَيِّنُ لَهُمُ ٱلْـَٔايَـٰتِ ثُمَّ ٱنظُرْ أَنَّىٰ يُؤْفَكُونَ (۷٥)
The Messiah, son of Mary, is but an apostle. Certainly, [other] apostles have passed before him, and his mother was a truthful one. Both of them would eat food. Look how We clarify the signs for them, and yet, look, how they go astray! (75)

Key Points

In this Ayah, Allah presents three reasons why Jesus (عليه السلام) is not God: 1- He was born of a mother and is the son of Mary. 2- Prophets like him have also existed, and he is not unique. 3- Like others, he needed food and derived His strength from a morsel of bread; therefore, he does not possess inherent power to be God. Indeed, one who needs food cannot be the Creator. These are the clear, universal, and easily understood statements of the Quran.

Siddīq (صدّیق) is someone who is extremely truthful and proves his truthfulness through his righteous deeds. In another Ayah, the quality of being a Siddīqah (صدّیقه) for Lady Mary is described thus: that she affirmed the divine words and was among the devout worshippers. «صدقت بكلمات ربّها و كتبه و كانت من القانتین» (153)

153) التحریم، 12.

Messages

1- Possessing certain distinctions and exceptional matters is not proof of divinity. Adam (عليه السلام) was also without a father and mother, yet no one considered him God. «قد خلت من قبله الرسل»

2- Mary is among the friends (Awliyā') of Allah. The Quran honors her and considers her a Siddīqah. «امّه صدّیقة»

3- Death is certain for all Prophets. «قد خلت من قبله الرسل» Therefore, Jesus is mortal and is not God.

4- If obstinacy intervenes, even the clearest proofs and arguments will be ineffective. «كیف نبیّن ... أنّى یؤفكون»

5- A woman can reach the highest spiritual ranks, to the extent that Allah praises her. «و اُمّه صدّیقة»

6- Familiarity with past history and tracing its roots is beneficial for future generations. «ثمّ انظر...»

Ayah

قُلْ أَتَعْبُدُونَ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ مَا لَا يَمْلِكُ لَكُمْ ضَرًّا وَلَا نَفْعًا ۚ وَٱللَّهُ هُوَ ٱلسَّمِيعُ ٱلْعَلِيمُ (۷٦)
Say, ‘Do you worship, besides Allah, what has no power to bring you any benefit or harm, while Allah—He is the All-hearing, the All-knowing?!’ (76)

Key Points

In this Ayah, a group of Christians are rebuked by Allah due to their polytheism (Shirk) and exaggeration (Ghuluw) regarding Jesus عليه السلام. "قل أتعبدون من دون اللَّه..."

Messages

1- Regarding the invalidity of the path of polytheism (Shirk), refer to your own intellect and conscience. "أتعبدون"

2- The axis and root of worship is attracting benefit or repelling harm, and none other than Allah can repel harm or attract benefit. "لا یملك لكم ضراً و لانفعاً..."

3- Only Allah is the Hearer of requests and the Knower of the profit and loss of human beings, not other deities. "و اللَّه هو السمیع العلیم"

Ayah

قُلْ يَـٰٓأَهْلَ ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ لَا تَغْلُوا۟ فِى دِينِكُمْ غَيْرَ ٱلْحَقِّ وَلَا تَتَّبِعُوٓا۟ أَهْوَآءَ قَوْمٍ قَدْ ضَلُّوا۟ مِن قَبْلُ وَأَضَلُّوا۟ كَثِيرًا وَضَلُّوا۟ عَن سَوَآءِ ٱلسَّبِيلِ (۷۷)
Say, ‘O People of the Book! Do not unduly exceed the bounds in your religion and do not follow the fancies of a people who went astray in the past, and led many astray, and [themselves] strayed from the right path.’ (77)

Key Points

Perhaps it can be inferred from this Ayah that considering Christ as God is a form of exaggeration (ghuluww) derived from the polytheistic ideas of previous communities. As we read in Ayah 30 of Surah At-Tawbah: «و قالت الیهود عُزیرٌ ابن اللّه و قالت النّصارى المسیح ابن اللّه ذلك قولهم بافواههم یضاهؤن قول الّذین كفروا من قبل» The Jews said: Uzayr is the son of Allah, and the Christians said: The Messiah is the son of Allah. This exaggeration (ghuluww) and baseless talk resembles the statements of the disbelievers who came before.

Messages

1- In all religions, intellectual and doctrinal boundaries must be maintained, and exaggeration (ghuluww) regarding personalities is forbidden. «یا اهل الكتاب لاتغلوا»

2- Exaggeration (ghuluww) regarding divine leaders is exaggeration in religion. (Considering the previous verses where they considered Christ as God) «لاتغلوا فى دینكم»

3- If exaggeration (ghuluww) is forbidden in the religion of Allah and concerning the divine saints (Awliya), then exaggeration regarding others is also impermissible. «لاتغلوا... غیر الحقّ»

4- Blind imitation is forbidden. «ولاتتّبعوا اهواء قوم»

5- Exaggeration (ghuluww) was not only about Jesus عليه السلام. Some Jews also exaggerated concerning 'Uzayr, considering him the son of Allah. «ضلوا من قبل»

6- Ancient sciences and arts are valuable, but the old thoughts and beliefs of ancestors, which are based on whims and lack foundation, are worthless and should not be followed. «ولاتتّبعوا....ضلوا من قبل»

7- Misguidance has stages: first, a person becomes misguided himself, «ضلّوا» then he misguides others, «اضلّوا» and gradually he is permanently prevented from the true guidance. «ضلّوا... اضلّوا... ضلّوا»

Ayah

لُعِنَ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ مِنۢ بَنِىٓ إِسْرَٰٓءِيلَ عَلَىٰ لِسَانِ دَاوُۥدَ وَعِيسَى ٱبْنِ مَرْيَمَ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ بِمَا عَصَوا۟ وَّكَانُوا۟ يَعْتَدُونَ (۷۸)
The faithless among the Children of Israel were cursed on the tongue of David and Jesus son of Mary. That, because they would disobey and they used to commit transgression. (78)

Key Points

Prophet David (علیه السلام) cursed the Children of Israel because they violated the command regarding the cessation of work on Saturdays. And Prophet Jesus (علیه السلام) cursed them because, after eating the heavenly table (Mā'idah) which had descended through his prayer and for their reassurance, the Children of Israel still committed disbelief. [154] Imam Baqir (علیه السلام) said: The curse of Prophet David turned them into pigs, and the curse of Prophet Jesus turned them into monkeys. [155]

154) Tafsir Namūneh. 155) Tafsir Nūr al-Thaqalayn and Kāfī, Vol. 8, p. 200.

Messages

1- A group of the Children of Israel were subjected to the curse of their own Prophets. «لعن الّذین كفروا»

2- Disobedience and transgression lead man to disbelief. «كفروا... بما عصوا و كانوا یعتدون»

3- Prophets are not always intercessors; sometimes they curse. «لُعن... على لسان داود و...»

4- Sin and transgression result in the curse of the Prophets. «لعن... بما عصوا و كانوا یعتدون»

5- Transgression and law-breaking are the characteristic methods of the Children of Israel. «كانوا یعتدون»

Ayah

كَانُوا۟ لَا يَتَنَاهَوْنَ عَن مُّنكَرٍ فَعَلُوهُ ۚ لَبِئْسَ مَا كَانُوا۟ يَفْعَلُونَ (۷۹)
They would not forbid one another from the wrongs that they committed. Surely, evil is what they had been doing. (79)

Key Points

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام says: This group of the Children of Israel whom Allah has condemned in this Ayah were those who did not enter gatherings of sin, but when encountering sinners, they treated them with smiles and familiarity. [156] In another narration from Hadrat Ali عليه السلام, it is stated: Because the Children of Israel saw evil committed by the oppressors but did not forbid the evil due to the benefits they received from them (so that their interests would not be jeopardized or danger would not befall them), Allah mentioned them negatively. [157]

156) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 157) Wasā'il, Vol. 16, p. 130.

Messages

1- Whoever is indifferent towards corruption is cursed by the prophets. «لعن ...لایتناهون»

2- Forbidding evil (Nahy 'an al-Munkar) is not exclusive to the religion of Islam. «كانوا لایتناهون»

3- Forbidding evil is a universal and public duty. «لایتناهون»

4- Corruption was widespread in the society of the Children of Israel and the Jews. «منكر فعلوه»

5- Abandoning enjoining good (Amr bi al-Ma'ruf) is a precursor to disbelief (Kufr). «لُعن الّذین كفروا... كانوا لایتناهون...»

Ayah

تَرَىٰ كَثِيرًا مِّنْهُمْ يَتَوَلَّوْنَ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ ۚ لَبِئْسَ مَا قَدَّمَتْ لَهُمْ أَنفُسُهُمْ أَن سَخِطَ ٱللَّهُ عَلَيْهِمْ وَفِى ٱلْعَذَابِ هُمْ خَـٰلِدُونَ (۸٠)
You see many of them fraternizing with the faithless. Surely evil is what they have sent ahead for their souls, as Allah is displeased with them and they shall remain in punishment [forever]. (80)

Key Points

| In this Ayah, another reason for the cursing of the Children of Israel is stated: that they openly and permanently established friendship with the disbelievers. (The word "تَرى" indicates openness, and the word "یتولّون" indicates permanence.) Imam Baqir عليه السلام, explaining this Ayah, said: This group consisted of those who: «یتولّون الملوك الجبّارین و یزیّنون لهم اهوائهم لیصیبوا من دنیاهم» (They befriended the tyrannical kings and made their lustful desires appear beautiful to them so that they could benefit from their worldly possessions.) [158]

158) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

| 1- Friendship with disbelievers is a cause of divine wrath and anger. «یتولّون الّذین كفروا... سخط اللَّه علیهم» 2- The People of the Book accepted the dominance and friendship of the disbelievers, but they would not cooperate with the Muslims. «یتولّون الّذین كفروا» 3- Allah desires independence even for the People of the Book and condemns dependence and accepting the guardianship (wilayah) of the polytheists. «ترى كثیراً منهم یتولّون» 4- One of the significant evils that was not forbidden among the Children of Israel was the relationship of patronage (wilā’ī) with the disbelievers (and the polytheists of Mecca). «لا یتناهون عن منكر فعلوه... ترى» 5- Every sin becomes a prelude to committing greater sins. (In the previous Ayahs, three sins were mentioned: «عصوا... یعتدون... لایتناهون», and these became the prelude to accepting the dominance of the disbelievers. «ترى... یتولّون»)

Ayah

وَلَوْ كَانُوا۟ يُؤْمِنُونَ بِٱللَّهِ وَٱلنَّبِىِّ وَمَآ أُنزِلَ إِلَيْهِ مَا ٱتَّخَذُوهُمْ أَوْلِيَآءَ وَلَـٰكِنَّ كَثِيرًا مِّنْهُمْ فَـٰسِقُونَ (۸۱)
Had they believed in Allah and the Prophet and what has been sent down to him, they would not have taken them for allies. But most of them are transgressors. (81)

Key Points

| Several possibilities have been given regarding the meaning of this Ayah, including: A: If the Jews had become Muslims, they would not have taken the disbelievers as their protectors (wali). [159] B: If the Jews had believed in Moses عليه السلام and the Torah, they would never have taken the polytheists as their protectors (wali). [160] C: If the disbelievers and polytheists had become Muslims, the Jews would not have chosen them as their friends. [161] The apparent meaning of the Ayah, considering the preceding verses, strengthens the first possibility.

159) Tafsir Namuneh. 160) Tafsir Ruh al-Ma'ani. 161) Tafsir Namuneh.

Messages

| 1- The belief of the People of the Book is so remote that it resembles a mere wish. «ولو كانوا یؤمنون...» (The word law [لو] is used in cases where something is impossible, like an old man saying: If only I were young!) 2- Whoever has sincere faith in Allah and the Prophet is not willing to accept non-Divine guardianship (wilayah). «لو كانوا یؤمنون... مااتّخذوهم اولیاء» (The path to achieving true independence and freedom from the domination of the disbelievers is faith.) 3- Transgression (fisq) and lack of faith are the causes of accepting the domination of the disbelievers. «لو كانوا یؤمنون... ما اتّخذوهم اولیاء» 4- Faith is incompatible with the guardianship (wilayah) of the disbelievers. One who accepts domination is compromising, irreligious, and transgressive (fasiq). «لو كانوا یؤمنون... ولكن كثیراً منهم فاسقون» 5- Transgression (fisq) is in conflict with faith. «لو كانوا یؤمنون... ولكن ... فاسقون»

Ayah

۞ لَتَجِدَنَّ أَشَدَّ ٱلنَّاسِ عَدَٰوَةً لِّلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ ٱلْيَهُودَ وَٱلَّذِينَ أَشْرَكُوا۟ ۖ وَلَتَجِدَنَّ أَقْرَبَهُم مَّوَدَّةً لِّلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ ٱلَّذِينَ قَالُوٓا۟ إِنَّا نَصَـٰرَىٰ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ بِأَنَّ مِنْهُمْ قِسِّيسِينَ وَرُهْبَانًا وَأَنَّهُمْ لَا يَسْتَكْبِرُونَ (۸۲)
Surely You will find the most hostile of all people towards the faithful to be the Jews and the polytheists, and surely you will find the nearest of them in affection to the faithful to be those who say ‘We are Christians.’ That is because there are priests and monks among them, and because they are not arrogant. (82)

Key Points

Qissīs (قِسّیس) means priest (Kashīsh) and refers to the religious scholars who hold the religious leadership of the Christians. And Ruhbān (رُهبان) means monk (Tarsā) and refers to Christian worshippers/ascetics. The occasion of revelation (Sha'n Nuzūl) for this Ayah up to Ayah 85 is said to be the good treatment shown by Negus (Najāshī), the King of Abyssinia (Habasha), and the Christians of that country toward the Muslim emigrants who, led by Ja'far ibn Abī Ṭālib علیهما السلام, migrated from Mecca to that land in the fifth year of the Prophetic mission (Bi'that). Under the protection of Negus, they remained safe from the aggression of the polytheists and their envoys sent to Abyssinia. While the Jews of Medina, despite witnessing the Prophet's miracles and noble character, did not believe, participated in conspiracies against the Muslims, broke covenants, and stirred up sedition, the Christian clergy in Abyssinia wept upon hearing the verses of Sūrat Maryam and sided with the Muslims. (162) Imam Sadiq علیه السلام, after reciting this Ayah, said: «اولئك كانوا قوما بین عیسى و محمّد و ینتظرون محمّدصلى الله علیه وآله» These Christian scholars and monks were a group who lived in the time between Prophet Jesus علیه السلام and Prophet Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله and were awaiting the arrival of the Prophet of Islam. (163)

162) Tafsīr Majma‘ al-Bayān. 163) Tafāsīr Nūr al-Thaqalayn and ‘Ayyāshī.

Messages

1- The enmity of the Jews toward Muslims is historical and deep-rooted. «لتجدنّ اشدّ النّاس عداوة... الیهود»

2- Enemies of Islam and non-Muslims must be dealt with according to their own behavior. «اشدّ النّاس عداوة... اقربهم مودّة»

3- We must recognize our friends and enemies along with a correct analysis of their psychological and social factors. «اشدّ الناس عداوة... اقربهم مودةّ...»

4- The foundations for growth in society are three things: being knowledgeable, fearing God, and lacking an arrogant spirit. «قسّیسین و رهباناً و انّهم لایستكبرون»

5- Religious scholars and God-fearing worshippers play an effective role in reforming the beliefs and morals of society. «ذلك بان منهم قسیسین و رهباناً و انّهم لایستكبرون»

6- If knowledge, worship, and morality are linked together, a person becomes truth-seeking and abandons fanaticism. «ذلك بانّ منهم قسیسین و رهبانا و انّهم لایستكبرون»

7- Islam does not hold undue fanaticism and fairly praises the scholars of other religions who are God-fearing and just. «ذلك بانّ منهم قسیسین و رهبانا و انّهم لایستكبرون»

8- Propagating Islam among Christians is more effective. «انّهم لایستكبرون» (Although Christians hold the deviant belief of the "Trinity," due to their healthier spirit, they have a greater readiness to accept the truth.)

Ayah

وَإِذَا سَمِعُوا۟ مَآ أُنزِلَ إِلَى ٱلرَّسُولِ تَرَىٰٓ أَعْيُنَهُمْ تَفِيضُ مِنَ ٱلدَّمْعِ مِمَّا عَرَفُوا۟ مِنَ ٱلْحَقِّ ۖ يَقُولُونَ رَبَّنَآ ءَامَنَّا فَٱكْتُبْنَا مَعَ ٱلشَّـٰهِدِينَ (۸۳)
When they hear what has been revealed to the Apostle, you see their eyes fill with tears because of the truth that they recognize. They say, ‘Our Lord, we believe; so write us down among the witnesses. (83)

Key Points

An example of the Christians' tears of joy was one time when Ja'far ibn Abi Talib (علیهما السلام) recited the verses of Surah "Maryam" for the Negus in Abyssinia, and another time was when a group of Christians, accompanying Ja'far, came to Medina and heard the verses of Surah "Yasin". [164]

164) Tafsir Namuneh.

Messages

1- The sign of humble and receptive individuals is that they are transformed immediately upon hearing the truth. (But the unworthy are not moved even by seeing the truth.) «لایستكبرون و اذا سمعوا... تفیض من الدمع... یقولون ربّنا آمنّا»

2- Tears, if accompanied by knowledge (ma'rifah), are a sign of perfection. «تفیض من الدمع ممّا عرفوا»

3- The human soul and nature (fitrah) are captivated by the truth, and when they reach the Beloved, they shed tears of joy. «تفیض من الدمع»

4- Faith and affirmation must be based on recognition (knowledge). «ممّا عرفوا من الحقّ یقولون ربّنا آمنّا...»

5- In supplication (du'a), we should seek help through the sacred word "Rabbana" (Our Lord). «ربّنا آمنّا...»

6- Recognition and knowledge (ma'rifah), tears, and confession of one's own shortcomings are signs of spiritual growth and refinement. «عرفوا... یقولون ربّنا آمنّا»

7- Supplication (du'a) is effective alongside affirmation and faith. «آمنّا فاكتبنا»

8- Temporary faith is not sufficient; it must be permanent, established, and accompanied by a good ultimate outcome (husn al-'aqibah). «آمنّا فاكتبنا»

9- Covering a hundred years' journey in one night is valuable. Hearing («سمعوا»), recognizing («عرفوا»), affirming («آمنّا»), joining («مع الشاهدین»).

10- Believers must pray to reach higher stages. (First faith, then the higher stage, which is witnessing/shahadah.) «فاكتبنا مع الشاهدین»

Ayahs

وَمَا لَنَا لَا نُؤْمِنُ بِٱللَّهِ وَمَا جَآءَنَا مِنَ ٱلْحَقِّ وَنَطْمَعُ أَن يُدْخِلَنَا رَبُّنَا مَعَ ٱلْقَوْمِ ٱلصَّـٰلِحِينَ (۸٤)
Why should we not believe in Allah and the truth that has come to us, eager as we are that our Lord should admit us among the righteous people?’ (84)
فَأَثَـٰبَهُمُ ٱللَّهُ بِمَا قَالُوا۟ جَنَّـٰتٍ تَجْرِى مِن تَحْتِهَا ٱلْأَنْهَـٰرُ خَـٰلِدِينَ فِيهَا ۚ وَذَٰلِكَ جَزَآءُ ٱلْمُحْسِنِينَ (۸٥)
So, for what they said, Allah requited them with gardens with streams running in them, to remain in them [forever], and that is the reward of the virtuous. (85)

Ayah

وَٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ وَكَذَّبُوا۟ بِـَٔايَـٰتِنَآ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ أَصْحَـٰبُ ٱلْجَحِيمِ (۸٦)
But those who are faithless and deny Our signs—they shall be the inmates of hell. (86)

Key Points

Those who, after understanding the truth, bravely, clearly, and sincerely acknowledge it, and are not afraid of the atmosphere of their co-religionists, are among the best doers of good. This is because they have both done good to themselves, saving themselves from the torment of Hell, and by their acknowledgment, they have opened the way for others.

Messages

1- After recognizing the truth, «ممّاعرفوا», there is no excuse for rejecting it. «ومالنا...»

2- Association with the righteous is a sacred aspiration. «مع القوم الصالحین»

3- The sign of a sound aspiration is faith and practical action. «و ما لنا لانؤمن... و نطمع»

4- Faith in Allah is not separate from faith in revelation. «نؤمن باللّه وما جاءنا من الحقّ»

5- The reward for faith is Paradise. «ان یدخلنا ربّنا مع القوم الصالحین» (Based on the premise that the omitted object in «یدخلنا» is the word «الجنّة»)

6- One of the paths to perfection is returning to one's own conscience and questioning oneself. «وما لنا... فاثابهم اللَّه بماقالوا» In another Ayah, we read: «ومالى لاأعبد الّذى فطرنى...» [165]

7- Verbal acknowledgment of Tawhid (Divine Unity), the prophethood of the Prophet, and the truthfulness of the Quran is a necessary matter. «فاثابهم اللَّه بما قالوا...»

165) Yasin, 22.

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ لَا تُحَرِّمُوا۟ طَيِّبَـٰتِ مَآ أَحَلَّ ٱللَّهُ لَكُمْ وَلَا تَعْتَدُوٓا۟ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَا يُحِبُّ ٱلْمُعْتَدِينَ (۸۷)
O you who have faith! Do not prohibit the good things that Allah has made lawful to you, and do not transgress. Indeed Allah does not like the transgressors. (87)

Key Points

One day, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله was speaking about the Day of Judgment (Qiyamah) and the scenes of the Great Gathering (Mahshar). The people were so deeply moved and wept so much that some decided never to eat good food again, to forbid comfort upon themselves, to fast, to abandon their wives, to sleep less at night, and they swore an oath to this decision. When the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله was informed, He gathered the people in the mosque and said: I eat food, I sleep at night, and I do not abandon My wives. Our religion is not a path of isolation and monasticism (Rahbaniyyah). The monasticism of My Ummah is Jihad. Whoever deviates from My method is not a Muslim. [166]

Some asked: What should we do regarding the oaths we swore? The subsequent verses were revealed.

Declaring lawful things (Halal) unlawful is either accompanied by the belief in their prohibition, which constitutes innovation (Bid'ah) and is forbidden (Haram), or it is without the belief in their prohibition, meaning one merely refrains from benefiting from them, which is still prohibited.

A Muslim is submissive to the Divine command; he neither forbids lawful things on his own accord, nor does he treat unlawful things similarly.

166) Wasā’il, Vol. 20, p. 21.

Messages

1- The people of faith must utilize pure and good things (Tayyibāt). (The address uses) «یا ایّها الّذین آمنوا» instead of «یا ایّها النّاس».

2- Faith is incompatible with altering or tampering with Divine laws. «یا ایّها الّذین آمنوا لاتحرّموا...»

3- Islam is the religion of innate nature (Fitrah), and suppressing the innate nature is forbidden. «لا تحرّموا طیّبات» (The word Tayyib is used where something is in accordance with human nature and disposition.)

4- Lawful foods, clothing, and pleasures have been created for mankind. «لكم»

5- Faith is incompatible with extremism (Ifrāt) and negligence (Tafrīt). «یا ایّها الّذین آمنوا... لاتعتدوا...»

6- In the school of Islam, isolation, monasticism (Rahbaniyyah), extremism, and negligence are forbidden. «لا تحرّموا طیّبات... و لاتعتدوا»

7- Depriving oneself of good things (Tayyibāt) is an act of transgression and excess. «لاتعتدوا»

8- In utilizing lawful things, we should not commit extravagance (Isrāf) or excess. «لا تعتدوا»

9- Extremism and negligence lead to being deprived of Allah’s love. «انّ اللّه لایحبّ المعتدین»

Ayah

وَكُلُوا۟ مِمَّا رَزَقَكُمُ ٱللَّهُ حَلَـٰلًا طَيِّبًا ۚ وَٱتَّقُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ ٱلَّذِىٓ أَنتُم بِهِۦ مُؤْمِنُونَ (۸۸)
Eat the lawful and good things Allah has provided you, and be wary of Allah in whom you have faith. (88)

Key Points

Usually, the command "كلوا" (Eat) in the Quran is accompanied by another instruction, such as: "كلوا . . . واشكروا" [167] (Eat and be grateful). "كلوا . . . ولاتطغوا" [168] (Eat and do not transgress). "كلوا . . . واعملوا" [169] (Eat and perform righteous deeds). "كلوا . . . وأطعموا" [170] (Eat and feed others). "كلوا . . . ولاتسرفوا" [171] (Eat and do not be extravagant). "كلوا . . . ولاتتّبعوا خطوات الشیطان" [172] (Eat and do not follow the footsteps of Satan). It is stated in a Hadith: Allah has designated the provision (rizq) of people from what is lawful (halal). Whoever turns towards the unlawful (haram), his share of the lawful will be reduced. [173]

167) Baqarah, 172. 168) Taha, 81. 169) Mu’minun, 51. 170) Hajj, 28. 171) A’raf, 31. 172) An’am, 142. 173) Tafsir Atayeb al-Bayan.

Messages

1- Utilizing lawful material resources does not contradict faith. «یا ایّها الّذین آمنوا... و كلوا» Those who consider piety (taqwa) to mean abandoning material things are living in delusion.

2- We must be careful regarding matters of nutrition/food. «حلالاً، طیباً، واتّقوا» (Both the method of earning and the method of consuming it must be lawful.)

3- The provision (rizq) of everyone is in His hands, so we should avoid haste, greed, and consuming the unlawful (haram). «رزقكم اللّه حلالا طیّبا»

4- Piety (taqwa) is a prerequisite for faith. «واتّقوا اللّه الّذى أنتم به مؤمنون»

Ayah
