لَا يُؤَاخِذُكُمُ ٱللَّهُ بِٱللَّغْوِ فِىٓ أَيْمَـٰنِكُمْ وَلَـٰكِن يُؤَاخِذُكُم بِمَا عَقَّدتُّمُ ٱلْأَيْمَـٰنَ ۖ فَكَفَّـٰرَتُهُۥٓ إِطْعَامُ عَشَرَةِ مَسَـٰكِينَ مِنْ أَوْسَطِ مَا تُطْعِمُونَ أَهْلِيكُمْ أَوْ كِسْوَتُهُمْ أَوْ تَحْرِيرُ رَقَبَةٍ ۖ فَمَن لَّمْ يَجِدْ فَصِيَامُ ثَلَـٰثَةِ أَيَّامٍ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ كَفَّـٰرَةُ أَيْمَـٰنِكُمْ إِذَا حَلَفْتُمْ ۚ وَٱحْفَظُوٓا۟ أَيْمَـٰنَكُمْ ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ يُبَيِّنُ ٱللَّهُ لَكُمْ ءَايَـٰتِهِۦ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَشْكُرُونَ (۸۹)
Allah shall not take you to task for what is frivolous in your oaths; but He shall take you to task for what you pledge in earnest. The atonement for it is to feed ten needy persons with the average food you give to your families, or their clothing, or the freeing of a slave. He who cannot afford [any of these] shall fast for three days. That is the atonement for your oaths when you vow. But keep your oaths. Thus does Allah clarify His signs for you so that you may give thanks. (89)

Key Points

Vain oaths (oaths of laghw) are those made without intention or attention, or aimlessly and out of habit, or in a state of excitement and anger, or based on incorrect information, or for the purpose of committing unlawful acts. These do not require expiation (kaffarah). However, an oath made intentionally and for a beneficial purpose is binding and must be acted upon; otherwise, it requires expiation. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام, in interpreting the word laghw regarding oaths, said: Saying "لا واللَّه" (No, by Allah) and "بلى واللَّه" (Yes, by Allah) without having a serious intention constitutes a vain oath. (174)

Some have interpreted the word "أوسط" (average/middle) as the best type of food. Similar to the Ayah in Surah Al-Qalam where "قال أوسطهم" means the best and most excellent among them said such-and-such.

Clothing the deprived, which is part of the expiation for an oath, includes all types of covering: winter, summer, women's, and men's clothing.

A universal and eternal religion must have regulations and punishments that are adaptable, such as freeing a slave, feeding the poor, or clothing the needy, which are actionable at any time, by any individual, anywhere.

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: Some eat more than ten seers (a measure of weight/volume) of food and some eat less than ten seers. Therefore, calculate the average amount for one meal as ten seers (one mudd). (175)

Imam Kadhim عليه السلام, in explaining the above Ayah, said: A person who possesses no more than the sustenance required for his family is an instance of the phrase "لم یجد" (he who finds not). (176)

174) Tafsir Noor al-Thaqalayn and Kafi, Vol. 7, p. 443. 175) Tafsir Noor al-Thaqalayn; Kafi, Vol. 7, p. 453. 176) Tafsir Noor al-Thaqalayn and Kafi, Vol. 7, p. 452.

Messages

1- Removing the penalty for aimless oaths is a favor and divine grace from Allah. "لایؤاخذكم اللَّه بالّلغو..." (We, too, should not hold people accountable for the insults they direct at us while angry.)

2- Islam is a religion of ease and has not mandated expiation for non-serious oaths. "لایؤاخذكم..." (The criterion for words and deeds is intention and purpose.)

3- Financial penalties also exist in Islam. "اطعام...أو كسوتهم..." (Feeding... or clothing them...)

4- Islamic laws are aimed at poverty alleviation, and the individual rulings of Islam are consistent with the interests of society. (Feeding, or clothing, or freeing a slave) "فكفّارته اطعام عشرة مسكین..." (Its expiation is the feeding of ten needy persons...)

5- Moderation and justice are values everywhere, even in the type of penalty. "من أوسط..." (Based on the interpretation that "أوسط" means average, not best.)

6- The penalty must be commensurate with the financial status of the individuals. "ما تطعمون أهلیكم" (what you feed your own families)

7- The performance of duty is conditional upon ability and power. "فمن لم یجد..." (But he who finds not...)

8- In penalties and expiation, the average standard of people's daily and ordinary lives must be considered, not exceptional days. "من أوسط" (Based on the interpretation that "أوسط" means average.)

9- When paying expiation, we must not break the spirit and dignity of the poor; we should treat them like our own family. "تطعمون أهلیكم" (you feed your own families)

10- Compensate for the audacity of breaking an oath through the hardship of fasting or paying the penalty. (Paying penalties and expiation in Islam is a program of self-purification.) "فكفّارته..." (Its expiation...)

11- Islam has utilized every opportunity to free slaves. "تحریر رقبة" (The freeing of a slave) (It has even commanded the freeing of a slave to compensate for and expiate certain actions.)

12- We must respect the sacred Name of Allah. Either do not swear, or certainly fulfill the oath, or compensate by paying expiation. "واحفظوا أیمانكم" (And guard your oaths)

13- Some should not imagine that they have the right to swear oaths at any moment and simply pay the penalty. "واحفظوا أیمانكم" (We are responsible for our oaths.)

14- He who lacks financial ability must rely on his physical capacity. "فصیام ثلاثة أیام" (then fasting for three days)

15- The openness of the path to compensate for shortcomings is a divine blessing worthy of gratitude. "ذلك كفّارة ایمانكم... یبیّن اللَّه... لعلّكم تشكرون" (That is the expiation for your oaths... Allah makes clear... so that you may be grateful.)

16- Acting upon the divine rulings is the best form of gratitude. "لعلّكم تشكرون" (From the fact that He said "لعلّكم تشكرون" instead of "یبیّن اللَّه لكم آیاته لعلّكم تعملون" [Allah makes clear His signs to you so that you may act], it is inferred that action is the best gratitude.)

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوٓا۟ إِنَّمَا ٱلْخَمْرُ وَٱلْمَيْسِرُ وَٱلْأَنصَابُ وَٱلْأَزْلَـٰمُ رِجْسٌ مِّنْ عَمَلِ ٱلشَّيْطَـٰنِ فَٱجْتَنِبُوهُ لَعَلَّكُمْ تُفْلِحُونَ (۹٠)
O you who have faith! Indeed wine, gambling, idols, and the divining arrows are abominations of Satan’s doing, so avoid them, so that you may be felicitous. (90)

Key Points

The Arabs had a strong attachment to poetry and wine (sharāb). For this reason, the prohibition of wine was carried out gradually. Initially, an Ayah came stating that from dates and grapes one could prepare both good sustenance and intoxicating wine. (177) In another Ayah, it referred to the benefits of gambling and wine, and that their sin is greater than their benefit. (178) Later, an Ayah came stating that you should not perform prayer while intoxicated. (179) Then the above Ayah was revealed, declaring it impure (foul) and a work of Satan, and issuing the command to abstain from it.

Khamr (wine) is from the same root as the word khimār, meaning to cover. The head covering (veil) of women is called khimār because it covers the hair. Wine is also called khamr because it covers the intellect.

Maysir is derived from yusr, meaning ease. Because in gambling, people acquire money without effort, it is called maysir.

The word ansāb is the plural of nuṣub, meaning stones that were erected around the Ka'bah upon which sacrifices were made, and from which they sought blessings. (180) Or, the intention is the sacrifice itself, which was slaughtered upon the stones. We read in a Hadith that the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said regarding the meaning of ansāb: It is something that the polytheists sacrifice for their idols. «ما ذبحوه لآلهتهم» (181)

Azlām refers to a type of lottery and gambling using sticks that was common during the Age of Ignorance (Jāhiliyyah).

Imam Baqir عليه السلام said concerning khamr: «كلّ مسكر... اذا اخمر فهو حرام» (Everything that intoxicates... when it covers the intellect, it is forbidden/harām). Everything that intoxicates a person is forbidden. (182)

The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said: Gambling, by whatever means, even walnuts, is forbidden. (183)

It is stated in a Hadith: «شارب الخمر كعابد الوثن» (The wine drinker is like an idol worshipper). (184)

Regarding wine, every type of cooperation in its production, distribution, and consumption is forbidden (harām). Imam Baqir عليه السلام narrates from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله that He cursed ten groups who are effective in some way in wine drinking: «غارسها و حارسها و عاصرها و شاربها و ساقیها و حاملها و المحمول الیه، بایعها و مشتریها و آكل ثمنها» (185) The one who plants the grape vine, the one who tends it (brings it to fruition), the maker (presser), the drinker, the server (sāqī), the carrier, the one to whom it is carried, the seller, the buyer, and anyone who benefits from its income in any way, is cursed (mal'ūn).

177) An-Nahl, 67. 178) Al-Baqarah, 219. 179) An-Nisā’, 43. 180) Tafsir Al-Mizan. 181) Kāfī, Vol. 5, p. 123. 182) Tafsir Noor Ath-Thaqalayn. 183) Kāfī, Vol. 5, p. 123. 184) Bihār, Vol. 27, p. 234. 185) Tafsir Noor Ath-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- Faith is incompatible with wine drinking and gambling. «یا ایّها الّذین آمنوا انّما الخمر و المیسر... فاجتنبوه...» 2- Wine and gambling are ranked alongside idol worship. «الخمر والمیسر والانصاب» (Based on the interpretation that ansāb refers to idols). 3- Islam prohibits impurities. «رجس... فاجتنبوه» 4- Not only should you not drink wine, but you should also not go near it. «فاجتنبوه», instead of saying: «لاتشربوا الخمر». 5- Lawful sustenance (luqmah-ye ḥalāl) and avoidance of unlawful consumption are effective in human happiness and salvation. «فاجتنبوه لعلّكم تفلحون»

Ayah

إِنَّمَا يُرِيدُ ٱلشَّيْطَـٰنُ أَن يُوقِعَ بَيْنَكُمُ ٱلْعَدَٰوَةَ وَٱلْبَغْضَآءَ فِى ٱلْخَمْرِ وَٱلْمَيْسِرِ وَيَصُدَّكُمْ عَن ذِكْرِ ٱللَّهِ وَعَنِ ٱلصَّلَوٰةِ ۖ فَهَلْ أَنتُم مُّنتَهُونَ (۹۱)
Indeed Satan seeks to cast enmity and hatred among you through wine and gambling, and to hinder you from the remembrance of Allah and from prayer. Will you, then, relinquish? (91)

Key Points

| Although according to the statistics presented, many murders, crimes, accidents, divorces, psychological and kidney diseases, etc., result from intoxicants, the Quran, in stating the philosophy of prohibition in this Ayah, emphasizes two points: one is social harm, meaning malice and enmity, and the other is spiritual harm, meaning heedlessness of prayer (Salat) and the remembrance of Allah. This Ayah considers the most prominent effect of intoxicants and gambling to be hindering (one) from the remembrance of Allah and prayer. Now, the question arises: if ordinary activities such as trade, sports, education, study, and the like also preoccupy us and keep us away from the remembrance of Allah and prayer, are they similar to intoxicants and gambling? Yes, anything that makes us heedless of Allah is undesirable, like intoxicants and gambling, even if Islam has not declared it forbidden (Haram) due to His grace and ease (upon us).

Messages

1- Anyone who becomes a factor of malice and enmity among people is like Satan. «انمّا یرید الشیطان...»

2- Satan's instigations are continuous. «انّما یرید الشیطان» (The imperfect verb [present tense] is the symbol of permanence and continuity.)

3- A society in which enmity exists is satanic. «انّما یرید الشیطان ان یوقع بینكم العداوة...»

4- We must safeguard affection and unity and fight against anything that destroys them. «انّما یرید الشیطان ان یوقع بینكم العداوة»

5- Affection and kindness among the believers are subject to Allah's special attention. «انّما یرید الشیطان ان یوقع بینكم العداوة»

6- Gambling and intoxicants are Satan's tools for creating malice and enmity. «انّما یرید الشیطان... فى الخمر و المیسر»

7- Prayer (Salat) is the supreme symbol of the remembrance of Allah. (Although prayer is the remembrance of Allah, it is mentioned separately.) «عن ذكر اللَّه و عن الصلوة»

8- Stating the philosophy behind rulings (Ahkam) is one of the factors that makes speech effective upon others. «العداوة و البغضاء... فهل انتم منتهون»

9- All factors that cause malice and enmity must be fought against. «یرید الشیطان ان یوقع بینكم العداوة و البغضاء... فهل انتم منتهون»

10- Some Muslims in the early days of Islam who were still engaged in intoxicants and gambling despite the prohibition were subjected to Allah's reproach. «فهل انتم منتهون»

Ayah

وَأَطِيعُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ وَأَطِيعُوا۟ ٱلرَّسُولَ وَٱحْذَرُوا۟ ۚ فَإِن تَوَلَّيْتُمْ فَٱعْلَمُوٓا۟ أَنَّمَا عَلَىٰ رَسُولِنَا ٱلْبَلَـٰغُ ٱلْمُبِينُ (۹۲)
And obey Allah and obey the Apostle, and beware; but if you turn your backs, then know that Our Apostle’s duty is only to communicate in clear terms. (92)

Messages

1- The governmental commands of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله, just like the divine commands, must be obeyed. «اطیعوا اللّه و اطیعوا الرسول» (The repetition of the word «أطیعوا» indicates two types of commands: the fixed divine commands and the governmental commands of the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله.)

2- We must fear the danger of disobeying the command of Allah and the Messenger. «واحذروا»

3- Man is free in choosing his path. «فان تولّیتم»

4- By transgression and turning away, we harm no one but ourselves. «فان تولّیتم... انّما على رسولنا البلاغ المبین»

5- The acceptance of the people is not a condition for the conveyance of divine rulings; we must complete the argument (establish proof). «فان تولّیتم... على رسولنا البلاغ المبین»

6- The duty of the Prophet is only the conveyance of the message, not coercion or imposition. «انما على رسولنا البلاغ المبین»

Ayah

لَيْسَ عَلَى ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَعَمِلُوا۟ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَـٰتِ جُنَاحٌ فِيمَا طَعِمُوٓا۟ إِذَا مَا ٱتَّقَوا۟ وَّءَامَنُوا۟ وَعَمِلُوا۟ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَـٰتِ ثُمَّ ٱتَّقَوا۟ وَّءَامَنُوا۟ ثُمَّ ٱتَّقَوا۟ وَّأَحْسَنُوا۟ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ يُحِبُّ ٱلْمُحْسِنِينَ (۹۳)
There will be no sin upon those who have faith and do righteous deeds in regard to what they have eaten [in the past] so long as they are Godwary and faithful and do righteous deeds, and are further Godwary and faithful, and are further Godwary and virtuous. And Allah loves the virtuous. (93)

Key Points

After the revelation of the verse prohibiting wine (intoxicants), some Muslims inquired about the punishment for those who had consumed wine and passed away before the prohibition. The above verse was then revealed. In this verse, faith (Iman) and righteous deeds (Amal Salih) are mentioned twice, piety (Taqwa) and faith are mentioned twice, and piety and goodness (Ihsan) are mentioned once. According to some commentators, the repetition occurred because the instances, stages, and degrees of faith, piety, and righteous deeds are different. (186) Some say the repetition is for the sake of the continuity (Istimrar) of faith and piety. (187) And some consider the repetition to be for emphasis, similar to: «كلاّ سیعلمون. ثمّ كلاّسیعلمون» (188) Piety (Taqwa) plays a role in various aspects of human life: In consumption and behavior: «طعموا اذا ما اتّقوا» In doctrine and belief: «ثمّ اتّقوا و آمنوا» In service and ethics: «ثمّ اتّقوا و أحسنوا»

186) Tafsir Namuneh. 187) Tafsir Qurtubi. 188) Naba, 4 to 5.

Messages

1- The past transgressions of believers are forgivable, provided they are not repeated and piety is observed in the future. «لیس... جناحٌ... اذا ما اتّقوا»

2- If they commit sins again after hearing the divine ruling, Allah will also hold them accountable for their previous sins. «لیس... جناح فیما طعموا اذا مااتّقوا»

3- Ihsan (goodness) and benevolence are the highest stage of perfection and a cause for popularity (being loved) by Allah. The word «احسنوا» is mentioned after «آمنوا و عملوا... ثمّ اتّقوا...», followed by the mention of being loved by Allah. «و اللَّه یحبّ المحسنین»

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ لَيَبْلُوَنَّكُمُ ٱللَّهُ بِشَىْءٍ مِّنَ ٱلصَّيْدِ تَنَالُهُۥٓ أَيْدِيكُمْ وَرِمَاحُكُمْ لِيَعْلَمَ ٱللَّهُ مَن يَخَافُهُۥ بِٱلْغَيْبِ ۚ فَمَنِ ٱعْتَدَىٰ بَعْدَ ذَٰلِكَ فَلَهُۥ عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌ (۹٤)
O you who have faith! Allah will surely test you with some of the game within the reach of your hands and spears, so that Allah may know those who fear Him in secret. So whoever transgresses after that, there is a painful punishment for him. (94)

Key Points

During the Hajj days and in the state of Ihrām (consecration), the pilgrim does not have the right to hunt. During those very days, sometimes game comes close to the person and can be easily reached, but the Divine test lies in refraining from hunting. We read in a Hadith: During the Umrah (Lesser Pilgrimage) of Hudaybiyyah, abundant game animals appeared to the Muslims, which the Muslims could easily catch. [189]

In the Quran, the issue of the stomach and food is presented as one of the means of Divine testing. Pay attention to the following examples: A: Adam and Eve failed in the matter of food. «لا تقربا هذه الشجرة» [190] B: The Children of Israel failed in the prohibition of fishing on Saturdays. «ولقد علمتم الّذین اعتدوا منكم فى السبت» [191] C: One of the commanders of the Children of Israel, when his soldiers passed through an area and reached a river, commanded them not to drink its water, but his companions, except for a few, drank. «فشربوا منه الاّ قلیلاً» [192] D: Fasting itself is a great test. E: The game approaching the pilgrims in the state of Ihrām, when they have no right to hunt. «تناله أیدیكم»

Question: When a human tests something, it is for information and knowledge, but why does Allah, Who knows everything, test? Answer: Imam Ali عليه السلام states in Nahj al-Balaghah: Divine tests are so that man shows a reaction to bitter and sweet events, and in response to that, becomes deserving of punishment or reward. [193] Yes, Allah gives punishment and reward based on action, not based on His own knowledge, just as we do not reward an individual merely because we know of his skill and efficiency; rather, he must demonstrate work for us to receive wages.

Question: Should a guest and pilgrim of the House of Allah be afflicted with painful torment merely for hunting an animal? Answer: Hunting itself does not incur painful torment; rather, the torment is due to his violation of the sanctity of the Sanctuary (Haram), the state of Ihrām, and his breaking of the law. «مَن اعتدى بعد ذلك فله عذاب الیم»

189) تفسیر نورالثقلین و كافى، ج ۴، ص ۳۹۶. 190) بقره، ۳۵. 191) بقره، ۶۵. 192) بقره، ۲۴۹. 193) نهج البلاغه، خطبه ۱۹۲.

Messages

1- The testing of the believers is a definite tradition of the Lord. «یا ایّها الّذین آمنوا لیبلونّكم» (The letter lām, the imperfect verb, and the nūn of emphasis are signs of certainty.) 2- Fear of Allah becomes manifest when the opportunity for sin is available, yet the person does not commit the error. «تناله أیدیكم» 3- Accessible blessings are a means of testing. «لیبلونّكم اللّه بشى ء... تناله ایدیكم» 4- Not everything that our hands reach is sustenance (rizq) and lawful (halāl). «تناله أیدیكم» 5- The criterion for piety (taqwā) is inner fear, not merely outward shyness. «یخافه بالغیب» 6- Obligation and responsibility come after notification (or communication). «بعد ذلك» 7- The pilgrim who travels miles in the deserts out of love for the Truth (Allah) is sometimes undone by a small game animal, leading him to disobedience, the result of which is Divine torment. «مَن اعتدى بعد ذلك فله عذاب الیم»

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ لَا تَقْتُلُوا۟ ٱلصَّيْدَ وَأَنتُمْ حُرُمٌ ۚ وَمَن قَتَلَهُۥ مِنكُم مُّتَعَمِّدًا فَجَزَآءٌ مِّثْلُ مَا قَتَلَ مِنَ ٱلنَّعَمِ يَحْكُمُ بِهِۦ ذَوَا عَدْلٍ مِّنكُمْ هَدْيًۢا بَـٰلِغَ ٱلْكَعْبَةِ أَوْ كَفَّـٰرَةٌ طَعَامُ مَسَـٰكِينَ أَوْ عَدْلُ ذَٰلِكَ صِيَامًا لِّيَذُوقَ وَبَالَ أَمْرِهِۦ ۗ عَفَا ٱللَّهُ عَمَّا سَلَفَ ۚ وَمَنْ عَادَ فَيَنتَقِمُ ٱللَّهُ مِنْهُ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ عَزِيزٌ ذُو ٱنتِقَامٍ (۹٥)
O you who have faith! Do not kill any game when you are in pilgrim sanctity. Should any of you kill it intentionally, its atonement, the counterpart from cattle of what he has killed, as judged by two fair men among you, will be an offering brought to the Ka‘bah, or an atonement by feeding needy persons, or its equivalent in fasting, that he may taste the untoward consequences of his conduct. Allah has excused what is already past; but should anyone resume, Allah shall take vengeance on him, for Allah is all-mighty, avenger. (95)

Key Points

The word "حُرُم" (Ḥurum) means someone who has entered the state of Ihrām (consecration) for Hajj or Umrah. The word "نَعَم" (Na'am), whose plural is "اَنعام" (An'ām), refers to camels, cattle, and sheep. The word "مسكین" (Miskīn), derived from sakan (dwelling), refers to someone who, due to destitution and poverty, does not leave his dwelling; in essence, his poverty has crippled him and confined him to his home. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: The expiation (kaffārah) for an ostrich is a camel, the expiation for a wild donkey is cattle, the expiation for a gazelle is a sheep, and the expiation for a wild cow is a domestic cow. [194]

194) Tahdhīb, Vol. 5, p. 341. Nūr al-Thaqalayn, Vol. 1, p. 673. For further explanation regarding the expiation for hunting, its rulings, and specifics, refer to books of jurisprudence (fiqh) and Tawdīh al-Masā'il (Practical Treatises).

Messages

  1. Allah has special expectations of the believers. "یا ایها الذین آمنوا لاتقتلوا..."
  2. Security during the state of Ihrām must be maintained even for animals. "لاتقتلوا الصید و انتم حُرُم"
  3. The philosophy behind prohibition or permissibility is not always inherent to the items themselves; rather, it is sometimes dictated by temporal and spatial conditions. Thus, time and place, history and geography, influence divine rulings. "و انتم حُرُم"
  4. Islam has specific instructions for special states. "و انتم حُرُم"
  5. Islamic laws are for everyone. "و من قتله منكم"
  6. More dangerous than the act or transgression itself is the motive, the goal, and the conscious intent (malice aforethought). "من قتله منكم متعمّداً"
  7. Punishment must be just. "مثل ما قتل"
  8. The payment of the penalty and fine must be precise. "یحكم به ذوا عدل"
  9. Islam pays attention to justice everywhere. "ذوا عدل منكم"
  10. Islam addresses the issue of the hungry and poverty alleviation on every suitable occasion. "طعام مسكین"
  11. Animals slaughtered in Mecca are offerings (hady). "هدیاً بالغ الكعبة"
  12. We should allow the offender flexibility in choosing the type of penalty (sacrifice, feeding, fasting) and not ignore his financial condition and physical ability. "فجزاء مثل ما قتل... او عدل ذلك صیاماً"
  13. The penalty for breaking the law is so severe that feeding sixty poor people or fasting for sixty days is only a part of it, not the entirety. "لیذوق" (So that he may taste [the consequence]).
  14. The implementation of laws occurs after their official promulgation. "عفى اللّه عما سلف" (Allah has forgiven what is past). The law does not cover previous instances.
  15. In the repetition of hunting, besides the fine, there is also divine vengeance and wrath. "و من عاد فینتقم اللَّه"
  16. Insistence on sin and its repetition is extremely dangerous and entails severe punishment. "و من عاد فینتقم اللَّه منه و اللّه عزیز ذو انتقام"
  17. We must take divine warnings seriously. "فینتقم اللّه... عزیز ذو انتقام"

Ayah

أُحِلَّ لَكُمْ صَيْدُ ٱلْبَحْرِ وَطَعَامُهُۥ مَتَـٰعًا لَّكُمْ وَلِلسَّيَّارَةِ ۖ وَحُرِّمَ عَلَيْكُمْ صَيْدُ ٱلْبَرِّ مَا دُمْتُمْ حُرُمًا ۗ وَٱتَّقُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ ٱلَّذِىٓ إِلَيْهِ تُحْشَرُونَ (۹٦)
You are permitted the game of the sea and its food, a provision for you and for the caravans, but you are forbidden the game of the land so long as you remain in pilgrim sanctity, and be wary of Allah toward whom you will be gathered. (96)

Key Points

Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan, some other exegeses, and jurisprudential books have inferred from this Ayah that while in the state of Ihrām (consecration), hunting and consuming sea animals is lawful (halāl), but hunting and consuming land animals is unlawful (harām). Of course, the intent of "sea animal" refers to aquatic creatures and animals that live in water, even if they are in a river. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said regarding "وطعامه متاعاً لكم و للسّیارة" (and its food is a provision for you and for the travelers): The intent is salted fish that they eat. [195] (Which they prevented from spoiling for a long time and during travels by salting it.)

195) Kāfī, Vol. 4, p. 392.

Messages

1- Not all paths are closed for those in the state of Ihrām. (Alongside prohibiting land hunting, Allah has permitted sea hunting.) "احلّ... حرم"

2- Hunting is lawful only when it is for sustenance, not for recreation or frivolity. "متاعاً لكم"

3- The benefits and products of the sea are not exclusive to coastal dwellers. "متاعاً لكم و للسّیارة"

4- Coastal dwellers have priority in utilizing the sea. "لكم وللسیارة" (The word "لكم" comes before the word "للسیارة")

5- Hunting land animals or eating them while in the state of Ihrām is a lack of piety. "حرّم علیكم صید البرّ ما دمتم حرماً و اتّقواللَّه"

6- Transgressors must be warned. "واتّقواللَّه الّذى الیه تحشرون"

7- Belief in the Day of Judgment and the reckoning of deeds is a deterrent against sin. "واتّقوا اللَّه الّذى الیه تحشرون"

Ayah

۞ جَعَلَ ٱللَّهُ ٱلْكَعْبَةَ ٱلْبَيْتَ ٱلْحَرَامَ قِيَـٰمًا لِّلنَّاسِ وَٱلشَّهْرَ ٱلْحَرَامَ وَٱلْهَدْىَ وَٱلْقَلَـٰٓئِدَ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ لِتَعْلَمُوٓا۟ أَنَّ ٱللَّهَ يَعْلَمُ مَا فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَمَا فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ وَأَنَّ ٱللَّهَ بِكُلِّ شَىْءٍ عَلِيمٌ (۹۷)
Allah has made the Ka‘bah, the Sacred House, a [means of] sustentation for mankind, and [also] the sacred month, the offering and the garlands, so that you may know that Allah knows whatever there is in the heavens and whatever there is in the earth, and that Allah has knowledge of all things. (97)

Key Points

Qiyām (standing/upholding) is the means of permanence, like the pillar of a building or the pole of a tent. (196) Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: The House of God is called Bayt al-Harām (The Sacred House) because the entry of disbelievers (kuffār) into it is forbidden (harām). (197) Al-Hady (the offering) refers to an unmarked sacrifice, and al-Qalā'id (the garlands) refers to marked sacrifices. Centrality, security, sanctity, worship, antiquity, unity, politics and disavowal (of polytheists), knowledge, simplicity, purity, sincerity, and distance from argumentation (jidāl), lust, and transgression (fusūq) are all found in Hajj. The Sacred Months (Ashhur al-Hurum) are: Rajab, Dhul-Qa'dah, Dhul-Hijjah, and Muharram, during which warfare is forbidden. The gathering of millions of Muslims in Mecca, without formalities or privileges, and without verbal argumentation or practical conflict, in a sacred place, is one of the distinctions of Islam. If we consider the various benefits derived from the Hajj rituals—such as seeking forgiveness (from others) before departure, visiting one another, commercial prosperity, paying khums and zakāt, becoming acquainted with knowledge and nations, being situated in the oldest center of Monotheism (Tawhīd), echoing the cries of the prophets and following in their footsteps, repenting in the plains of Arafat and Mash'ar, remembering the Day of Judgment, the political maneuver (of disavowal), and disavowal of the disbelievers (kuffār), and other blessings—we understand that these Hajj programs originate from the infinite knowledge of God, Who is aware of everything in existence, and limited knowledge could never issue an instruction with such comprehensiveness and appeal.

(196) Mufradāt Rāghib. (197) Tafsir Nūr al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- Hajj is the source of stability and sustenance. «قیاماً للناس» 2- Organizing affairs requires gathering, unity, worship, respect and sanctity, «بیت الحرام» (The Sacred House), tranquility and security «الشهرالحرام» (The Sacred Month), and both hidden/unmarked and marked actions «الهدى و القلائد» (the offerings and the garlands), and the provision of necessary food. «قیاماً للنّاس» 3- Only He Who is aware of all existence has the right to legislate. «یعلم ما فى السماوات و ما فى الارض» 4- The mosque (Ka'bah) and the place of sacrifice (Mina/Maslakh) are the means of sustaining the religion and the worldly life of the people. «الكعبة... الهدى و القلائد» Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: «جَعَلها اللّه لدینهم و معیشتهم» (Allah made it for their religion and their livelihood). (198) 5- A Hajj that does not lead to the stability of society is not a true Hajj. «الكعبة... قیاماً للناس» 6- If society has stability, the ground for the growth of God-knowledge (Theology) develops within it. «قیاماً... لتعلموا ان اللّه...»

(198) Tafsir Burhān.

Ayahs

ٱعْلَمُوٓا۟ أَنَّ ٱللَّهَ شَدِيدُ ٱلْعِقَابِ وَأَنَّ ٱللَّهَ غَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ (۹۸)
Know that Allah is severe in retribution, and that Allah is all-forgiving, all-merciful. (98)
مَّا عَلَى ٱلرَّسُولِ إِلَّا ٱلْبَلَـٰغُ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ يَعْلَمُ مَا تُبْدُونَ وَمَا تَكْتُمُونَ (۹۹)
The Apostle’s duty is only to communicate and Allah knows whatever you disclose and whatever you conceal. (99)

Key Points

If we consider this Ayah to be a continuation of the previous Ayah, it can be said that violating the sanctity of the Ka'bah and the Sacred Months, opposing the sacrificial offerings and the rulings of Hajj, and deeming the rituals of Hajj purposeless, will result in severe punishment. Just as respecting the Ka'bah and the Sacred Months, performing the sacrificial offerings, and the rituals of Hajj, will result in divine forgiveness and mercy. (Of course, the Ayah can be interpreted independently, without reference to the previous Ayah, which aligns with the standard translation.)

Messages

1- Encouragement and threat must exist side by side. «شَدِيدُ الْعِقَابِ غَفُورٌ رَحِيمٌ» (Man must live between fear and hope; he should not become completely despairing due to a transgression or violating the sanctity of the Ka'bah, nor should he become arrogant due to good behavior and deeds.) 2- The duty of the Prophet is only the conveyance (of the religion), not compulsion or imposition. «مَا عَلَى الرَّسُولِ إِلَّا الْبَلَاغُ» 3- The acceptance or rejection by the people does not harm the Prophet. «مَا عَلَى الرَّسُولِ إِلَّا الْبَلَاغُ» As we read in Ayah 20 of Surah Aal-i-Imran: «فَإِنْ تَوَلَّوْا فَإِنَّمَا عَلَيْكَ الْبَلَاغُ» 4- Since divine knowledge is comprehensive, concealment or disclosure is the same to Him. «يَعْلَمُ مَا تُبْدُونَ وَمَا تَكْتُمُونَ...»

Ayah

قُل لَّا يَسْتَوِى ٱلْخَبِيثُ وَٱلطَّيِّبُ وَلَوْ أَعْجَبَكَ كَثْرَةُ ٱلْخَبِيثِ ۚ فَٱتَّقُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ يَـٰٓأُو۟لِى ٱلْأَلْبَـٰبِ لَعَلَّكُمْ تُفْلِحُونَ (۱٠٠)
Say, ‘The good and the bad are not equal, though the abundance of the bad should amaze you.’ So be wary of Allah, O you who possess intellect, so that you may be felicitous! (100)

Key Points

Tayyib (the good/pure) and Khabith (the bad/vile) encompass every type of purity and vileness in human beings, properties, incomes, foods, and various objects.

Messages

1- The criterion for values is truth (Haqq) and falsehood (Batil), not quantity (Kathrah) or scarcity (Qillah). «ولواعجبك كثرة الخبیث»

2- "Majority" and abundance are deceptive; we must be careful. «اعجبك» (The majority is neither a sign of truthfulness nor a sign of superiority.)

3- The logic of "If you don't want to be disgraced, conform to the crowd" is not Quranic. «و لو اعجبك كثرة الخبیث»

4- Lack of piety (God-fearingness) is a sign of lack of wisdom. «فاتّقوا اللّه یا اولى الالباب»

5- Recognizing the pure from the impure, possessing piety, and not submitting to the wave and clamor of the crowd is solely the work of the wise. «فاتّقواللّه یا اولى الالباب»

6- Salvation, in addition to intellect and wisdom, requires Divine piety. «فاتّقواللَّه یا اولى الالباب لعلّكم تفلحون»

Ayahs

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ لَا تَسْـَٔلُوا۟ عَنْ أَشْيَآءَ إِن تُبْدَ لَكُمْ تَسُؤْكُمْ وَإِن تَسْـَٔلُوا۟ عَنْهَا حِينَ يُنَزَّلُ ٱلْقُرْءَانُ تُبْدَ لَكُمْ عَفَا ٱللَّهُ عَنْهَا ۗ وَٱللَّهُ غَفُورٌ حَلِيمٌ (۱٠۱)
O you who have faith! Do not ask about things, which, if they are disclosed to you, will upset you. Yet if you ask about them while the Quran is being sent down, they shall be disclosed to you. Allah has excused it, and Allah is all-forgiving, all-forbearing. (101)
قَدْ سَأَلَهَا قَوْمٌ مِّن قَبْلِكُمْ ثُمَّ أَصْبَحُوا۟ بِهَا كَـٰفِرِينَ (۱٠۲)
Certainly some people asked about them before you and then came to disbelieve in them. (102)

Key Points

The Noble Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله was speaking to the people about Hajj. Someone asked: Is Hajj obligatory every year, or only once in a lifetime? The Prophet did not answer. He asked several times. The Messenger of Allah said: Why all this insistence? If I say every year, the matter will become difficult for you (199). Do not ask about a subject until I have brought it up. One of the factors leading to the destruction of past nations was their inappropriate questions (200). Although we must ask the people of knowledge about what we do not know, "فاسئلوا اهل الذّكر ان كنتم لاتعلمون" (201), there are some questions whose answers, if revealed, would be detrimental to the individual or society, such as asking about the faults of others, or military secrets. Examples and manifestations:

  • Officials should not disclose certain information they possess to the public. Such as economic issues like wheat shortages or...
  • Honesty is good, but frankness is not always beneficial.
  • News and information must be ranked and classified. (Jābir al-Juʿfī knew thousands of narrations from Imam Baqir عليه السلام which he did not have the right to disclose to all people (202).)
  • Some news should not be broadcast by media and newspapers, as it is detrimental to the people. (Sometimes silence and non-disclosure are wisely necessary) "ان تبدلكم تسؤكم"
  • Teachers and speakers must consider the capacity of their listeners when speaking.
  • In some cases, beliefs must be concealed using the method of Taqiyyah (dissimulation), and some questions should not be answered. "ان تبدلكم تسؤكم"
  • A person does not have the right to disclose the secrets of the people or military secrets.

199) Mustadrak, Vol. 8, p. 13. 200) Bihar, Vol. 1, p. 221. 201) Nahl, 43. 202) Mu'jam Rijāl al-Hadīth, Vol. 4, p. 22.

Messages

1- Knowing everything is neither necessary nor beneficial; rather, one must seek useful knowledge. "لاتسألوا" (The sense of curiosity must be moderated, and one should not pursue information that causes resentment, problems, or disruption of the social order.) 2- Not obligating people to certain rulings is a reflection of Divine pardon and forgiveness. "لاتسألوا... عفى اللَّه عنها" 3- Allah grants His servants a respite for repentance. "غفور حلیم" 4- If people lack the capacity, the disclosure of certain truths will lead to their disbelief. "قد سألها قوم من قبلكم ثمّ اصبحوا بها كافرین"

Ayah

مَا جَعَلَ ٱللَّهُ مِنۢ بَحِيرَةٍ وَلَا سَآئِبَةٍ وَلَا وَصِيلَةٍ وَلَا حَامٍ ۙ وَلَـٰكِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ يَفْتَرُونَ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ ٱلْكَذِبَ ۖ وَأَكْثَرُهُمْ لَا يَعْقِلُونَ (۱٠۳)
Allah has not prescribed any such thing as Bahirah, Sa’ibah, Waseelah, or Haam; but those who are faithless fabricate lies against Allah, and most of them do not exercise their reason. (103)

Key Points

Drawing near to Allah must be through correct and rational means. One cannot approach God through every vow or every path. The people of the Age of Ignorance (Jahiliyyah) believed that by releasing the aforementioned animals, they would draw near to God or the idols. We still observe a similar respect for animals, concerning the cow, in the country of India. It is mentioned in a narration that Bahīrah refers to a camel that has given birth five times. If the fifth offspring was female, they would slit its ear and consider eating its meat forbidden (harām). [203] Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: During the Age of Ignorance, if a camel gave birth to twins, they would call it Waṣīlah and considered the slaughter and eating of that camel's meat forbidden. If a camel gave birth ten times, they would call it Sā’ibah, and in that case, they considered riding it or eating it forbidden. And Ḥām was a male camel used for breeding (faḥl), whose slaughter they also did not consider lawful (ḥalāl). [204]

203) Wasā’il, Vol. 25, p. 61. 204) Wasā’il, Vol. 25, p. 61.

Messages

1- Religion must be purified from superstitions and innovations (bid‘ah). «ما جعل اللّه»

2- Unless a law regarding the prohibition of animals has been established by Allah, the default principle is their lawfulness (ḥalāl). «ما جعل اللّه»

3- Wasting wealth and releasing an animal is a form of fabricating a lie against Allah and is forbidden (harām). «یفترون على اللّه الكذب» (They fabricate lies against Allah). (Releasing an animal and leaving it unused is not permissible, let alone abandoning a human being.)

4- Innovation (bid‘ah) in religion is disbelief (kufr). «لكن الّذین كفروا یفترون...» (But those who disbelieve fabricate...). A disbeliever (kāfir) is not only someone who denies God; those who accept God but fabricate lies against Him are also disbelievers.

5- The root of Divine rulings lies in real benefits (maṣāliḥ), but superstitions are rooted in ignorance and lack of reason. «لایعقلون»

6- If the majority of society possesses reason, innovations will not flourish. «اكثرهم لایعقلون»

Ayah

وَإِذَا قِيلَ لَهُمْ تَعَالَوْا۟ إِلَىٰ مَآ أَنزَلَ ٱللَّهُ وَإِلَى ٱلرَّسُولِ قَالُوا۟ حَسْبُنَا مَا وَجَدْنَا عَلَيْهِ ءَابَآءَنَآ ۚ أَوَلَوْ كَانَ ءَابَآؤُهُمْ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ شَيْـًٔا وَلَا يَهْتَدُونَ (۱٠٤)
When they are told, ‘Come to what Allah has sent down and [come] to the Apostle,’ they say, ‘Sufficient for us is what we have found our fathers following.’ What, even if their fathers did not know anything and were not guided?! (104)

Key Points

| Perhaps the Ayah relates to the superstitions mentioned in the previous Ayah, where whenever they are told to abandon these superstitions, they say: «وجدنا علیه اباءنا»

Messages

| 1- The Quran calls people to follow the commands of Allah and the Messenger. «تعالوا الى...» | 2- Accepting the call of Islam is a cause for elevation and growth. (The word «تَعالَوا» means movement towards loftiness and growth.) | 3- The foundation is the Divine culture, not the culture of predecessors. «ما أنزل اللّه» | 4- The Quran alone is not sufficient; the Sunnah, the conduct (Sirah), and the governance of the Messenger of Allah are also criteria for action. «تعالوا الى ما انزل اللّه و الى الرسول» | 5- People of superstition, reactionaries, and regressives are not willing to listen to the truth. «قالوا حسبنا» | 6- Neither traditionalism nor modernism is the foundation; the foundation is knowledge and guidance. «لایعلمون، لایهتدون» | 7- Let us make our conscience the judge. «أولو كان آباؤهم...» | 8- Respect and reverence for predecessors, yes; following their ignorant ideas and misplaced loyalty, no. «او لو كان آباؤهم لا یعلمون» | 9- Blind imitation is a sign of lack of intellect. «لایعقلون» in the previous Ayah, and fanaticism regarding ancestors in this Ayah. | 10- The imitation of an ignorant person by another ignorant person is meaningless. «وجدنا علیه آباءنا... آباؤهم لایعلمون» | 11- An ignorant person who accepts guidance is harmless. The danger lies where the ignorant do not accept guidance. «لایعلمون، لایهتدون»

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ عَلَيْكُمْ أَنفُسَكُمْ ۖ لَا يَضُرُّكُم مَّن ضَلَّ إِذَا ٱهْتَدَيْتُمْ ۚ إِلَى ٱللَّهِ مَرْجِعُكُمْ جَمِيعًا فَيُنَبِّئُكُم بِمَا كُنتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ (۱٠٥)
O you who have faith! Take care of your own souls. He who strays cannot hurt you if you are guided. To Allah will be the return of you all, whereat He will inform you concerning what you used to do. (105)

Key Points

Some have used this Ayah as a pretext, saying: the duty of every person is only to protect himself, and we are not responsible for the sins of others or for enjoining them (to good) and forbidding them (from evil)! In response to them, we say: Given the numerous verses and narrations (Ahadith) which all indicate the obligation of Amr bil Ma'ruf (enjoining good) and Nahy anil Munkar (forbidding evil), it must be stated that the intent of this Ayah is not to abandon these two duties. Rather, the intent is that if enjoining and forbidding took place but had no effect, then you have no further duty except to protect yourselves. [205] Furthermore, protecting society from sin through Amr bil Ma'ruf and Nahy anil Munkar is itself one of the manifestations of "protecting oneself."

205) We read this meaning in a narration from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله as well. Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- Do not fear loneliness on the path of truth. «عليكم أنفسكم لا يضركم»

2- If we cannot overcome others, let us overcome our own selves (nafs). «عليكم أنفسكم...»

3- The deviation of society does not license individuals to commit sin. «عليكم أنفسكم لا يضرّكم...»

4- On the Day of Judgment, everyone is responsible for his own deeds. «عليكم أنفسكم»

5- Do not seek to discover and expose the faults of others. [206] «عليكم أنفسكم»

6- First self-building, then society-building. «عليكم أنفسكم»

7- The people of faith will not be held accountable for the wrongful conduct and false beliefs of the misguided. «عليكم أنفسكم»

8- In the path of saving others, do not drown yourselves. «عليكم أنفسكم لا يضرّكم»

9- We must be trained in such a way that the corruption of the environment and society does not affect us. «لا يضرّكم»

10- No one from outside can harm you. If you suffer defeat, it is from yourselves. «عليكم أنفسكم لا يضرّكم من ضلّ»

11- Do not lose your morale because of the deviation of others. «عليكم أنفسكم لا يضرّكم من ضلّ»

12- The path of Islam is so logical and firm that the doubts and deviations of others cannot undermine it. «لا يضرّكم من ضلّ إذا اهتديتم»

13- Although the deviants are lying in wait, the way to attain immunity is through accepting guidance. «لا يضرّكم من ضلّ إذا اهتديتم»

14- Belief in the Resurrection (Ma'ad) is a factor in self-building. «إلى اللّه مرجعكم»

15- Following ancestors and blindly imitating others will not save a person on the Day of Judgment; rather, everyone must be accountable for his own path and action. «عليكم أنفسكم... فينبّئكم»

16- A person's behavior and deeds in this world clarify his ultimate outcome in the Hereafter. «فينبّئكم بما كنتم تعملون»

206) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ شَهَـٰدَةُ بَيْنِكُمْ إِذَا حَضَرَ أَحَدَكُمُ ٱلْمَوْتُ حِينَ ٱلْوَصِيَّةِ ٱثْنَانِ ذَوَا عَدْلٍ مِّنكُمْ أَوْ ءَاخَرَانِ مِنْ غَيْرِكُمْ إِنْ أَنتُمْ ضَرَبْتُمْ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ فَأَصَـٰبَتْكُم مُّصِيبَةُ ٱلْمَوْتِ ۚ تَحْبِسُونَهُمَا مِنۢ بَعْدِ ٱلصَّلَوٰةِ فَيُقْسِمَانِ بِٱللَّهِ إِنِ ٱرْتَبْتُمْ لَا نَشْتَرِى بِهِۦ ثَمَنًا وَلَوْ كَانَ ذَا قُرْبَىٰ ۙ وَلَا نَكْتُمُ شَهَـٰدَةَ ٱللَّهِ إِنَّآ إِذًا لَّمِنَ ٱلْـَٔاثِمِينَ (۱٠٦)
O you who have faith! The witness between you, when death approaches any of you, while making a bequest, shall be two fair men from among yourselves—or two from among others, if you are journeying in the land and the affliction of death visits you. You shall detain the two of them after the prayer, and, if you have any doubt, they shall vow by Allah, ‘We will not sell it for any gain, even if it were a relative, nor will we conceal the testimony of Allah, for then we would indeed be among the sinners.’ (106)

Key Points

A Muslim named "Ibn Abi Mariyah," accompanied by two Christians named Tamim and Adiyy, who were brothers, went on a trade journey. The Muslim fell ill, wrote a will, hid it among his belongings, and entrusted his wealth to the two Christians to deliver to his heirs. After his death, the two men saw valuable goods among his possessions and took them. After returning to Medina, they gave the remainder of the wealth to the heirs. The heirs found the will among the belongings, which listed all the possessions. When they demanded the missing items, the two Christians denied it. They took the complaint to the Prophet, and it was at this time that the above Ayah was revealed. [207] According to a Hadith from Usul al-Kafi, the Prophet took an oath from the two and acquitted them. However, when their lie was discovered through the letter (the will), the Prophet summoned them again. The heirs swore an oath that there were other goods, and they recovered them. [208]

207) Tafsir al-Mizan. 208) Tafsir Namuneh.

Messages

1- The threshold of death is the last opportunity to make a will. «اذا حضر أحدكم الموت حین الوصیة»

2- Death is the same for all people. «حضر أحدكم الموت»

3- When making a will, the believer must exercise the necessary caution. «حین الوصیّة اثنان ذوا عدل منكم»

4- Take two just witnesses to fulfill the rights of the people. «اثنان ذوا عدل»

5- To fulfill the rights of the people, if a Muslim is not present, secure the matter with a non-Muslim. «غیركم» (But a non-Muslim who accepts God so that he can swear by His sacred Name) «فیقسمان باللَّه»

6- Observing the rights of the people is important everywhere and is not restricted by location. «ضربتم فى الارض»

7- Eliminate any doubt when paying a right. «تحبسونهما... ان ارتبتم»

8- An oath is one of the ways to remove doubt. «فیقسمان»

9- Only swearing by the Name of "Allah" is valuable and valid. «فیقسمان باللّه»

10- To fulfill a right, we should seek help from religious and celestial settings, and sacred places and times. [209] «من بعد الصلوة فیقسمان باللَّه»

11- The best opportunities must be utilized to fulfill a right. «بعدالصلاة» (Prayer plays a role in purity and avoiding lies.)

12- One of the factors leading to deviation is the pursuit of money. «لانشترى به ثمناً»

13- One of the factors leading to deviation is familial love and affection. «لانشترى به ثمناً و لو كان ذاقربى»

14- The only instance where the text of the "oath" has been dictated through revelation is concerning the "rights of the people" (Haqq an-Nas). «لانشترى ...»

15- Concealing the truth and betraying trust in testimony turns a just person into a transgressor (fasiq) and is considered forbidden in all divine religions. («ذواعدل», becomes «من الآثمین»)

209) Imam Ali عليه السلام told a person who requested funds from the public treasury (Bayt al-Mal) beyond his normal share to come to him on Friday. He then took him to the Friday prayer and showed him the congregation, saying: "The meaning of your undue request is theft from the wealth of these people." In this scene, we see that the Imam utilized the prayer, the congregation, and Friday to make His luminous words impactful.

Ayah

فَإِنْ عُثِرَ عَلَىٰٓ أَنَّهُمَا ٱسْتَحَقَّآ إِثْمًا فَـَٔاخَرَانِ يَقُومَانِ مَقَامَهُمَا مِنَ ٱلَّذِينَ ٱسْتَحَقَّ عَلَيْهِمُ ٱلْأَوْلَيَـٰنِ فَيُقْسِمَانِ بِٱللَّهِ لَشَهَـٰدَتُنَآ أَحَقُّ مِن شَهَـٰدَتِهِمَا وَمَا ٱعْتَدَيْنَآ إِنَّآ إِذًا لَّمِنَ ٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ (۱٠۷)
But if it is found that both of them were guilty of a sin, then two others shall stand up in their place from among those nearest in kinship to the claimants and swear by Allah: ‘Our testimony is surely truer than their testimony, and we have not transgressed, for then we would indeed be among the wrongdoers.’ (107)

Key Points

The word 'uthūr' (عثور) means awareness without investigation. [210] It should be noted that the testimony and oath of the deceased's awliyā' (guardians/closest kin) are based on information they previously possessed regarding his property during travel or otherwise. Two meanings can be assigned to the word 'awliyān' (اَوْلیان) in the Ayah: A: Two heirs against whom testimony has been given, but who are closer and have priority (awlā) to the deceased because they are heirs. B: The reference is to the two witnesses whom the deceased requested to bear witness and who were present at the moment of death, and who gained priority (awlawiyyah) due to this presence or request. Therefore, the translation is as follows: If it becomes known that the first two persons committed a sin, then two other persons should stand up to testify in their place, and those two persons should be from among those against whom the first two witnesses committed the sin. The testimony and witnessing must come from both persons and be sworn by the blessed Name of Allah, and the content of the oath must be what is stated in the Ayah: firstly, that our oath is truthful and we do not intend to transgress the rights of the people; secondly, if our oath was false, we admit to our own wrongdoing.

210) Mufradāt al-Rāghib.

Messages

1- You do not have the right to investigate or pry, but if information is discovered, the duty changes. «فان عُثر...» 2- False testimony is a form of transgression and injustice against the rights of the people. «اعتدینا... لمن الظالمین»

Ayah

ذَٰلِكَ أَدْنَىٰٓ أَن يَأْتُوا۟ بِٱلشَّهَـٰدَةِ عَلَىٰ وَجْهِهَآ أَوْ يَخَافُوٓا۟ أَن تُرَدَّ أَيْمَـٰنٌۢ بَعْدَ أَيْمَـٰنِهِمْ ۗ وَٱتَّقُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ وَٱسْمَعُوا۟ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ لَا يَهْدِى ٱلْقَوْمَ ٱلْفَـٰسِقِينَ (۱٠۸)
That makes it likelier that they give the testimony in its genuine form, or fear that other oaths will be taken after their oaths. Be wary of Allah and listen, and Allah does not guide the transgressing lot. (108)

Key Points

This verse explains the philosophy behind the strictness and precision in the matter of testimony and taking witnesses, which was discussed in the previous verses. It explains that taking an oath after the prayer in the presence of the people causes the testimonies to be truthful, because if their oath and testimony are not accepted, their reputation in society will be ruined.

Messages

1- Ceremonies and formalities that serve to preserve and establish the rights of the people are valuable. «ذلك أدنى أن ...» 2- One of the deterrents against sin is the fear of disgrace in society. «أو یخافوا أن تُرّد ایمان» 3- We should live in such a way that the unworthy who pretend to be just cannot waste our efforts with false oaths, and they should know that their incorrect statements will be rejected by a better group. «أن تردّ ایمان بعد ایمانهم» 4- In the matter of will, oath, and testimony, we must possess piety (Taqwa). «واتّقوا اللَّه» 5- Unjust testimony is a sign of transgression (Fisq). «واللّه لا یهدى القوم الفاسقین»

Ayah

۞ يَوْمَ يَجْمَعُ ٱللَّهُ ٱلرُّسُلَ فَيَقُولُ مَاذَآ أُجِبْتُمْ ۖ قَالُوا۟ لَا عِلْمَ لَنَآ ۖ إِنَّكَ أَنتَ عَلَّـٰمُ ٱلْغُيُوبِ (۱٠۹)
The day Allah will gather the apostles and say, ‘What was the response to you?’ They will say, ‘We have no knowledge. Indeed You are knower of all that is Unseen.’ (109)

Key Points

Imam Baqir عليه السلام, regarding the interpretation (ta'wil) of this Ayah, said: Allah's questioning of the Prophets is a question concerning their successors (awsiya) who were the deputies of the Prophets. The Divine Messengers will also reply: We have no knowledge concerning what they did regarding our successors after us. [211] True knowledge belongs to Allah, and whoever possesses knowledge, possesses it from Him, just as only He knows the Unseen (Ghayb) and He grants that knowledge to whomever He wills.

211) Kafi, Vol. 8, 338.

Messages

1- On the Day of Judgment, the Prophets will be questioned about how the people behaved towards them. «ماذا اجبتم»

2- The knowledge of the Prophets is nothing compared to Divine knowledge. «لا علم لنا»

Ayah

إِذْ قَالَ ٱللَّهُ يَـٰعِيسَى ٱبْنَ مَرْيَمَ ٱذْكُرْ نِعْمَتِى عَلَيْكَ وَعَلَىٰ وَٰلِدَتِكَ إِذْ أَيَّدتُّكَ بِرُوحِ ٱلْقُدُسِ تُكَلِّمُ ٱلنَّاسَ فِى ٱلْمَهْدِ وَكَهْلًا ۖ وَإِذْ عَلَّمْتُكَ ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ وَٱلْحِكْمَةَ وَٱلتَّوْرَىٰةَ وَٱلْإِنجِيلَ ۖ وَإِذْ تَخْلُقُ مِنَ ٱلطِّينِ كَهَيْـَٔةِ ٱلطَّيْرِ بِإِذْنِى فَتَنفُخُ فِيهَا فَتَكُونُ طَيْرًۢا بِإِذْنِى ۖ وَتُبْرِئُ ٱلْأَكْمَهَ وَٱلْأَبْرَصَ بِإِذْنِى ۖ وَإِذْ تُخْرِجُ ٱلْمَوْتَىٰ بِإِذْنِى ۖ وَإِذْ كَفَفْتُ بَنِىٓ إِسْرَٰٓءِيلَ عَنكَ إِذْ جِئْتَهُم بِٱلْبَيِّنَـٰتِ فَقَالَ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ مِنْهُمْ إِنْ هَـٰذَآ إِلَّا سِحْرٌ مُّبِينٌ (۱۱٠)
When Allah will say, O Jesus son of Mary, remember My blessing upon you and upon your mother, when I strengthened you with the Holy Spirit, so you would speak to the people in the cradle and in adulthood, and when I taught you the Book and wisdom, the Torah and the Evangel, and when you would create from clay the form of a bird, with My leave, and you would breathe into it and it would become a bird, with My leave; and you would heal the blind and the leper, with My leave, and you would raise the dead, with My leave; and when I held off [the evil of] the Children of Israel from you when you brought them manifest proofs, whereat the faithless among them said, ‘This is nothing but plain magic.’ (110)

Key Points

From this Ayah until the end of the Surah, the discussion is about Prophet Jesus عليه السلام. In this Ayah, various types of Divine favor are mentioned, starting with the confirmation (or support) by the Holy Spirit (Ruh al-Qudus) regarding Christ. At the beginning of the Ayah, He says: O Jesus! Remember the blessings I bestowed upon you and upon your mother. However, all the blessings visible in the Ayah relate only to Prophet Jesus (not his mother). Perhaps this is because a blessing given to a child is, in reality, a blessing to the mother. Or perhaps the intent of the Ayah refers to the blessings mentioned about Lady Maryam in Surah Aal-Imran. [212] Perhaps the intent of "the Book" (al-Kitab) is books other than the Torah and the Gospel (Injil), and the names of the Torah and the Gospel are mentioned separately due to their importance, even though they are also books. They asked Imam Ali عليه السلام: What is the animal that came into existence without being placed in a womb (or an egg)? He replied: The bird that Prophet Jesus brought into existence by breathing into the statue. [213]

212) Aal-Imran, 42 to 45. 213) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- Remembering Allah's favors upon His chosen ones is a source of encouragement for those who follow the path of truth. «اذ»

2- Even the Prophets should not neglect remembering Allah's blessings. «يا عيسى ابن مريم اذكر...»

3- A woman reaches a status where she is mentioned alongside a Prophet. «عليك و على والدتك». Moreover, a woman and her Prophet son are counted together as one sign (Ayah). «و جعلناها وابنها آية» [214]

4- Prophet Jesus, by speaking in the cradle, proved both his own prophethood and the chastity and infallibility of his mother. «تُكلّم النّاس فى المهد»

5- Jesus's words in the cradle were a manifestation of revelation. «أيّدتك بروح القدس تكلّم...»

6- The Divine Will resolves the need for experience, power, and the passage of time. Prophet Jesus, without experience, practice, or the expenditure of time, speaks the same correct words in childhood that He speaks in maturity, not childish talk. «فى المهد وكهلاً»

7- Prophets must possess knowledge «الكتاب», insight «الحكمة», knowledge of the sayings of their predecessors «التوراة», and a new message. «الانجيل»

8- The expression of creation (khalq) is also attributed to others besides Allah. «إذ تخلق»

9- Allah's permission was the justification for making statues of birds. «تخلق من الطّين كهيئة الطير بإذنى»

10- Allah's special chosen ones possess cosmic authority (Wilayah Takwini). (The phrases «تخلق», «تنفخ», «تبرء» and «تخرج» are addressed to Prophet Jesus.)

11- In the miracle of Jesus عليه السلام, both his Christ-like breath (Nafs Masihayi) and the art of statue-making played a role. «كهيئة الطير ... فتنفخ»

12- The Christ-like breath made an inanimate object fly, but it did not affect the hearts of the Children of Israel. «فتنفخ فيها فتكون طيراً»

13- Where the danger of polytheism (Shirk) is high, the repetition of Monotheism (Tawhid) is necessary. (The repetition of «تخلق... بإذنى... تبرء... بإذنى... تخرج الموتى بإذنى»)

14- When Allah grants the power of revival and healing to the Prophets, the people's seeking of intercession (Tawassul) and aid must also be permissible. «تخلق من الطين... فتكون طيراً بإذنى و تبرء الأكمه و الأبرص بإذنى و إذ تخرج الموتى بإذنى» (Is it possible for Allah to grant power to someone, yet forbid people from paying attention to it?!)

15- The revival of the dead and the "Return" (Raj'ah) have taken place in this very world. «تخرج الموتى»

16- The assassination attempt by the Children of Israel against Prophet Jesus عليه السلام was repelled by Allah. «كففت بنى إسرائيل عنك»

214) Al-Anbiya, 91.

Ayah

وَإِذْ أَوْحَيْتُ إِلَى ٱلْحَوَارِيِّـۧنَ أَنْ ءَامِنُوا۟ بِى وَبِرَسُولِى قَالُوٓا۟ ءَامَنَّا وَٱشْهَدْ بِأَنَّنَا مُسْلِمُونَ (۱۱۱)
And when I inspired the Disciples, [saying], ‘Have faith in Me and My apostle,’ they said, ‘We have faith. Bear witness that we are Muslims.’ (111)

Key Points

The meaning of Wahy (revelation) to the Disciples (al-Hawariyyun) might be inspiration (Ilham) to their own hearts, not a message of revelation through Prophet Jesus عليه السلام. Just as Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said regarding this revelation: "اُلهموا" (ulhimū), meaning they were inspired. [215] According to Imam Reza عليه السلام, the number of Disciples was twelve, and the reason for naming them al-Hawariyyun was their inner purity and their cleansing of society from sins through admonition and remembrance (of God). [216]

215) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 216) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- Sometimes Allah inspires receptive hearts. «أوْحَيْتُ إِلَى الْحَوَارِيِّينَ»

2- Faith in Allah is inseparable from faith in His Messenger. «آمِنُوا بِي وَبِرَسُولِي»

3- When guidance is internal and divine, its effect is deep and rapid. «أوْحَيْتُ ... قَالُوا آمَنَّا»

4- Guidance (Irshad) without divine light from within is either ineffective, minimally effective, or unstable. «أوْحَيْتُ... آمَنَّا وَاشْهَدْ بِأَنَّنَا مُسْلِمُونَ»

5- Divine inspirations to people are in the path of confirming the revelation (Wahy) of the prophets, not in opposition to them. «أوْحَيْتُ... آمِنُوا بِي وَبِرَسُولِي»

6- The sign of inner faith is verbal declaration and affirmation, just as heartfelt faith is the support for outward slogans. «وَاشْهَدْ بِأَنَّنَا مُسْلِمُونَ»

Ayahs

إِذْ قَالَ ٱلْحَوَارِيُّونَ يَـٰعِيسَى ٱبْنَ مَرْيَمَ هَلْ يَسْتَطِيعُ رَبُّكَ أَن يُنَزِّلَ عَلَيْنَا مَآئِدَةً مِّنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ ۖ قَالَ ٱتَّقُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ إِن كُنتُم مُّؤْمِنِينَ (۱۱۲)
When the Disciples said, ‘O Jesus son of Mary! Can your Lord send down to us a table from the sky?’ Said he, ‘Be wary of Allah, should you be faithful.’ (112)
قَالُوا۟ نُرِيدُ أَن نَّأْكُلَ مِنْهَا وَتَطْمَئِنَّ قُلُوبُنَا وَنَعْلَمَ أَن قَدْ صَدَقْتَنَا وَنَكُونَ عَلَيْهَا مِنَ ٱلشَّـٰهِدِينَ (۱۱۳)
They said, ‘We desire to eat from it, and our hearts will be at rest: we shall know that you have told us the truth, and we shall be among the witnesses to it.’ (113)

Key Points

The naming of this Surah as "Al-Ma'idah" (The Table Spread) is due to this very request for the heavenly Ma'idah. Ma'idah means both food and the table spread upon which food is placed. Because the manner in which the Disciples (al-Hawariyyun) questioned Jesus عليه السلام was somewhat impolite, instead of saying, "Ya Rasul Allah" (O Messenger of Allah), they said, "Ya 'Isa" (O Jesus); and instead of asking, "Will God bestow His favor?" they asked, "Can He do it?" (آیا مى تواند؟); and instead of saying, "Our Lord," they said, "Your Lord." Consequently, they received the response, "اتّقوا اللّه" (Fear Allah).

Messages

1- O Prophet! Do not expect too much from people! Even the Disciples of Jesus, despite being inspired and acknowledging their faith (Iman) and submission (Islam), still requested miracles according to their desires. "اذ قال الحواریّون..."

2- Doubting the power of Allah ("هل یستطیع ربّك"), after witnessing all those miracles performed by Prophet 'Isa (Jesus)—speaking in the cradle and raising the dead—and then requesting a new miracle, was not befitting the Disciples. "اتّقوا اللّه"

3- Even if we do not harbor ill intentions, we must maintain respect for individuals in our addresses and conversations. "اتّقوا اللّه"

4- A believer should not test God. "هل یستطیع ربّك... اتّقوا اللَّه ان كنتم مؤمنین"

5- Piety (Taqwa) is a sign of faith (Iman). "اتّقوا اللّه ان كنتم مؤمنین"

6- Tranquility of the heart (itmi'nan-e qalbi) is a stage higher than faith (Iman). "اتّقواللَّه ان كنتم مؤمنین قالوا نرید أن نأكل منها و تطمئنّ قلوبنا" Prophet Ibrahim (Abraham), in response to Allah's question, "أو لم تؤمن" (Do you not believe?), said, "بلى و لكن لیطمئنّ قلبى" (Yes, but so that my heart may be tranquil). (217)

(217) Al-Baqarah, 260.

Ayah

قَالَ عِيسَى ٱبْنُ مَرْيَمَ ٱللَّهُمَّ رَبَّنَآ أَنزِلْ عَلَيْنَا مَآئِدَةً مِّنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ تَكُونُ لَنَا عِيدًا لِّأَوَّلِنَا وَءَاخِرِنَا وَءَايَةً مِّنكَ ۖ وَٱرْزُقْنَا وَأَنتَ خَيْرُ ٱلرَّٰزِقِينَ (۱۱٤)
Said Jesus son of Mary, ‘O Allah! Our Lord! Send down to us a table from the sky, to be a festival for us, for the first ones and the last ones among us and as a sign from You, and provide for us; for You are the best of providers.’ (114)

Key Points

| Usually, the supplications (Du'as) in the Quran begin with the Name "Rabb" (Lord), but in this Ayah, it appears with the two words "Allāhumma Rabbanā" (O Allah, our Lord). Perhaps this is due to the importance of this event and its consequences.

Messages

| 1- Seeking intercession (Tawassul) through the friends of Allah (Awliyā') for the fulfillment of needs is permissible. «قال الحواریّون یا عیسى.... قال عیسى ابن مریم اللّهم....» | 2- For the Prophets, the people of history and future generations are important. «لاوّلنا و آخرنا» | 3- Lessons must be learned from the divine signs forever. «و آیة منك» | 4- Religious festivals and celebrations (Eid) are considered correct actions according to the Quran. «تكون لنا عیداً لاوّلنا و آخرنا» (The birth of the friends of Allah and the appointment (Bi'that) of the Prophet are no less significant than the descent of the heavenly table.) | 5- In his supplication, Prophet 'Īsā (Jesus) focuses on the divine nature of the table (Mā'idah) rather than merely the issue of eating. «آیةً منك» | 6- We should quote the disrespectful expressions of others in a corrected form. (Their question was: «هل یستطیع ربّك»? [Can your Lord do this?], but in his supplication, Prophet 'Īsā عليه السلام decisively requested the table for a higher purpose, omitting what indicated doubt and disrespect.) | 7- In supplication, we should call upon God with complete reverence and with the Attribute appropriate to the request. The beginning of the Ayah is «الّلهم ربّنا» and the end of the Ayah is «خیر الرّازقین». | 8- We should give spiritual direction to material requests. (The Disciples stated their first goal as eating and then gaining assurance «نأكل منها و تطمئن», but Prophet 'Īsā first mentioned leaving a sign of joy for history «عیداً لاوّلنا و آخرنا», then a sign of Allah's power «وآیة منك», and only in the third stage did He raise the issue of sustenance «وارزقنا و انت خیر الرّازقین». In this way, He made everyone understand that spiritual and social matters precede group and economic matters.) | 9- We should turn our attention to Allah and not seek others. «و انت خیر الرّازقین»

Ayah

قَالَ ٱللَّهُ إِنِّى مُنَزِّلُهَا عَلَيْكُمْ ۖ فَمَن يَكْفُرْ بَعْدُ مِنكُمْ فَإِنِّىٓ أُعَذِّبُهُۥ عَذَابًا لَّآ أُعَذِّبُهُۥٓ أَحَدًا مِّنَ ٱلْعَـٰلَمِينَ (۱۱٥)
Allah said, ‘I will indeed send it down to you. But should any of you disbelieve after this, I will indeed punish him with a punishment such as I do not punish anyone in all creation.’ (115)

Key Points

The story of the descent of the heavenly table (Mā'idah), as narrated in the Quran, is not mentioned in the Gospel. [218] Some have said that upon hearing the Divine threat, the disciples (Hawāriyyūn) withdrew their request and the table did not descend. However, this contradicts the apparent meaning of the Ayah and the narrations. The phrase «انّى منزّلها» (I am sending it down) signifies a definite descent, not merely a promise of descent. [219] It is mentioned in narrations that after the descent of the table, some became disbelievers and were transformed into pigs. [220] In some narrations, we read that what descended was bread and meat. [221] If heavenly food descended for the companions of Jesus عليه السلام, then according to narrations, heavenly fruit also descended for the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله, which became the origin of the creation of Fatimah al-Zahra عليها السلام. [222]

218) Tafsir-e Nemooneh. 219) Tafsir-e Atyab al-Bayan. 220) Tafsir al-Mizan. 221) Tafasir Noor al-Thaqalayn and Majma' al-Bayan. 222) Bihar, Vol. 43, p. 5.

Messages

1- The prayers of the prophets are answered. «ربّنا انزل علینا... انّى منزّلها»

2- Those who attain knowledge, certainty, and direct witnessing (shuhūd) bear a heavier responsibility, and the punishment for their transgression is more severe. «فمن یكفر بعد منكم...» Therefore, those who have not reached the station of knowledge and witnessing are further removed from Allah's wrath.

3- Allah's punishment has degrees. «عذاباً لا اُعذبه احداً»

4- He who expects more and asks for a heavenly table (Mā'idah) must also bear a greater commitment. «مائدة من السماء... عذاباً لا اُعذبه احداً»

Ayah

وَإِذْ قَالَ ٱللَّهُ يَـٰعِيسَى ٱبْنَ مَرْيَمَ ءَأَنتَ قُلْتَ لِلنَّاسِ ٱتَّخِذُونِى وَأُمِّىَ إِلَـٰهَيْنِ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ ۖ قَالَ سُبْحَـٰنَكَ مَا يَكُونُ لِىٓ أَنْ أَقُولَ مَا لَيْسَ لِى بِحَقٍّ ۚ إِن كُنتُ قُلْتُهُۥ فَقَدْ عَلِمْتَهُۥ ۚ تَعْلَمُ مَا فِى نَفْسِى وَلَآ أَعْلَمُ مَا فِى نَفْسِكَ ۚ إِنَّكَ أَنتَ عَلَّـٰمُ ٱلْغُيُوبِ (۱۱٦)
And when Allah will say, ‘O Jesus son of Mary! Was it you who said to the people, ‘‘Take me and my mother for gods besides Allah’’?’ He will say, ‘Immaculate are You! It does not behoove me to say what I have no right to [say]. Had I said it, You would certainly have known it: You know whatever is in my self, and I do not know what is in Your Self. Indeed, You are knower of all that is Unseen. (116)

Key Points

In Ayah 109 of this very Surah, we read that Allah will gather the Prophets on the Day of Judgment and ask them: "What response did you receive from the people?" This Ayah describes the conversation between God and Prophet Jesus (عليه السلام) on that Day. Although Christians today do not consider Mary (عليه السلام) to be God, the term Ilah (deity) is used because a group of them at that time believed in Mary's divinity, or because worshiping her statue is tantamount to taking her as a deity. The phrase «اتّخذونى و اُمّى الهین» (Did you tell people to take Me and My mother as two gods) «دون اللّه» (besides Allah) is a sign of Shirk (polytheism), not the negation of God. That is, considering Jesus and Mary as deities in addition to God constitutes Shirk. Of course, the current Christian Trinity consists of the Father, the Son, and the Holy Spirit. Imam Reza (عليه السلام) said: Two groups will perish concerning me, and I am not to blame: one group consists of the extremist devotees, and the other consists of the baseless haters. [223] The glorification (Tasbih) by Prophet Christ (عليه السلام) is itself another sign that Allah is exalted above having any partner. It is mentioned in other Ayahs: «وقالوا اتّخذ الرّحمن ولداً سبحانه» [224] (And they say: "The Most Gracious has taken a son." Glory be to Him!), and «ویجعلون للّه البنات سبحانه» [225] (And they assign daughters to Allah. Glory be to Him!), which declare God exalted above having any kind of offspring, whether daughters or sons.

223) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 224) Al-Anbiya, 26. 225) An-Nahl, 57.

Messages

1- Sometimes the questioning and reprimand of an innocent person is meant as a warning to others. «ءأنت قلت» (Did you say?) Sometimes they speak to the door so the wall may hear.

2- The claim of divinity for a human being, even if He is a Prophet, is a false claim. «ما یكون لى ان اقول ما لیس لى بحقّ» (It is not for me to say what I have no right to say.)

3- Prophets are infallible (Ma'sum). «مایكون لى أن أقول ما لیس لى بحقّ»

4- Prophets disavow the extremism (ghuluww) of their followers. «ان كنت قلته فقد علمته» (If I had said it, You would have known it.)

5- All of man's sayings and the secrets within his heart are clear and known to Allah. «فقد علمته تعلم ما فى نفسى» (You would have known it; You know what is within my soul.)

6- Glorifying (Tasbih) and exalting God above every inappropriate attribution is necessary. «سبحانك» (Glory be to You!)

7- The knowledge of the Prophets is limited. «لا اعلم ما فى نفسك» (I do not know what is within Yourself.) And whoever's knowledge is limited is not worthy of divinity.

8- The thoughts and secrets of people are among the unseen matters (ghayb) of which God is aware. «تعلم ما فى نفسى... انّك انت علاّم الغیوب» (You know what is within my soul... indeed, You are the Knower of the unseen.)

9- Complete and extensive awareness of all realities belongs exclusively to God. «انّك انت علاّم الغیوب» (Indeed, You are the Knower of the unseen.)

Ayah

مَا قُلْتُ لَهُمْ إِلَّا مَآ أَمَرْتَنِى بِهِۦٓ أَنِ ٱعْبُدُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ رَبِّى وَرَبَّكُمْ ۚ وَكُنتُ عَلَيْهِمْ شَهِيدًا مَّا دُمْتُ فِيهِمْ ۖ فَلَمَّا تَوَفَّيْتَنِى كُنتَ أَنتَ ٱلرَّقِيبَ عَلَيْهِمْ ۚ وَأَنتَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ شَهِيدٌ (۱۱۷)
I did not say to them [anything] except what You had commanded me [to say]: ‘‘Worship Allah, my Lord and your Lord.’’ And I was a witness to them so long as I was among them. But when You had taken me away, You Yourself were watchful over them, and You are witness to all things. (117)

Key Points

The term Tawaffā means the complete and full taking or receiving, and death is one instance of it. Allah took Jesus (عليه السلام) from among his people and keeps him alive until the time of the advent of Imam Mahdi (عليه السلام), and He will appear alongside that Imam.

Messages

1- Prophets are infallible and do nothing except by the command of Allah; they do not alter or manipulate the revelation. «ما قلت لهم الاّ ما أمرتنى به»

2- Jesus considers Himself, like others, to be nurtured by Allah. «ربّى و ربّكم»

3- Prophets are witnesses over the actions of the people. «كنت علیهم شهیداً»

4- Invitation to monotheism is the most important duty of the Prophets. «ان اعبدوا اللَّه» (Although the mission of Jesus was not solely monotheism, the Ayah only mentions monotheism.)

5- The reason and criterion for worshipping Allah is His Lordship. «ان اعبدواللَّه ربّى و ربّكم»

Ayah

إِن تُعَذِّبْهُمْ فَإِنَّهُمْ عِبَادُكَ ۖ وَإِن تَغْفِرْ لَهُمْ فَإِنَّكَ أَنتَ ٱلْعَزِيزُ ٱلْحَكِيمُ (۱۱۸)
If You punish them, they are indeed Your creatures; but if You forgive them, You are indeed the All-mighty, the All-wise.’ (118)

Key Points

In response to Allah, Who asked: "Did you tell the people to take me and my mother as gods?" Jesus (عليه السلام), by distancing himself from them and affirming that only Allah is their Owner, stated that he had no role in the polytheistic tendencies of his community. According to a narration by Abu Dharr, the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله recited this Ayah throughout one night until morning, while in Ruku' (bowing) and Sujud (prostration). I asked the Prophet how it was that He recited this Ayah until morning. The Prophet replied: "I was asking Allah for intercession for My Ummah, and Allah finally granted it to Me, and My Ummah will attain it, provided they do not commit Shirk (polytheism)." [226] In another narration, it is mentioned that when the Prophet recited this Ayah, He raised His blessed hands and, weeping, said: «اللّهم امّتى» (O Allah, my Ummah), and prayed for His community. Allah sent Gabriel to the Prophet and said: «انّا سنرضیك فى امّتك و لا نسوئك» (We shall surely satisfy you concerning your Ummah and will not let you be distressed). [227]

226) Tafsir Maraghi. 227) Tafsir Ibn Kathir.

Messages

1- The Prophets are submissive to their Lord. «ان تعذّبهم... و ان تغفر لهم» (If You punish them... and if You forgive them). One cannot question Allah's actions: «لایسئل عمّا یفعل» [228] 2- The punishment or forgiveness of polytheists is in Allah's hands. «ان تعذّبهم...و ان تغفر لهم» 3- Prophets hold the status of intercession, but sometimes the sin and crime are of such a magnitude that even they withdraw themselves. «ان تعذّبهم فانّهم عبادك» 4- Allah's wrath or mercy is based on His Might and Wisdom. «انت العزیز الحكیم»

228) Al-Anbiya, 23.

Ayahs

قَالَ ٱللَّهُ هَـٰذَا يَوْمُ يَنفَعُ ٱلصَّـٰدِقِينَ صِدْقُهُمْ ۚ لَهُمْ جَنَّـٰتٌ تَجْرِى مِن تَحْتِهَا ٱلْأَنْهَـٰرُ خَـٰلِدِينَ فِيهَآ أَبَدًا ۚ رَّضِىَ ٱللَّهُ عَنْهُمْ وَرَضُوا۟ عَنْهُ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ ٱلْفَوْزُ ٱلْعَظِيمُ (۱۱۹)
Allah will say, ‘This day truthfulness shall benefit the truthful. For them there will be gardens with streams running in them, to remain in them forever. Allah is pleased with them and they are pleased with Him. That is the great success.’ (119)
لِلَّهِ مُلْكُ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ وَمَا فِيهِنَّ ۚ وَهُوَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ قَدِيرٌۢ (۱۲٠)
To Allah belongs the kingdom of the heavens and the earth and whatever there is in them, and He has power over all things. (120)

Key Points

In the previous verses, the words of Jesus (عليه السلام) were presented before Allah, stating: 1- I speak nothing but the truth. «ما یكون لى ان اقول ما لیس بحقّ» 2- If I have said anything untrue, You know it. «ان كنت قلته فقد علمته» 3- I told the people nothing except monotheism, which You commanded me (to convey). «ما قلت لهم الاّ ما امرتنى به أن اعبدوا اللّه» 4- As long as I was among them, I was a watchful witness over them so that they would not deviate. «كنت علیهم شهیداً» In this verse, Allah says: All the words of Jesus are correct and true, and today is the Day when the truthfulness of the truthful ones is salvific.

Messages

1- If believers endure difficulties in this world because of their sincerity, this truthfulness and sincerity will be effective for them in the Hereafter. «ینفع الصادقین صدقهم»

2- Only the truthful benefit, not the pretenders, slogan-givers, or hypocrites. «ینفع الصادقین...»

3- Paradise, alongside the pleasure of Allah, is the great triumph and salvation. «جناتٍ... رضى اللَّه... ذلك الفوز العظیم»

4- Worship is only worthy of Him Who holds the sovereignty of the universe and the absolute power of existence, not others, even if they are prophets. «للّه ملك السموات و الارض»

Portrait of Surah Al-An'am

This Surah has 165 verses, and all its verses were revealed at once in Mecca with special ceremony. Gabriel brought this Surah down to the Prophet of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله accompanied by seventy thousand angels.

The main message of the verses of this Surah is the struggle against polytheism (Shirk) and the invitation to monotheism (Tawhid). Since the polytheists of the Arabian Peninsula, based on their beliefs, considered some livestock lawful and others unlawful, the Quran, in the context of combating such superstitions and false beliefs, states rulings regarding livestock starting from verse 136 of this Surah. For this reason, the Surah is named "Al-An'am" (The Cattle/Livestock).

Narrations have been reported concerning the virtue of this Surah and the fulfillment of needs through its recitation. For instance, it is narrated from Imam Sadiq (عليه السلام) that whoever performs four units of prayer (with two salutations), then recites this Surah, and then recites a specific supplication, his needs will be fulfilled. [229]

The word "Qul" (Say) has not appeared in any other Surah as frequently as it has in this one. The 44-time repetition of this address to the Prophet is perhaps because this Surah discusses the false beliefs, deviations, and undue expectations of the polytheists, necessitating decisiveness in the matter. This point indicates that the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله is commanded to convey the text of the revelation without addition or omission.

229) Tafsir Atayeb al-Bayan.
